text: In SLE/lupus nephritis, {{c1::hydroxychloroquine}} should be prescribed to all patients unless contraindicated.
A neonatal nontreponemal titer that is {{c1::fourfold (≥2 dilutions) higher}} than the maternal titer at delivery indicates confirmed or highly probable {{c2::congenital syphilis}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Congenital Syphilis, Evaluation and Treatment of Neonates section

text: High-intensity statin options in PAD include {{c1::atorvastatin 40–80 mg}} daily or {{c1::rosuvastatin 20–40 mg}} daily, targeting ≥50% LDL-C reduction.
extra: Section 5.2 (Lipid-Lowering Therapy) / Table 11 — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: The ACR 2021 guideline diverges from EULAR by recommending {{c1::temporal artery biopsy}} over {{c1::temporal artery ultrasound or cranial MRI}} for GCA diagnosis in the US, citing limited US expertise with imaging modalities.
Normal BP is defined as SBP {{c1::<120}} mm Hg AND DBP {{c1::<80}} mm Hg.
extra: Table 4 (Categories of Blood Pressure in Adults) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Modeled lifetime breast cancer deaths averted per 1000 women: {{c1::8.2}} with biennial screening starting at age 40 vs. {{c2::6.7}} starting at age 50 (all women).
extra: Table 2 — Estimated median lifetime benefits per 1000 women, biennial DBT starting age 40 vs. 50

text: The therapeutic trough target for adalimumab in Crohn's disease is ≥{{c1::5 μg/mL}}.
extra: Anti-TNF Agents section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: For SBP, repeat diagnostic paracentesis {{c1::48 hours}} after starting antibiotics; treatment failure is defined as {{c2::<25% decrease}} in PMN count from baseline.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: SBP/SBE Diagnosis and Treatment

text: An excellent response to DTC therapy is defined as a suppressed Tg {{c1::<0.2 ng/mL}} OR a TSH-stimulated Tg {{c2::<1 ng/mL}}, with negative imaging and no anti-Tg antibody interference.
If cardiovascular surgery cannot be delayed in a patient with acute HIT, the preferred intraoperative anticoagulant is {{c1::bivalirudin}}.
By Baveno VI criteria, screening EGD can be avoided when LSM < {{c1::20 kPa}} AND platelets > {{c1::150 K/mm³}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: In moderate-to-severe renal impairment, reduce naltrexone-bupropion ER to a maximum of {{c1::1 tablet twice daily (50% of full dose)}}; in moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment, limit to a maximum of {{c2::1 tablet daily}}.
extra: Recommendation 5 / Special Clinical Considerations — Naltrexone-bupropion ER dose adjustments.

text: The optimal treatment for Cushing's disease in both adults and children is {{c1::transsphenoidal selective adenomectomy (TSS)}} performed by {{c2::an experienced}} pituitary surgeon.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Rec. 3.1c / Section 3 (1|QQQQ)

text: During the low-FODMAP diet restriction phase, responders are typically identified within {{c1::2–6 weeks}}; the plan follows 3 phases: {{c2::restrict → reintroduce → personalize}}.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Low-FODMAP Diet section; Dionne et al. Am J Gastroenterol 2018

text: In decompensated cirrhosis, preferred antiviral agents are {{c1::entecavir or TDF}} (or TAF if renal/bone concerns); {{c2::peg-IFN}} is contraindicated.

To prevent osmotic demyelination syndrome (ODS) in cirrhosis, correct sodium at {{c1::4–8 mEq/L per day}}, not exceeding {{c2::10–12 mEq/L per 24 hours}} (standard risk) or {{c3::≤8 mEq/L per 24 hours}} (high risk, including advanced liver disease).
Antibiotics are indicated during a COPD exacerbation when {{c1::purulent sputum is present}} or {{c1::mechanical ventilation is required}}, for a course of {{c2::5 days}}.
30-day mortality with restrictive vs liberal RBC transfusion strategy in adults: RR {{c1::1.00}} (95% CI, 0.86–1.16), indicating {{c1::no significant difference}} in mortality.
In early-stage, H. pylori-positive gastric MALT lymphoma, complete remission is achieved in approximately {{c1::75.2%}} of patients after successful H. pylori eradication.
For low-risk PE, {{c1::outpatient treatment}} is recommended over hospitalization when access to medications, outpatient care, and home circumstances are adequate.
extra: Guidance Statement 10; Summary of Recommendations, p. e547

text: In the captopril challenge test for PA, aldosterone suppression of <{{c1::30%}} after 25–50 mg captopril orally indicates a positive result (PA not excluded).
IV insulin targeting blood glucose {{c1::80–130 mg/dL}} in hospitalized AIS patients is {{c2::not recommended}} (COR 3: No Benefit; SHINE trial).
After TNFi discontinuation in stable axSpA, approximately {{c1::60–74%}} of patients experience disease relapse.

Secondary rheumatic fever prophylaxis is {{c1::penicillin G benzathine 1.2 million U IM every 4 weeks}}; duration is ≥10 years or until age {{c2::40}} (whichever is longer) if residual VHD is present.
extra: 2020 ACC/AHA VHD Guideline — Table 6 and Table 7

text: Source control in sepsis should ideally be achieved within {{c1::6–12 hours}} of diagnosis.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Source Control section

text: Compared to conventional troponin (serial sampling at {{c1::3–6 hours}}), hs-cTn allows serial sampling at {{c2::1–2 hours}} from ED arrival.

In the 2022 ATS/ERS/JRS/ALAT guideline, {{c1::transbronchial lung cryobiopsy (TBLC)}} is conditionally recommended as an acceptable alternative to {{c2::surgical lung biopsy}} for histopathological diagnosis of ILD of undetermined type in centers with appropriate expertise.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF (TBLC section)

text: All adults on long-term GCs should take calcium {{c1::1,000–1,200}} mg/day and vitamin D {{c2::600–800}} IU/day, targeting serum 25-OH vitamin D ≥{{c3::20}} ng/ml.
extra: Figure 3; Thresholds & Doses — Calcium and Vitamin D doses

text: PK monitoring for {{c1::aminoglycosides}} is a strong recommendation, whereas PK monitoring for {{c2::vancomycin}} is a weak (suggested) recommendation per IDSA/SHEA 2016.
The 2025 guideline uses the {{c1::PREVENT}} risk calculator (replacing the {{c2::Pooled Cohort Equations}}) to guide pharmacotherapy thresholds, with a cutoff of {{c3::≥7.5%}} 10-year CVD risk.
extra: Section 5.2.2 (BP Treatment Threshold and CVD Risk Estimation) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: In the IMbrave 050 trial, adjuvant atezolizumab plus bevacizumab achieved a 12-month recurrence-free survival of {{c1::78%}} vs. {{c1::65%}} for surveillance alone.
extra: Surgical Resection / Adjuvant Therapy section — IMbrave 050 RCT, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: The INR target for a mechanical mitral valve replacement is {{c1::3.0}} (range {{c1::2.5–3.5}}).
Bempedoic acid is dosed at {{c1::180 mg orally once daily}}, reducing LDL-C by ~{{c2::21–24%}} as monotherapy.
A CDAI of {{c1::220–450}} defines moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease.
extra: Table 1 (GRADE Recommendations Summary); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: HCC surveillance should NOT be performed in Child-Turcotte-Pugh {{c1::C}} patients unless they are on the {{c2::liver transplant waitlist}}.
Routine echocardiogram to screen for pulmonary hypertension is {{c1::NOT recommended}} in asymptomatic children or adults with SCD; perform only when clinical signs or symptoms are present.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Pulmonary Hypertension: Screening section

text: All patients with severe VHD being considered for intervention should be evaluated by a {{c1::multidisciplinary Heart Valve Team}} with referral to a Primary or Comprehensive Valve Center.
Ascitic protein {{c1::<1.5 g/dL}} is a risk factor for primary SBP; consider antibiotic prophylaxis in high-risk patients.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: SBP/SBE Diagnosis and Treatment

text: For children 6–11 years at Steps 1–2, preferred reliever options are {{c1::as-needed low-dose ICS-formoterol}} or {{c2::as-needed ICS+SABA}} (combination or separate inhalers), rather than SABA alone.
extra: Box 4-8B (p.91); What's New in GINA 2026 (pp.20–23) — CARE trial

text: Low-dose aspirin increases intracranial bleed risk by approximately {{c1::31%}} (Peto OR 1.31, 95% CI 1.11–1.54) in primary prevention trials.
extra: Supporting Evidence / Harms of Preventive Medication; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: After the initial 3-day high-dose PPI course, high-risk patients (Rockall score ≥6) should receive {{c1::twice-daily oral PPI}} from days {{c2::4–14}} post-endoscopy.
extra: Antisecretory Therapy section, Figure 3 (Cheng 2014 RCT) — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: Entecavir is dosed at {{c1::0.5 mg}} daily in treatment-naïve patients and {{c2::1 mg}} daily if lamivudine-experienced or in decompensated cirrhosis.
In patients {{c1::>80 years}} of age requiring AVR, {{c2::TAVI}} is preferred or equivalent to SAVR.

In adults aged 40–75 years with diabetes (without ASCVD), statin therapy targets ≥30%–49% LDL-C reduction and LDL-C {{c1::<100 mg/dL}}; escalate to {{c2::high-intensity}} statin for multiple risk factors.
The initial screening test for suspected thyrotoxicosis is {{c1::serum TSH}}; add {{c2::free T4 and total T3}} when thyrotoxicosis is strongly suspected.
Finerenone starting dose: {{c1::10 mg/day}} if eGFR 25–59 ml/min/1.73 m²; {{c1::20 mg/day}} if eGFR ≥60 ml/min/1.73 m². Hold if K⁺ >{{c2::5.5 mmol/l}}.
DMARD tapering should only be considered after a patient has been at target (low disease activity or remission) for at least {{c1::6 months}}.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 5 (Tapering/Discontinuation); conditional recommendation

text: If parenteral anticoagulation is refused or not tolerated for superficial vein thrombosis, the alternative is rivaroxaban {{c1::10 mg}} orally once daily.
For Acinetobacter HAP/VAP susceptible only to polymyxins, use IV {{c1::colistin or polymyxin B}} plus adjunctive {{c2::inhaled colistin}}; avoid {{c3::tigecycline}}.
ACR 2020 recommends testing for {{c1::HLA-B*5801}} before starting allopurinol in patients of {{c2::Southeast Asian (Han Chinese, Korean, Thai) or African American}} descent.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 4 (Specific ULT medication recommendations)

text: For children with ITP who fail first-line therapy, ASH 2019 recommends the following hierarchy (most to least preferred): {{c1::TPO-RAs}} > {{c2::rituximab}} > {{c3::splenectomy}} (all conditional recommendations).
In active AS and nr-axSpA, {{c1::NSAIDs}} are strongly recommended as {{c2::first-line}} pharmacological therapy.
Routine use of IVC filters in cancer patients is {{c1::not recommended}}; cancer is neither a specific indication nor contraindication for filter placement.
In pregnant women with ASB, the preferred antimicrobial classes for safety are {{c1::nitrofurantoin}} and {{c2::β-lactams}}.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section III (Pregnant Women); Key Recommendations

text: Genetic testing for FH-I (GRA) is indicated when PA onset is before age {{c1::20}} or there is a family history of PA or stroke before age {{c2::40}}.

Platelet transfusion {{c1::should NOT}} be given for spontaneous ICH in patients on antiplatelet therapy unless emergency surgery or severe thrombocytopenia is present, because it {{c2::worsens functional outcome}} (PATCH trial).
For stage 2 hypertension, initiate therapy with {{c1::2 first-line agents from different classes}} in a {{c2::single-pill combination (SPC)}} to improve adherence and accelerate BP control.

Etomidate is the only {{c1::IV}} option for severe life-threatening hypercortisolism; it is given as a loading dose of {{c2::3–5 mg IV}}, then a continuous infusion of {{c2::0.03–0.10 mg/kg/h}}, targeting serum cortisol {{c2::10–20 µg/dL}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Section 6.4 (Etomidate subsection), Table 1

text: For the On-X mechanical AVR (≥3 months post-op, no additional risk factors), a reduced INR target of {{c1::1.5–2.0}} plus aspirin {{c2::75–100 mg}} daily is acceptable.
In GPA/MPA patients on rituximab maintenance, add immunoglobulin supplementation when IgG is {{c1::< 3 g/L}} AND there are {{c1::recurrent severe infections}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Rec 15 (GPA/MPA)

text: Per-unit transfusion risks per AABB 2023: TACO {{c1::1:125}}, TRALI {{c2::1:1,250}}, HIV 1:600,000.
The NELSON trial demonstrated a lung cancer mortality incidence rate ratio of {{c1::0.75}} (95% CI, 0.61–0.90) at {{c2::10 years}} of follow-up with LDCT screening.
extra: Benefits of Early Detection and Treatment section

text: {{c1::Nintedanib}} is conditionally recommended for treatment of PPF in patients who have failed standard management for fibrotic ILD other than IPF (low-quality evidence).
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of PPF — Nintedanib section (INBUILD trial, Flaherty 2019, NEJM)

text: After STI treatment, patients should be rescreened for chlamydia, gonorrhea, and trichomoniasis at {{c1::3 months}}, regardless of partner treatment status.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Clinical Prevention Guidance, Retesting section

text: In NICUs with an invasive candidiasis rate >{{c1::10%}}, neonates with birth weight <{{c2::1000 g}} should receive fluconazole {{c3::3–6 mg/kg twice weekly for 6 weeks}} as prophylaxis.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section VII, Recommendation 51

text: The PHQ-9 cutoff of {{c1::≥10}} has a sensitivity of {{c2::0.85}} and specificity of {{c2::0.85}} for depression screening vs. semistructured interview.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests — 47 studies, n=11,234

text: In ACS patients not at high bleeding risk, the default DAPT duration after PCI is {{c1::at least 12 months}}.
Corticosteroids {{c1::should not}} be used to manage elevated ICP after ICH; they confer no benefit and increase {{c2::infectious and metabolic complications}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.5 (Neuroinvasive Monitoring, ICP, and Edema Treatment)

text: Absolute contraindications to initiating T therapy include: breast or prostate cancer, {{c1::elevated hematocrit}}, untreated severe OSA, severe LUTS, uncontrolled heart failure, MI or stroke within {{c2::6 months}}, thrombophilia, and near-term fertility plans.
ACR 2020 strongly recommends initiating ULT in gout patients with ≥1 subcutaneous tophus, radiographic damage attributable to gout, or frequent flares defined as {{c1::≥2 flares/year}}.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 1 (Indications for pharmacologic ULT)

text: For subclinical hyperthyroidism with TSH persistently {{c1::<0.1 mU/L}}, treat {{c2::patients ≥65 years}} and those with cardiac disease, osteoporosis, or hyperthyroid symptoms regardless of age.
Diagnostic paracentesis should be performed in {{c1::all cirrhotic patients admitted emergently}}, even without signs of infection, to exclude SBP.
In chronic coronary disease (CCD), first-line lipid-lowering therapy is {{c1::high-intensity statin}}, targeting an LDL-C reduction of {{c1::≥50%}} from baseline.
extra: 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline, Section 4.2.6 (Lipid Management), Table 11

text: Adults ≥40 years at LOW fracture risk on long-term GCs should be treated with {{c1::calcium and vitamin D alone}}; bisphosphonates should NOT be added.
extra: Table 2 — Initial treatment recommendations by fracture risk category

text: For nosocomial or MDRO-risk SBP, empirical antibiotic coverage should be broadened to {{c1::piperacillin/tazobactam ± vancomycin}} (or meropenem).
extra: AASLD 2021 — Table 9: Antibiotics for Infections in Cirrhosis

text: In HTAD or BAV aortopathy, rapid growth warranting surgery is defined as ≥{{c1::0.3 cm/year}}.
The USPSTF 2023 states that {{c1::no evidence supports a specific optimal screening interval}} for depression; clinicians should use {{c2::clinical judgment}} based on prior history and risk factors.
In axSpA with coexisting ulcerative colitis where TNFi are not an option, {{c1::tofacitinib}} is preferred over {{c2::IL-17 inhibitors}}.
Before discontinuing cervical cancer screening in a post-hysterectomy patient, cervix removal should be confirmed via {{c1::surgical records or direct examination}}.
The high-risk CHA2DS2-VASc threshold favoring anticoagulation in AF is {{c1::≥2}} in men and {{c1::≥3}} in women.
Neurosyphilis, ocular syphilis, and otosyphilis are treated with {{c1::aqueous crystalline penicillin G 18–24 million units/day IV}} for {{c2::10–14 days}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Syphilis, Neurosyphilis section

text: ACEi/ARB should be temporarily suspended in SCD patients during {{c1::intercurrent illness, planned IV radiocontrast, bowel preparation, or prior to major surgery}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Albuminuria Management section

text: During finerenone therapy, withhold the drug if serum K⁺ >{{c1::5.5}} mmol/l and reinitiate at 10 mg when K⁺ {{c2::drops to ≤5.0 mmol/l}}.
The KDIGO 2021 SBP target in adults with CKD not on dialysis (using standardized office measurement) is {{c1::<120 mm Hg}} (Level 2B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.1.1; based on SPRINT CKD subgroup

text: High-dose continuous IV PPI after endoscopic hemostasis: {{c1::80-mg IV bolus}} followed by {{c2::8 mg/hr continuous infusion}} for 72 hours.
extra: Antisecretory Therapy section, Figure 3 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: The 2021 USPSTF guideline recommends beginning CRC screening in average-risk adults at age {{c1::45}} years, down from {{c2::50}} years in the 2016 guideline.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation; Recommendation Summary Table

text: The 2026 guideline recommends measuring Lp(a) {{c1::at least once}} in {{c2::all adults}} for ASCVD risk assessment.
KDIGO 2012 recommends against using {{c1::diuretics}} to {{c2::prevent AKI}} (Grade 1B).
After PCI with DES, a DAPT duration of {{c1::≤6 months}} is safe and preferred when bleeding risk is high and ischemic risk is low-to-moderate.
Antibiotic duration for SBP is {{c1::5–7 days}}; may shorten if ascitic PMN count falls to {{c2::<250/mm³}}.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: SBP/SBE Diagnosis and Treatment

text: In moderately to severely active UC, {{c1::vedolizumab}} is preferred over {{c1::adalimumab}} based on a head-to-head RCT demonstrating superior clinical remission and endoscopic improvement at 52 weeks.
The ACP 2023 recommendation for bisphosphonates as first-line therapy for primary osteoporosis applies to both {{c1::postmenopausal females}} and {{c1::males}}.
extra: ACP 2023, Recommendations 1 & 2 — Ann Intern Med doi:10.7326/M22-1034

text: For persistent exacerbations on LABA+LAMA, add ICS if eosinophils {{c1::≥ 100 cells/µL}}; if eosinophils {{c1::< 100 cells/µL}}, consider azithromycin (non-smokers) or roflumilast instead.
UNOS downstaging (UNOS-DS) upper limits include: single lesion {{c1::5.1–8 cm}}; 2–3 lesions each {{c2::<5 cm}}; or 4–5 lesions each <3 cm with total diameter <8 cm.
extra: Liver Transplantation section / Figure 12, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: Brugada syndrome is characterized by ST elevation {{c1::≥2 mm}} with {{c2::type 1 morphology}} in ≥1 right precordial lead (V1–V2), spontaneously or after IV Class I antiarrhythmic drug.

Biobehavioral therapies (CBT, biofeedback, relaxation, mindfulness) have {{c1::Grade A}} evidence for migraine prevention and are especially recommended for patients who are {{c2::pregnant}}, overusing acute medications, or prefer nonpharmacologic treatment.
In sepsis/septic shock, {{c1::prolonged (extended or continuous) infusion}} of beta-lactam antibiotics is preferred over standard bolus infusion when feasible.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Antibiotic Administration section

text: Sulfasalazine and methotrexate are {{c1::not recommended}} for {{c2::axial}} disease in axSpA; they may be considered only for prominent {{c3::peripheral arthritis}} or when biologics are unavailable.

In renal transplant recipients {{c1::>1 month}} post-transplant, IDSA 2019 recommends {{c2::against}} screening or treating ASB (strong recommendation, high-quality evidence); no recommendation can be made for the {{c1::first month}} post-transplant.

In mild acute pancreatitis, oral feeding with a {{c1::low-fat soft/solid diet}} should be initiated early without waiting for pain resolution or enzyme normalization; a stepwise clear-liquid-to-solid progression is {{c2::unnecessary}}.
extra: Nutrition in AP section — Bakker et al., NEJM 2014

text: Urgent surgical hematoma evacuation is recommended for cerebellar ICH when volume is {{c1::≥15 mL}}, or when neurological deterioration, brainstem compression, or hydrocephalus is present.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 6.1.4 (Craniotomy for Posterior Fossa Hemorrhage)

text: After LVP removing >5 L of ascites, infuse albumin at {{c1::6–8 g per liter removed}} to prevent postparacentesis circulatory dysfunction (PPCD).
In ATTR-CM, {{c1::tafamidis}} is the only therapy proven to reduce all-cause mortality and CV hospitalizations; the standard dose is {{c2::80 mg (or 61 mg) once daily}}.
IL-12/23i may be preferred over IL-17i in treatment-naive PsA when the patient has {{c1::concomitant IBD}} or prefers {{c2::less frequent dosing}}.
extra: Table 1 — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendation.

text: COPD is confirmed by a post-bronchodilator {{c1::FEV1/FVC < 0.7}}; pre-bronchodilator spirometry may exclude COPD but {{c2::cannot confirm}} the diagnosis.

For gastric varices, {{c1::BRTO}} is preferred over TIPS when MELDNa > {{c2::20}} or FIPS > 0.92; TIPS is preferred when MELDNa is below that threshold with large esophageal varices, ascites, or portal vein thrombosis.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Gastric varices / Management of initial and recurrent bleeding

text: In adults at intermediate primary prevention risk (5%–<10%), initiate at least {{c1::moderate-intensity}} statin targeting {{c1::≥30%–49%}} LDL-C reduction.
For moderate-to-severe DKA, start a fixed-rate IV insulin infusion at {{c1::0.1 units/kg/h}}; an optional loading dose of {{c1::0.1 units/kg}} IV or SC may precede the infusion.
Carvedilol dosing for portal hypertension: start at {{c1::6.25 mg/day}}, increase to {{c2::12.5 mg/day}} after 2–3 days if tolerated; down-titrate to 6.25 mg/day if SBP < 90 mm Hg.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Which is the optimal NSBB for PH? / Table 3

text: In adults aged 40–75 with diabetes and no ASCVD, a {{c1::moderate-intensity statin}} targets LDL-C <{{c2::100 mg/dL}}; escalate to {{c3::high-intensity statin}} (LDL-C goal <70 mg/dL) if multiple ASCVD risk factors are present.
DTG/3TC as a 2-drug initial regimen is contraindicated if HIV RNA is {{c1::≥500,000 copies/mL}}, lamivudine resistance is present, hepatitis B co-infection exists, or the patient is {{c2::pregnant}}.
In LQTS, a QTc {{c1::>500 ms}} defines the high-risk group associated with increased SCA risk; QT-prolonging drugs should be avoided.
Icosapent ethyl {{c1::4 g/day (2 g twice daily with food)}} is indicated for TG {{c2::150–499 mg/dL}} in adults already on a statin with ASCVD or diabetes plus risk factors.
In STEMI with multivessel disease, staged PCI of a significantly stenosed non-culprit artery should be performed {{c1::in-hospital or within 45 days}} of MI to reduce death or MI (COMPLETE trial).
extra: Section 5.2 / Figure 4 — COMPLETE trial; staged PCI within 45 days

text: Long-acting cabotegravir plus rilpivirine injections are recommended for virologically suppressed patients but are contraindicated if {{c1::documented resistance to either agent}} is present or if {{c2::chronic hepatitis B}} co-infection exists.
In children with CKD, the BP target by 24-hour ABPM is a MAP of {{c1::≤50th percentile}} for age, sex, and height (Level 2C).
extra: Chapter 5, Recommendation 5.1 / Practice Point 5.3; based on ESCAPE trial

text: Fulminant CDI is treated with high-dose oral vancomycin {{c1::500 mg}} 4× daily ± rectal instillation ± IV {{c2::metronidazole}}; fidaxomicin is {{c3::not supported}} for fulminant disease.
In OSM/biologic-naive PsA patients with concomitant diabetes, an OSM other than {{c1::MTX}} is preferred over TNFi due to higher risk of {{c2::hepatotoxicity}} with diabetes and fatty liver disease.
extra: Table 5 — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendation.

text: In biologic- and immunomodulator-naive patients with moderate-to-severe CD, combination therapy with {{c1::infliximab plus azathioprine}} is more effective than either agent alone.
extra: Anti-TNF Agents section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: In DMARD-naive RA patients with moderate-to-high disease activity, {{c1::methotrexate monotherapy}} is strongly recommended as first-line DMARD therapy.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 2 (DMARD Initiation); strong recommendation

text: The LUS probe for adult CAP evaluation must provide a penetration depth of at least {{c1::14–16 cm}}, requiring a {{c2::low-frequency}} probe.
extra: Question 1 (Lung Ultrasound) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; Thresholds & Doses.

text: Prone positioning for >{{c1::12 hours/day}} is recommended for severe ARDS defined by PaO₂/FiO₂ <{{c2::150 mmHg}}.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Prone Ventilation section

text: TBLC is relatively contraindicated when FVC is {{c1::<50%}} predicted or DlCO is {{c2::<35%}} predicted.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF (TBLC section)

text: After management of a precancerous cervical lesion, routine screening should continue for at least {{c1::20 years}}, even if this extends beyond age 65 years.
After AAV remission, maintenance therapy uses {{c1::rituximab}} or {{c1::azathioprine}} plus low-dose glucocorticoids.
Stage 2 hypertension is defined as SBP {{c1::≥140}} mm Hg OR DBP {{c1::≥90}} mm Hg.
extra: Table 4 (Categories of Blood Pressure in Adults) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Acceptable stool-based CRC screening intervals: high-sensitivity gFOBT or FIT every {{c1::1 year}}, and sDNA-FIT every {{c2::3 years}}.
extra: Table 1: Characteristics of Recommended Colorectal Cancer Screening Strategies

text: In acutely ill medical inpatients, {{c1::LMWH or fondaparinux}} is preferred over {{c2::UFH}} for VTE prophylaxis (conditional recommendation).
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 1–3 (Acutely ill patients: pharmacological prophylaxis)

text: {{c1::Alternating bundle branch block}} (alternating LBBB and RBBB morphology) is an indication for permanent pacing due to high risk of {{c2::complete AV block}}.
Short-term DAPT (aspirin + clopidogrel) for {{c1::21–90 days}} is recommended for minor ischemic stroke (NIHSS ≤{{c2::3}}) or high-risk TIA (ABCD2 ≥{{c2::4}}) presenting within 24 hours.
extra: Section 5.19 (Figure 6) — DAPT 21–90 days for early minor stroke/high-risk TIA

text: In known CYP2C19 rapid or ultrarapid metabolizers requiring a PPI for H. pylori treatment, preferred PPIs are {{c1::esomeprazole or rabeprazole}}, or a {{c2::PCAB (vonoprazan)}}-based regimen should be substituted.
For children with {{c1::biventricular-repaired}} congenital heart disease, AABB 2023 recommends considering transfusion at a threshold of {{c2::7 g/dL}} (conditional, low certainty).
In the Swedish case-crossover study (Janszky 2018), blood transfusion was associated with an IE odds ratio of {{c1::6.69}} (95% CI 4.43–10.11) in high-risk inpatients.
extra: Table 3 / Janszky 2018 section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Clinicians should maintain heightened suspicion for IE in high-risk patients who have undergone any NDIP within the preceding {{c1::3 months (12 weeks)}}.
extra: Future Considerations section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: In PMR, GCs should be given as a {{c1::single daily}} dose; divided dosing is only considered when {{c2::the maintenance dose is low}}.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 6

text: CAC 1–99 AU and below the 75th percentile: treat with {{c1::moderate-intensity statin}} targeting LDL-C {{c1::<100 mg/dL}} and non–HDL-C <130 mg/dL.
For TG 500–999 mg/dL, add {{c1::fibric acid derivatives}} or {{c1::prescription omega-3 fatty acids}} to reduce pancreatitis risk, and refer to an RDN.
The 2016 Endocrine Society guideline added {{c1::sustained BP >150/100 mmHg on 3 separate days}} as a new indication to screen for primary aldosteronism using the ARR.
The 2024 USPSTF recommends {{c1::biennial screening mammography}} for all women aged {{c1::40–74}} years (Grade B).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade B for biennial mammography ages 40–74

text: In childhood steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome, the initial second-line therapy is {{c1::cyclosporine or tacrolimus}}.
The preferred intervention for symptomatic severe rheumatic MS with favorable valve morphology, no LA thrombus, and MR ≤mild is {{c1::percutaneous mitral balloon commissurotomy (PMBC)}}.
First-line pharmacotherapy for IBS-C (strong recommendation) includes chloride channel activators ({{c1::lubiprostone}}) or guanylate cyclase-C agonists ({{c2::linaclotide, plecanatide}}).
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — IBS-C Pharmacotherapy sections; Table 2

text: AAN 2018 recommends discussing a switch to a lower PML-risk DMT in JCV antibody–positive natalizumab users, especially when the antibody index is {{c1::>0.9}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Switching Rec 6 / Statement 6b

text: TAVI is not recommended when life expectancy is {{c1::<1 year}} or chance of survival with meaningful benefit is {{c2::<25%}} at 2 years.
extra: 2020 ACC/AHA VHD Guideline — Table 8 / Key Recommendations

text: For PTH-lowering in CKD G5D, KDIGO 2017 lists {{c1::calcimimetics}}, {{c1::calcitriol}}, and {{c1::vitamin D analogs}} as equivalent first-line options with no single agent prioritized.

Urgent treatment is recommended within {{c1::24–72 hours}} for life-threatening Cushing's syndrome complications such as infection, pulmonary thromboembolism, cardiovascular events, or acute psychosis.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Rec. 8.1 / Section 8 (1|QQQE)

text: In active GPA/MPA glomerulonephritis, plasma exchange should {{c1::NOT}} be routinely added to induction therapy; it may be considered only in patients at highest risk of {{c2::ESKD}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Rec 2 (GPA/MPA)

text:
Dofetilide maintenance dosing by CrCl:
CrCl >60 mL/min → {{c1::500 μg twice daily}}
CrCl 40–60 → {{c1::250 μg twice daily}}
CrCl 20–40 → {{c1::125 μg twice daily}}
CrCl <20 mL/min → contraindicated
NIV is first-line ventilatory support during COPD exacerbations when PaCO2 {{c1::≥ 45 mmHg}} and pH {{c2::≤ 7.35}} (acute hypercapnic respiratory failure).
ASUC by Truelove & Witts criteria: ≥{{c1::6 bowel movements/day}} plus ≥1 of: tachycardia, fever, hemoglobin <{{c2::10.5 g/dL}}, or ESR >30 mm/hr.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; ASUC Management section

text: In pregnant women with mechanical valves on LMWH, the anti-Xa target is {{c1::0.8–1.2 U/mL}} (peak, 4–6 h post-dose) with a trough {{c2::>0.6 IU/mL}}.
AAN 2018 requires PML risk counseling for patients on natalizumab, fingolimod, rituximab, ocrelizumab, and {{c1::dimethyl fumarate}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Switching Rec 6 / Statements 6a–6b

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::CRRT}} rather than standard intermittent RRT in hemodynamically unstable patients or those with raised intracranial pressure (Grade 2B).
RAI dose for Graves' disease: fixed mean dose {{c1::10–15 mCi (370–555 MBq)}} or calculated to deliver >{{c2::150 μCi/g}} of thyroid tissue.
EVT for anterior circulation ICA or M1 LVO within 6 hours requires NIHSS ≥{{c1::6}}, prestroke mRS {{c2::0–1}}, and ASPECTS {{c3::3–10}} (COR 1).
Euglycemic DKA is defined as glucose {{c1::<200 mg/dL}} with ketosis and acidosis criteria met, and is especially associated with {{c2::SGLT2 inhibitor}} use.
Adjuvant {{c1::atezolizumab plus bevacizumab}} for 12 months is recommended for HCC patients at high risk of recurrence after resection or local ablation (IMbrave 050 trial).
extra: Surgical Resection / Adjuvant Therapy section — IMbrave 050 RCT, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: The BP target for adults requiring antihypertensive pharmacotherapy is {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}}.

For adjuvant RAI therapy targeting suspected microscopic residual disease, ATA 2015 recommends up to {{c1::150 mCi}}, noting no clear benefit beyond this dose for T3/N1 without known distant metastases.
Routine {{c1::serial spine radiographs}} at scheduled intervals (e.g., every 2 years) are {{c2::not recommended}} in axSpA.
extra: Table 2 (AS recommendations, Rec. 49–51) / Table 3 (nr-axSpA, Rec. 84–86) — ACR/SAA/SPARTAN 2019

text: M. genitalium is managed with two-stage therapy: {{c1::doxycycline 100 mg BID × 7 days}} first, followed by {{c2::azithromycin}} (macrolide-sensitive) or {{c2::moxifloxacin 400 mg daily × 7 days}} (macrolide-resistant).
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Mycoplasma genitalium, Treatment section

text: On intestinal ultrasound, bowel wall thickness >{{c1::3 mm}} indicates moderate-to-severe active UC; IUS can detect treatment response as early as {{c2::2 weeks}}.
For outpatients and inpatients with nonsevere CAP who reach clinical stability, antibiotic duration should be {{c1::less than 5 days}} (minimum {{c2::3 days}}).
extra: Question 3 (Antibiotic Duration) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; Table 6; conditional, low-quality evidence.

text: Per IDSA 2019, the only populations in whom screening and treatment of ASB is recommended are {{c1::pregnant women}} and {{c2::patients prior to endoscopic urologic procedures with mucosal trauma}}.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Sections III & XIII; Key Recommendations

text: Add {{c1::5–10% dextrose}} to IV fluids and reduce insulin infusion to 0.05 units/kg/h once glucose falls to {{c2::<250 mg/dL}} in DKA, to allow continued insulin until ketoacidosis resolves.
In CKD G3a–G5D, parathyroidectomy is indicated for {{c1::refractory severe hyperparathyroidism}} that is unresponsive to pharmacotherapy.
In clinical trials, severe psoriasis is defined as PASI ≥{{c1::12}} and BSA ≥{{c1::10%}} — the threshold used to consider IL-17i or IL-12/23i over TNFi or OSM.

AGA 2022 recommends adding pharmacotherapy to lifestyle intervention in adults with BMI {{c1::≥30 kg/m²}}, or BMI {{c2::≥27 kg/m²}} with at least one weight-related complication, who have an inadequate response to lifestyle changes alone.
extra: Table 1, Recommendation 1 — strong recommendation, moderate certainty.

text: Postoperative remission in pediatric Cushing's disease is defined by a serum cortisol of {{c1::<1 µg/dL (28 nmol/L)}} or {{c1::<1.8 µg/dL (50 nmol/L)}} depending on the series.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Thresholds & Doses / Section 3

text: First-line therapy for chronic euvolemic (SIAD) hyponatremia is fluid restriction to {{c1::<1 L/day}} (or <500 mL/day if urine-to-serum electrolyte ratio <1).
extra: Treatment of Chronic Hyponatremia section; Table 2

text: The recommended biopsy protocol for celiac disease diagnosis is {{c1::1–2 biopsies from the duodenal bulb}} plus {{c2::≥4 biopsies from the postbulbar duodenum}}.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Diagnosis section; Figure 1

text: High-intensity statin regimens consist of {{c1::atorvastatin 40–80 mg}} or {{c1::rosuvastatin 20–40 mg}} daily.
ACR 2020 mandates starting allopurinol at {{c1::≤100 mg/day}} (or {{c2::≤50 mg/day in CKD stage ≥3}}) before titrating up to a maximum of 800 mg/day.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2; Thresholds & Doses

text: Per ACP 2023, {{c1::bisphosphonates}} are recommended as first-line pharmacologic treatment to reduce fracture risk in postmenopausal females with primary osteoporosis.
extra: ACP 2023, Recommendation 1 — Ann Intern Med doi:10.7326/M22-1034

text: For SIAD, the US guideline lists vaptans as {{c1::second-line}} therapy if fluid restriction fails, whereas the European guideline does not recommend vaptans at SNa <130 mmol/L and recommends {{c2::against}} them at SNa <125 mmol/L.
For 2026, {{c1::ISPAD}} newly endorsed Section {{c2::14}} (Children and Adolescents) of the ADA Standards of Care.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Methodology and Procedure section

text: For outpatient CAP with a positive respiratory virus test and NO comorbidities, the 2025 ATS/IDSA guideline recommends {{c1::withholding}} empiric antibiotics.
extra: Question 2 (Empiric Antibiotics with Positive Virus Test) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; conditional, very low-quality evidence.

text: In fulminant CDI (especially with ileus), metronidazole is added IV at {{c1::500 mg every 8 hours}}.
For mild gallstone pancreatitis, {{c1::same-admission cholecystectomy}} is recommended; cholecystectomy should be {{c2::delayed}} when pancreatic necrosis is present.
extra: Surgery in AP section — Da Costa et al., Lancet 2015

text: Standardized office BP should be supplemented with ABPM or HBPM to detect {{c1::white-coat hypertension}}, {{c2::masked hypertension}}, and {{c2::masked uncontrolled hypertension}} in CKD patients (Level 2B).
Alteplase for AIS is dosed at {{c1::0.9 mg/kg IV}} with a maximum of {{c2::90 mg}}, with {{c3::10%}} given as a bolus over 1 minute and the remainder over 60 minutes.
extra: Table 7 (Treatment of AIS in Adults: IVT)

text: In the ProtecT trial, 10-year overall survival was {{c1::99%}} across radical prostatectomy, radiation therapy, and active surveillance groups; {{c2::54.8%}} of men on active surveillance crossed over to active treatment within 10 years.
In ACS patients requiring oral anticoagulation, the preferred P2Y12 inhibitor is {{c1::clopidogrel}}.
The urine output criterion for AKI is {{c1::<0.5 ml/kg/h}} for {{c2::≥6 h}}.
When clopidogrel is given with a fibrinolytic in STEMI: loading dose is {{c1::300 mg}} if age ≤75 y; if age >75 y, {{c2::no loading dose}} is given (start 75 mg daily).

In PsA with concomitant active IBD, {{c1::monoclonal antibody TNFi}} or {{c2::IL-12/23i}} should be used over IL-17i (strong recommendation), because {{c3::IL-17i and etanercept}} are not effective for IBD.
extra: Table 5 (PICOs 58–60) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; strong recommendations, moderate-quality evidence.

text: When additional LDL-C lowering is needed beyond maximally tolerated statin therapy, {{c1::ezetimibe}} should be added before a {{c2::PCSK9 monoclonal antibody}}.
PPCD risk increases when more than {{c1::8 L}} of ascites is removed in a single LVP session.
All acute VTE requires at least {{c1::3 months (12 weeks)}} of anticoagulation before reassessing for extended therapy.
Post-MI pericarditis is treated with high-dose aspirin {{c1::500-1000 mg q6-8h}} plus colchicine {{c2::0.5-0.6 mg once or twice daily}} for 3 months.
The 2022 guideline conditionally recommends {{c1::against}} antacid medication in IPF for the purpose of improving {{c2::respiratory outcomes}} (antacids remain appropriate for symptomatic GERD).
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of IPF (Antacid section)

text: A 4Ts score of {{c1::4–5}} indicates intermediate probability and a score of {{c2::≥6}} indicates high probability for HIT, both requiring heparin discontinuation and initiation of a non-heparin anticoagulant.
ICD is recommended for Brugada syndrome patients with {{c1::prior SCA}} or {{c1::spontaneous type 1 ECG with syncope}}.
Milan criteria for liver transplantation in HCC: {{c1::single lesion 1–5 cm}} OR {{c2::2–3 lesions each 1–3 cm}}.
extra: Liver Transplantation section / Table 3, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: An hsCRP of ≥{{c1::2.0 mg/L}} is a risk-enhancing factor that may favor statin initiation in borderline- or intermediate-risk adults.
For most patients with T2D + CKD and eGFR ≥30 ml/min/1.73 m², KDIGO 2022 recommends {{c1::metformin AND an SGLT2i}} as first-line glucose-lowering therapy.
AKI Stage 3 is defined by SCr ≥{{c1::3×}} baseline, or absolute SCr ≥{{c2::4.0 mg/dl}}, or initiation of RRT.
For symptomatic CCD, either a {{c1::beta blocker}} or {{c1::calcium channel blocker}} is recommended as first-line antianginal therapy.
extra: 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline, Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: Lp(a) is considered elevated (a risk enhancer) at ≥{{c1::125 nmol/L}} (≥{{c1::50 mg/dL}}), conferring approximately a {{c2::1.4}}-fold increase in ASCVD risk.
Maintenance therapy for lupus nephritis Class III/IV is {{c1::mycophenolic acid analogs (MPAA)}} for a total immunosuppression duration of ≥{{c2::36 months}}.
The USPSTF found {{c1::insufficient evidence}} to prefer any adjunctive PSA screening method — including PSA velocity, free PSA, or genetic tests — over {{c2::standard PSA testing}}.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Screening — no recommendation to favor one PSA screening method

text: {{c1::Didactic education alone}} should NOT be relied upon as the primary stewardship strategy; it should complement active interventions such as PAF.
For low-risk or lower-intermediate-risk remnant ablation, ATA 2015 favors an administered ¹³¹I activity of {{c1::~30 mCi}} over higher activities.
For SVT at increased risk of progression, first-line treatment is {{c1::fondaparinux 2.5 mg subcutaneously daily}} for {{c2::45 days}}.
KDIGO 2024 LN recommendations are graded using the {{c1::GRADE}} framework, supplemented by expert {{c2::practice points}}.
In adrenal crisis, give {{c1::100 mg IV hydrocortisone}} as an immediate bolus (children: {{c2::50 mg/m²}}), followed by 200 mg/24 h continuous infusion plus isotonic saline resuscitation.
extra: Rec. 4.1, Table 3 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: In acute variceal hemorrhage (AVH), vasoactive therapy and IV antibiotic prophylaxis should be started {{c1::immediately on presentation}} and maintained for {{c2::2–5 days}}; upper endoscopy should be performed within {{c3::12 hours}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations; Section: AVH, initial bleed / Figure 4

text: Baclofen is dosed at {{c1::5–20 mg three times daily}} for objective continued reflux despite optimal PPI therapy.
For thyroid storm, PTU dosing is: {{c1::500–1000 mg}} loading dose, then {{c2::250 mg every 4 hours}}.
CSPH is excluded when LSM < {{c1::15 kPa}} AND platelets > {{c1::150 K/mm³}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: First-line treatment for torsades de pointes is {{c1::magnesium IV 1–2 g}}.
T therapy should be stopped if hematocrit exceeds {{c1::54%}}; before reinitiating at a reduced dose, evaluate for {{c2::hypoxia and sleep apnea}}.
An implantable cardiac monitor (loop recorder) is indicated for {{c1::recurrent unexplained syncope suspected to be arrhythmic}} after a nondiagnostic initial workup.
Hypertensive patients planning pregnancy or who are pregnant should be counseled about low-dose aspirin starting at {{c1::12 weeks' gestation}} to reduce {{c2::preeclampsia}} risk.
extra: Section 5.5 (Hypertension and Pregnancy) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: {{c1::TPMT}} testing should be performed before initiating {{c2::azathioprine or 6-mercaptopurine}} in Crohn's disease.

ICD for primary prevention of SCD is indicated in HFrEF with LVEF {{c1::≤35%}}, NYHA class {{c2::II–III}}, and expected survival >1 year; for ischemic cardiomyopathy, requires ≥{{c3::40 days}} post-MI on GDMT.
Adjuvant {{c1::sorafenib}} after resection or ablation for HCC is {{c2::not recommended}} per AASLD 2023.
extra: Surgical Resection / Adjuvant Therapy section, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: For acute spontaneous ICH with SBP 150–220 mm Hg, lower SBP to {{c1::130–<140 mm Hg}} for at least {{c2::7 days}}; stop antihypertensives if SBP falls below 130 mm Hg.
extra: Section 5.3.9.1 (Acute Intracerebral Hemorrhage) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Intraarticular glucocorticoid injections are {{c1::strongly}} recommended for knee and/or hip OA; for hip injections specifically, {{c2::ultrasound guidance}} is strongly recommended.
The target digoxin serum concentration for rate control in AF is {{c1::0.5–0.9 ng/mL}}; concentrations {{c2::≥1.2 ng/mL}} are associated with increased mortality.
Clinically meaningful weight loss, defined as ≥{{c1::5%}} of initial body weight, is the threshold associated with improvements in BP, LDL-C, triglycerides, and glucose.
For secondary prevention post-MI, low-dose colchicine {{c1::0.5 mg daily}} may be used to reduce MACE; it is contraindicated if CrCl <{{c2::15 mL/min}}.
extra: Section 11.4 — 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline; COLCOT trial

text: Virologic failure is defined as failure to achieve or maintain HIV RNA {{c1::<200 copies/mL}}; blips of {{c1::20–200 copies/mL}} should not prompt regimen change on a high-barrier regimen.
Corticosteroids {{c1::should not}} be used for maintenance of remission in UC; steroids should be tapered over {{c2::8–12 weeks}} with transition to steroid-sparing therapy.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC section

text: Hypogonadism is diagnosed only when symptoms/signs of T deficiency are present AND serum total T is confirmed low on {{c1::two separate fasting morning}} measurements.
ATA 2015 TSH suppression target for high-risk DTC (and structural incomplete response): {{c1::<0.1 mU/L}} indefinitely.
For adults aged {{c1::76–85}} years, the USPSTF recommends {{c1::selective/individualized}} CRC screening only (Grade C), based on overall health, prior screening history, and patient preferences.
extra: Recommendation Summary Table; Practice Considerations: Starting and Stopping Ages

text: In people living with HIV aged 40–75 on stable antiretroviral therapy, {{c1::pitavastatin}} is the preferred statin due to {{c2::minimal CYP3A4 metabolism}}.
USPSTF recommends stopping routine cervical cancer screening at age {{c1::65}} years if adequate prior screening is documented (Grade D against further screening).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade D recommendation; Thresholds & Doses

text: For non-intubated {{c1::COVID-19}} AHRF, the guideline suggests awake prone positioning to reduce {{c2::intubation risk}} (RR 0.84, 95% CI 0.73–0.96).
extra: Recommendation 7.3, Domain 7: Awake Prone Positioning — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: First-line treatment for grade 3 (large) ascites is {{c1::large-volume paracentesis (LVP)}}.
For acute gout flares, ACR 2020 strongly recommends {{c1::colchicine}}, {{c1::NSAIDs}}, or {{c1::glucocorticoids}} as first-line therapy over IL-1 inhibitors or ACTH.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 6 (Gout flare management)

text: For myxedema coma, the IV LT4 loading dose is {{c1::200–400 μg}}, followed by a daily maintenance dose of 1.6 μg/kg/day (reduced to 75% for IV route).
In septic shock, {{c1::vasopressin}} should be added (rather than escalating norepinephrine dose) when norepinephrine reaches {{c2::0.25–0.5 mcg/kg/min}}.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Vasoactive Agents section (Figure 2)

text: CSPH is presumed when {{c1::LSM ≥20 kPa}} + {{c2::platelets <150 K/mm³}}, OR when LSM 15–20 kPa + platelets <110 K/mm³.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: During acute asthma exacerbations, supplemental oxygen should be started only if SpO₂ is {{c1::<92%}}.
extra: What's New in GINA 2026 (pp.20–23) — revised acute exacerbation flowcharts

text: In TAK, consider tapering off glucocorticoids after achieving remission on glucocorticoids for at least {{c1::6–12 months}}.
extra: Table 4 (TAK PICO 5–26); Thresholds & Doses — TAK glucocorticoid tapering

text: Hydrocortisone dosing in thyroid storm: {{c1::300 mg IV}} loading dose, then {{c2::100 mg every 8 hours}}.
In gallstone pancreatitis, ERCP is indicated only when complicated by {{c1::concurrent cholangitis}} or {{c2::progressive cholestasis (rising bilirubin)}}; routine early ERCP is not recommended.
extra: ERCP in AP section — Schepers et al., Lancet 2020 (APEC trial)

text: In patients {{c1::<65 years}} of age requiring AVR, {{c2::SAVR}} is preferred over TAVI due to limited long-term durability data for transcatheter valves.
When prednisone dose >7.5 mg/day, adjust FRAX by multiplying major osteoporotic fracture risk ×{{c1::1.15}} and hip fracture risk ×{{c1::1.2}}.
extra: Figure 3; Thresholds & Doses — FRAX GC-dose adjustment

text: After ART initiation, HIV RNA should be checked at {{c1::4–6 weeks}}, then every {{c2::3 months}} until suppressed, then every 6 months; may reduce to annually after >5 years of stable suppression.
Trichomoniasis in men is treated with {{c1::metronidazole 2 g orally}} as a single dose.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Trichomoniasis, Treatment section

text: Hyponatremia is defined as serum sodium {{c1::<136 mmol/L}}.
extra: Table 1 — Classification of hyponatremia by severity

text: Compared with ACE inhibitor alone, {{c1::sacubitril/valsartan}} further reduces SCD in HFrEF patients.
With natalizumab use of 25–36 months (no prior immunosuppression) and JCV antibody index >{{c1::1.5}}, PML risk is approximately {{c2::3 per 1,000}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Rec 16

text: For LQT2 and CPVT, the preferred beta blocker is {{c1::nadolol}}.
Uncomplicated candidemia should be treated for {{c1::2 weeks}} after documented clearance of Candida from the bloodstream and resolution of symptoms.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section I, Recommendation 12

text: High-intensity statin options for very-high-risk ASCVD after ischemic stroke include {{c1::atorvastatin 80 mg/day}} or {{c2::rosuvastatin 20 mg/day}}.
CAC ≥1000 AU: target ≥50% LDL-C reduction and LDL-C {{c1::<55 mg/dL}}, non–HDL-C {{c1::<85 mg/dL}}.
In luminal GI malignancies with cancer-associated thrombosis, {{c1::apixaban}} is preferred over edoxaban or rivaroxaban due to lower GI bleeding risk.

For outpatient CAP with a positive respiratory virus test AND comorbidities (e.g., chronic pulmonary disease, end-stage liver/renal disease, cardiovascular disease), the 2025 ATS/IDSA guideline recommends {{c1::prescribing}} empiric antibiotics due to bacterial co-infection risk.
extra: Question 2 (Empiric Antibiotics with Positive Virus Test) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; Table 5; conditional, very low-quality evidence.

text: Propranolol dosing in thyroid storm is {{c1::60–80 mg every 4 hours}}.

The target iPTH range in CKD G5D is approximately {{c1::2–9×}} the upper limit of normal for the assay; PTH-lowering therapy should be reduced or stopped if iPTH falls below {{c1::2×}} the upper limit of normal.
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Thresholds & Doses / Chapter 4.2

text: Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists (MRAs) should be used with particular caution (hyperkalemia risk) when eGFR is {{c1::<45 ml/min/1.73 m²}} in CKD patients.
KDIGO 2022 advises sodium intake of <{{c1::2 g}}/day (<90 mmol/day, or <5 g NaCl/day) in patients with diabetes + CKD.
hrHPV testing alone is NOT recommended for cervical cancer screening in women under age {{c1::30}} years because of high rates of {{c2::transient HPV infection}}, leading to excess colposcopies.
extra: Discussion: Women Aged 21–29 Years (~400 additional colposcopies per 1000 women vs starting at age 30)

text: To rule out celiac disease in IBS-D, perform serologic testing with {{c1::IgA tissue transglutaminase}} and {{c2::quantitative IgA}}.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Diagnostic Testing section (Celiac Disease); Rubio-Tapia 2013, Irvine et al. Am J Gastroenterol 2017

text: In myotonic dystrophy type 1, pacemaker or ICD implantation should be considered if PR >{{c1::240 ms}}, QRS >{{c2::120 ms}}, or second/third-degree AV block is present.
extra: Section 7.5 (Management of Conduction Disorders); Myotonic dystrophy type 1 ECG criteria

text: ATA 2015 recommends near-total or total thyroidectomy (rather than lobectomy) when thyroid cancer is {{c1::>4 cm}}, there is {{c1::gross extrathyroidal extension}}, or there are {{c1::clinically apparent nodal or distant metastases}}.

Lowering the CRC screening start age from 50 to 45 years is estimated to yield approximately {{c1::22–27}} additional life-years gained and {{c2::1}} additional CRC death averted per 1,000 adults screened.
extra: Figure 1: Benefits of CRC Screening (CISNET modeling)

text: During acute BP lowering in ICH, SBP {{c1::<130 mmHg}} should be avoided because it is associated with increased renal adverse events and potential worsening of physical dysfunction.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.1 (Acute BP Lowering)

text: In pregnancy, the BP target for chronic hypertension is {{c1::<140/90 mm Hg}}.
extra: Section 5.5 (Hypertension and Pregnancy) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Initial fluid resuscitation in sepsis/septic shock requires at least {{c1::30 mL/kg}} IV crystalloid, with {{c2::balanced crystalloids}} preferred over normal saline.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Fluid Resuscitation section

text: PCAB-amoxicillin dual therapy for H. pylori uses vonoprazan {{c1::20 mg twice daily}} + amoxicillin {{c2::1,000 mg 3× daily}} × 14 days.
In adults with CVD, diabetes, CKD, or 10-year CVD risk ≥7.5% (PREVENT), initiate antihypertensive medication when average BP is ≥{{c1::130/80}} mm Hg.
extra: Section 5.2.2 (BP Treatment Threshold and CVD Risk Estimation) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: A SYNTAX score of {{c1::≥33}} defines high anatomic complexity, at which PCI carries higher mortality than CABG.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — SYNTAX score threshold for PCI vs. CABG

text: For moderate hyponatremia (serum Na {{c1::120–125 mEq/L}}) in cirrhosis, restrict water to {{c2::1,000 mL/day}} and stop diuretics.
Methotrexate should be titrated to a target dose of {{c1::≥15 mg/week}} within {{c1::4–6 weeks}} of initiation.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 3 (Methotrexate Administration); conditional recommendation

text: In GCA or TAK in apparent clinical remission, immunosuppression should {{c1::not be escalated}} for {{c2::elevated inflammation markers alone}} without other signs of active disease.
To avoid toxicity, flucytosine peak concentration should be kept below {{c1::<100 mg/L}}.
Hyponatremia is classified as acute vs. chronic based on a duration cutoff of {{c1::48 hours}}.
extra: Table 1 — Classification of hyponatremia by severity

text: The risk of warfarin embryopathy is reduced (<3%) when the daily warfarin dose is {{c1::≤5 mg}}; LMWH should be substituted in the first trimester if the dose exceeds this threshold.
CAC = 0 AU supports {{c1::deferring lipid-lowering therapy}} with reassessment via repeat CAC in {{c1::3–7 years}}, absent higher-risk conditions such as FH or diabetes.
The gold standard diagnostic test for adrenal insufficiency is the standard-dose corticotropin stimulation test using {{c1::250 mcg IV}} in adults and children ≥2 years.
extra: Rec. 2.1 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: Icosapent ethyl {{c1::2 g twice daily}} may be added to statin therapy for patients with ASCVD and fasting triglycerides {{c2::135–499 mg/dL}} and LDL-C 41–100 mg/dL (REDUCE-IT criteria).
Lp(a) ≥{{c1::50 mg/dL}} (or ≥{{c1::125 nmol/L}}) is a risk-enhancing factor for ASCVD primary prevention risk stratification.
In adults with CKD G1–G4 and moderately-to-severely increased albuminuria (A2 or A3) with diabetes, KDIGO 2021 recommends starting {{c1::ACEi or ARB}} (Level 1B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.2.3; key trials IDNT, RENAAL, Micro-HOPE

text: A LI-RADS {{c1::LR-5}} observation carries a {{c2::95–99%}} probability of HCC and can establish the diagnosis noninvasively in at-risk patients.
extra: Diagnosis section / Figure 6 (LI-RADS), AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: Smoking cessation is associated with a {{c1::44%}} improvement in GERD symptoms at 1 year, compared with {{c1::18%}} in continued smokers.

IVC filter placement is restricted to patients with acute VTE (diagnosed within ~1 month) who have a {{c1::contraindication to anticoagulation}}; the filter should be {{c2::removed promptly}} once anticoagulation can be initiated.
A {{c1::treat-to-target}} strategy is conditionally recommended in PsA; acceptable targets include {{c2::minimal disease activity (MDA)}} or {{c2::DAPSA}}.
extra: Table 5 (PICO 44) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendation, TICOPA trial, ref. 113.

text: All current smokers enrolled in a lung cancer screening program must receive {{c1::smoking cessation interventions}} concurrently with screening.
extra: Practice Considerations — Shared Decision-Making subsection; Key Recommendations

text: To avoid medication-overuse headache, acute migraine medications should be limited to an average of ≤{{c1::2}} headache days per week; patients exceeding this threshold should be offered {{c2::preventive treatment}}.
extra: Key Recommendations — acute medication use limit

text: Digital breast tomosynthesis (DBT) is an acceptable primary screening modality but must be used {{c1::concurrently with digital or synthetic digital mammography}}.
A {{c1::≥30% relative decline}} in MRI-PDFF is associated with a {{c2::5-fold}} improvement in odds of NASH resolution, and serves as a surrogate marker for histological response.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statement 29; Surrogate Markers section

text: For confirmed group A streptococcal necrotizing fasciitis, definitive antibiotic therapy is {{c1::penicillin plus clindamycin}}.

When endoscopic therapy fails for ulcer bleeding, {{c1::transcatheter arterial embolization (TAE)}} is preferred over surgery as initial salvage therapy due to fewer complications and equivalent mortality, despite higher {{c2::rebleeding}} rates.
extra: Failure of Endoscopic Therapy section — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: KDIGO 2017 recommends {{c1::restricting}} calcium-based phosphate binders in adult CKD G3a–G5D receiving phosphate-lowering therapy, removing the 2009 qualifier that restriction applied {{c2::only if hypercalcemia was present}}.
The CDC-harmonized lower limit of normal for total testosterone in healthy nonobese young men is {{c1::264 ng/dL (9.2 nmol/L)}}.
For chronic migraine prevention, the optimal initial dose of onabotulinumtoxinA is {{c1::155 units}}.
A first provoked VTE in SCD warrants anticoagulation for {{c1::3–6 months}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — VTE Management table

text: The approved rifaximin course for IBS-D is {{c1::550 mg three times daily for 2 weeks}}; up to {{c2::2}} repeat treatments are safe for symptom recurrence.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — IBS-D Pharmacotherapy section (Rifaximin); Pimentel et al. NEJM 2011, Lembo et al. Gastroenterology 2016

text: In the Swedish case-crossover study (Janszky 2018), bronchoscopy carried an IE odds ratio of {{c1::16.00}} (95% CI 2.12–120.65) in high-risk inpatients within 12 weeks.
extra: Table 3 / Janszky 2018 section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: In adult kidney transplant recipients, the recommended first-line antihypertensive agents are {{c1::dihydropyridine CCB}} or {{c1::ARB}} (Level 1C).
extra: Chapter 4, Recommendation 4.1; meta-analysis shows 26% reduction in graft loss with CCB vs. placebo

text: In AF, an estimated annual thromboembolic risk of {{c1::≥2%/year}} generally warrants oral anticoagulation; a risk of ~{{c2::1%/year}} may still warrant a DOAC given its favorable risk-benefit profile.

Before upper endoscopy for UGIB, infuse {{c1::erythromycin 250 mg IV}} over 20–30 minutes, administered {{c2::20–90 minutes}} before the procedure to reduce repeat endoscopy and shorten hospital stay.
extra: Pre-endoscopic Medical Therapy: Prokinetic section — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: Vancomycin dosing for MRSA SSTI in adults is {{c1::30 mg/kg/day IV in 2 divided doses}}, targeting a trough of {{c2::15–20 µg/mL}} in severe infections.
In HF patients with AF, anticoagulation with a {{c1::DOAC}} (preferred over warfarin) is recommended for CHA₂DS₂-VASc ≥{{c2::2}} in men or ≥{{c2::3}} in women.

Low-dose aspirin reduces {{c1::nonfatal MI}} (Peto OR 0.88) and {{c2::nonfatal ischemic stroke}} (Peto OR 0.88) but does NOT significantly reduce cardiovascular or all-cause mortality in primary prevention.
extra: Supporting Evidence / Benefits of Preventive Medication; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: An ABI of {{c1::≤0.90}} is diagnostic of PAD and triggers further evaluation and GDMT initiation.
extra: Section 3.1 (Resting ABI and Physiological Testing) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: Celiac disease prevalence in patients with {{c1::type 1 diabetes mellitus}} is approximately {{c2::3%–10%}}, warranting routine CD screening in this population.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Screening section, Table 4

text: When tapering DMARDs, {{c1::dose reduction}} is preferred over gradual taper-off, and {{c2::gradual discontinuation}} is preferred over abrupt stopping.
The most commonly used ARR cutoff for a positive PA screen is {{c1::30}} (PAC in ng/dL / PRA in ng/mL/h) or {{c2::750}} (PAC in pmol/L / PRA in ng/mL/h).
Moderate fracture risk (adults ≥40 years) is defined as FRAX (GC-adjusted) 10-year major OP fracture risk {{c1::10–19%}} OR hip fracture risk {{c2::>1% and <3%}}.

Trimester-specific TSH targets in pregnancy per ATA 2014:
1st trimester: {{c1::0.1–2.5 mIU/L}}
2nd trimester: {{c2::0.2–3.0 mIU/L}}
3rd trimester: {{c3::0.3–3.0 mIU/L}}
For uncomplicated gonorrhea (cervix/urethra/rectum), ceftriaxone is dosed at {{c1::500 mg}} IM for persons <150 kg and {{c2::1 g}} IM for persons ≥150 kg — single dose.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Gonococcal Infections, Uncomplicated section

text: Aspirin for primary prevention is classified as {{c1::Class III (Harm)}} in adults older than {{c2::70 years}}.
Post-COVID-19 discharge prophylaxis in high-risk patients: rivaroxaban {{c1::10 mg once daily}} for {{c2::35 days}} (MICHELLE trial).
extra: ITAC 2022, Appendix 6, Q10 Conclusions; MICHELLE RCT

text: For atrial fibrillation thromboprophylaxis in CKD G1–G4, KDIGO 2024 recommends {{c1::NOACs}} over {{c1::warfarin}} (Rec. 3.16.1, 1C).
Neonatal candidemia is treated with amphotericin B deoxycholate {{c1::1 mg/kg IV daily}} OR fluconazole {{c2::12 mg/kg IV/PO daily}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section VII (Neonatal), Thresholds & Doses

text: Suppressive antiviral therapy in HSV-positive pregnancies should begin at {{c1::36 weeks' gestation}}, using acyclovir 400 mg TID or valacyclovir {{c2::500 mg BID}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Genital Herpes, Pregnant Women section

text: For hospitalized {{c1::nonsevere}} CAP with a positive respiratory virus test, empiric antibiotics {{c2::should}} be prescribed.
extra: Question 2 (Empiric Antibiotics with Positive Virus Test) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; conditional, very low-quality evidence.

text: In patients at increased CI-AKI risk, use {{c1::iso-osmolar or low-osmolar}} rather than {{c1::high-osmolar}} iodinated contrast media (Grade 1B).

In adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome (FCS) and fasting TG ≥{{c1::1000 mg/dL}}, {{c2::olezarsen}} (an {{c3::apoC-III inhibitor}}) 80 mg SQ once monthly is indicated to reduce TG and pancreatitis risk.
For patients on long-term aspirin for primary prevention, modeling data support considering stopping aspirin around age {{c1::75}} years as net benefit diminishes with advancing age.
extra: Stopping Age section; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: KDIGO 2021 recommends using {{c1::standardized office BP measurement}} in preference to routine office BP for all BP management decisions in adults with CKD (Level 1B).
For SCD patients with CKD and worsening anemia, combination therapy with {{c1::hydroxyurea}} and {{c2::erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (ESAs)}} is suggested.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Hydroxyurea and Erythropoiesis-Stimulating Agents section

text: In cancer patients with thrombocytopenia, full-dose anticoagulation is generally maintained when platelets are ≥{{c1::50 × 10⁹/L}}; modified-dose anticoagulation is considered for platelets {{c1::20–50 × 10⁹/L}}.

If overcorrection is detected, both guidelines recommend relowering serum sodium with {{c1::hypotonic fluids or desmopressin}}; the US guideline triggers this intervention at baseline SNa {{c2::<120 mmol/L}}.
In stable CIS or relapsing MS, serial MRI at least annually for {{c1::5 years}} is an acceptable alternative to DMT if the patient has been off DMT for ≥{{c2::2 years}} with no new MRI activity.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level C, Statement 8 / Starting Rec 8

text: ATA 2015 TSH suppression target for intermediate-risk DTC (initial): {{c1::0.1–0.5 mU/L}}.
{{c1::Hydroxychloroquine}} and {{c1::methotrexate}} are strongly recommended against for all OA, including erosive hand OA.
When switching due to inadequate response to a bDMARD or tsDMARD, switching to a {{c1::different class}} is conditionally recommended over switching {{c1::within the same class}}.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 4 (Treatment Modification); conditional recommendation

text: A long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist is recommended (1B) in adults with T2D and CKD not at glycemic target despite {{c1::metformin and SGLT2i}}, or when those agents are contraindicated.
IDSA/SHEA 2016 recommends measuring antibiotic use in {{c1::days of therapy (DOTs)}} rather than {{c2::defined daily doses (DDDs)}}.
extra: Recommendation 21 (Section XX); NHSN AUR Module requires DOT reporting.

text: For resistant hypertension, the preferred 4th-line add-on agent is {{c1::spironolactone 25–50 mg/day}} (requires eGFR ≥{{c2::45}} mL/min/1.73 m²); if MRA is not tolerated, use {{c3::amiloride 10–20 mg/day}}.
extra: Section 5.6 (Resistant Hypertension and Renal Denervation) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: PMR follow-up should occur every {{c1::4–8 weeks}} in year 1, and every {{c2::8–12 weeks}} in year 2.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Overarching Principle 7

text: For extended-phase VTE anticoagulation, the preferred reduced dose of apixaban is {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}}.
For multiple myeloma patients on IMiD-based therapy, VTE prophylaxis options include LMWH, aspirin {{c1::100 mg/day}}, warfarin (INR {{c2::2–3}}), or apixaban 2.5 mg twice daily.
Lifelong surveillance for Cushing's syndrome recurrence is recommended for all patients EXCEPT those with a resected adrenal adenoma with CT density {{c1::<10 Hounsfield units}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Rec. 7.1 / Section 7 (1|QQQE)

text: Step-down from echinocandin to fluconazole for candidemia should occur within {{c1::5–7 days}} when the patient is clinically stable, blood cultures are negative, and the isolate is fluconazole-susceptible.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section I, Recommendation 4

text: When metformin + SGLT2i are insufficient or contraindicated in T2D + CKD, add a {{c1::long-acting GLP-1 RA}}, prioritizing agents with proven CV benefit (e.g., liraglutide, semaglutide [injectable], dulaglutide).
Hemodynamic criteria for cardiogenic shock include cardiac index <{{c1::2.2 L/min/m²}}, PCWP >{{c2::15 mmHg}}, and SBP <{{c3::90 mmHg}}.
In pregnancy, chlamydia is treated with {{c1::azithromycin 1 g orally}} as a single dose; doxycycline is {{c2::contraindicated}} in the 2nd and 3rd trimesters.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Chlamydial Infections / Pregnant Women section

text: For VAP, add empiric MRSA coverage when the ICU MRSA rate exceeds {{c1::10–20%}} of S. aureus isolates, or when prevalence is unknown.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X; Thresholds & Doses

text: In the DENSE trial, supplemental MRI screening was associated with a false-positive rate of {{c1::79.8}} per 1000 and a biopsy rate of {{c2::62.7}} per 1000 women screened.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Supplemental Screening — DENSE RCT (Bakker et al., NEJM 2019)

text: On echocardiography, a TRV {{c1::>2.8 m/s}} indicates intermediate or high probability of PH and triggers further workup.
extra: Table 16; Section on echocardiographic probability — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: The strongest predictor of future suicide death is {{c1::a previous suicide attempt}}, which should inform clinical vigilance even in the absence of a formal screening recommendation.

After bilateral adrenalectomy for Cushing's disease, patients should be monitored with regular {{c1::pituitary MRI}} and {{c2::ACTH levels}} to detect corticotroph tumor progression (Nelson's syndrome).
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Rec. 6.1b / Section 6.1 (1|QQQE)

text: The 2026 guideline recommends {{c1::against}} using dietary supplements (e.g., fish oil, red yeast rice, plant sterols) to lower LDL-C or TG in individuals with dyslipidemia.
Severe DKA is defined by β-hydroxybutyrate {{c1::>6.0 mmol/L}}, pH {{c2::<7.0}}, and bicarbonate {{c3::<10 mmol/L}}.
Lp(a) ≥{{c1::250 nmol/L (≥100 mg/dL)}} is associated with approximately {{c1::2}}-fold increased ASCVD risk.

pH monitoring {{c1::off PPIs (after 7-day washout)}} is recommended when GERD diagnosis is unestablished or before antireflux surgery; {{c2::impedance-pH monitoring on twice-daily PPIs}} is preferred for patients with established GERD and persistent symptoms.
Stage 1 hypertension (SBP 130–139 / DBP 80–89 mm Hg) is managed with {{c1::nonpharmacological therapy alone}} when 10-year ASCVD risk is {{c2::<10%}}.
A BMI reduction of ≥{{c1::3.5}} units is associated with a {{c2::40%}} reduction in frequent GERD symptoms.
When ICIs are used as bridging therapy before liver transplantation, they must be discontinued at least {{c1::3 months}} prior to transplantation.
extra: Systemic Therapy section — ICI bridging therapy, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: When oral bisphosphonates are contraindicated or not tolerated in GIOP, the step-down preference order is: {{c1::IV bisphosphonate}} → {{c1::teriparatide}} → {{c1::denosumab}} → raloxifene (postmenopausal women only).
For hospitalized severe CAP, systemic corticosteroids {{c1::should}} be administered, but this recommendation explicitly {{c2::excludes influenza}} pneumonia.
extra: Question 4 (Corticosteroids) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; conditional, low-quality evidence.

text: Addition of a bDMARD or tsDMARD is conditionally avoided within {{c1::12 months}} of a serious infection; a csDMARD combination is preferred instead during this period.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific Populations); conditional recommendation

text: The 2022 guideline conditionally recommends {{c1::against}} antireflux surgery in IPF for the purpose of improving {{c2::respiratory outcomes}}, noting a 30-day surgical complication rate of ~15%.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of IPF (Antireflux Surgery section); WRAP-IPF trial (Raghu 2018)

text: ARR screening for primary aldosteronism is indicated in patients with: spontaneous or diuretic-induced {{c1::hypokalemia}}, adrenal {{c2::incidentaloma}}, sleep apnea, family history of early-onset hypertension or stroke <40 years, and all hypertensive first-degree relatives of PA patients.
Penicillin allergy is reported in {{c1::10%–15%}} of all hospitalized patients and {{c2::15%–24%}} of those requiring antibiotics — the target population for ASP allergy assessment programs.
In LQTS, an ICD is indicated for high-risk patients with {{c1::recurrent syncope despite beta-blocker therapy}}.
In UGIB patients with pre-existing cardiovascular disease, the RBC transfusion threshold is hemoglobin {{c1::< 8 g/dL}} (vs. < 7 g/dL in other stable patients).
extra: Red Blood Cell Transfusion section, Table 3 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: First-line treatment for unilateral PA (APA or UAH) is {{c1::laparoscopic adrenalectomy}}; if surgery is refused or contraindicated, treat with a {{c2::mineralocorticoid receptor (MR) antagonist}}.

HBsAg-negative, anti-HBc–positive patients receiving {{c1::rituximab}} or undergoing {{c1::stem cell transplantation}} should receive anti-HBV {{c2::prophylaxis}} (rather than monitoring with on-demand therapy).
The four drug classes of GDMT for HFrEF are: {{c1::ARNi (or ACEi/ARB)}}, {{c1::beta blocker}}, {{c1::MRA}}, and {{c1::SGLT2 inhibitor}}.
extra: Section 7.3 / Table 14 — Target doses for all four GDMT drug classes

text: Icosapent ethyl {{c1::4 g/day}} is indicated for CCD patients with ASCVD, triglycerides {{c2::150–499 mg/dL}}, and LDL-C <100 mg/dL on statin therapy.
Non-purulent cellulitis (mild) requires {{c1::antistreptococcal}} coverage alone; MRSA coverage is added only if penetrating trauma, MRSA risk factors, purulent drainage, or SIRS are present.
extra: Section IV, Recommendations 14–19; Figure 1 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text: Confirmatory testing after a positive ARR may be omitted if all three criteria are met simultaneously: spontaneous {{c1::hypokalemia}}, undetectable {{c2::renin}}, and PAC >{{c3::20 ng/dL (550 pmol/L)}}.
After starting or dose-escalating an ACEi or ARB in SCD, check GFR and serum potassium within {{c1::1 week}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Albuminuria Management section

text: H. pylori resistance rates in the United States are <5% for {{c1::amoxicillin, tetracycline, and rifabutin}}.
In active lupus nephritis, {{c1::voclosporin}} is recommended as an {{c2::add-on}} immunosuppressive agent (not monotherapy), based on FDA and EMA approval.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Guideline Overview; Key Recommendation

text: Gepants or lasmiditan are appropriate acute alternatives when triptans are {{c1::contraindicated}} or when {{c2::two or more oral triptans}} have failed.
extra: Table 3 — Criteria for initiating acute treatment with gepants, ditans, or neuromodulatory devices

text: Both VAP and HAP should be treated with a {{c1::7-day}} antibiotic course, regardless of pathogen, with de-escalation guided by culture results.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Sections XXI–XXII (Duration of therapy)

text: Glucocorticoid prophylaxis after RAI for Graves' orbitopathy: {{c1::prednisone 0.4–0.5 mg/kg/day}} starting {{c2::1–3 days post-RAI}}, continued 1 month then tapered over 2 months.
For HHS without significant ketonemia, start IV insulin at {{c1::0.05 units/kg/h}}; if mixed DKA/HHS (β-hydroxybutyrate ≥3.0 mmol/L), use {{c2::0.1 units/kg/h}}.
{{c1::Ocrelizumab}} is the only DMT with demonstrated effect on disability progression in {{c1::primary progressive MS (PPMS)}}, and should be offered to ambulatory patients likely to benefit.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level B, Starting Rec 17 / Statement 17

text: The ADA Standards of Care defines older adults as age {{c1::≥65}} years and adolescents as age {{c2::12–17}} years.
Prophylactic antibiotics {{c1::should NOT be used}} in sterile pancreatic necrosis; antibiotics are reserved for {{c2::confirmed or strongly suspected infected necrosis}}.
extra: Antibiotics in AP section — Dellinger et al., Ann Surg 2007

text: Bezlotoxumab should be used with caution or reserved in patients with a history of {{c1::congestive heart failure (CHF)}}, per FDA warning, only when benefit outweighs risk.

In STEMI, primary PCI is preferred over fibrinolysis when total ischemic time from presentation to PCI can be kept {{c1::<120 minutes}}; fibrinolysis is used only if anticipated door-to-balloon time exceeds {{c1::120 minutes}}.
extra: Section 5.1 — Primary PCI superior to fibrinolysis; fibrinolysis only if door-to-balloon delay >120 min

text: The recommended adult prednisone dose for initial ITP therapy is {{c1::0.5–2 mg/kg/day}}.

First-line medical treatment for bilateral adrenal hyperplasia is an MR antagonist; {{c1::spironolactone}} (starting {{c2::12.5–25 mg/d}}, max 100 mg/d) is preferred, with {{c3::eplerenone}} (25 mg twice daily) as an alternative.
In membranous nephropathy, high-risk features include eGFR <60 ml/min per 1.73 m² and/or proteinuria >{{c1::8 g/d}} for >6 months, serum albumin <{{c2::25 g/l}}, or anti-PLA2R >{{c3::50 RU/ml}}.
{{c1::Radial}} access is preferred over {{c1::femoral}} access for PCI in ACS to reduce bleeding, vascular complications, and mortality.
For HIT with thrombosis (HITT), rivaroxaban is dosed at {{c1::15 mg twice daily × 3 weeks}}, then {{c1::20 mg once daily}}.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Thresholds & Doses; Table 2

text: {{c1::Adrenal insufficiency}} (primary and secondary) can mimic SIAD and should be excluded in all patients presenting with euvolemic hyponatremia.
For all adults with glomerular disease, the target SBP is <{{c1::120 mm Hg}}, achieved first with {{c2::ACEi or ARB}}.
Recommended antibiotic duration for cellulitis is {{c1::5 days}}, extended if there is no clinical improvement within that period.
For VAP, a {{c1::8-day}} course has equivalent outcomes to a 15-day course and is associated with ~4–5 more antibiotic-free days.
extra: Recommendation 14 (Section XIII) and Table 2, IDSA/SHEA 2016 ASP Guideline.

text: For PAH patients without cardiopulmonary comorbidities at low or intermediate risk, initial therapy is oral combination of an {{c1::ERA}} plus a {{c2::PDE5i}} (e.g., ambrisentan + tadalafil or macitentan + tadalafil).
extra: Recommendation Tables 8–10 (PAH treatment, pp. 3668–3670) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: In adults up to age 65, SSRIs are associated with an absolute risk of suicide attempts of {{c1::0.7%}} vs. {{c1::0.3%}} with placebo (OR 1.53 [95% CI 1.09–2.15]).
extra: Supporting Evidence: Harms of Screening and Treatment — 44 RCTs, USPSTF 2023

text: The three criteria for PPF are: (1) worsening {{c1::respiratory symptoms}}, (2) physiological progression (FVC or DlCO decline), and (3) {{c2::radiological progression}} on HRCT.
Treat HBeAg-positive immune-active CHB when HBV DNA >{{c1::20,000 IU/mL}} AND ALT ≥{{c2::2× ULN}}.
The AASLD 2018 ALT upper limit of normal for management decisions is {{c1::35 U/L}} in males and {{c2::25 U/L}} in females.
The 2020 AGA guideline recommends {{c1::against}} routine gastric biopsies to diagnose atrophic gastritis in patients with IDA — conditional recommendation, very-low-quality evidence.
In IgAN, ACEi or ARB at maximally tolerated dose is indicated for all patients with proteinuria >{{c1::0.5 g/d}}, regardless of hypertension status.
KDIGO 2022 recommends an individualized HbA1c target of {{c1::<6.5% to <8.0%}} in non-dialysis CKD; this target is {{c2::not applicable}} to dialysis patients.
extra: Recommendation 2.2.1 / Figure 14 (p. S23, S59–S62); evidence grade 1C

text: Transcatheter mitral edge-to-edge repair (TEER) for secondary MR requires LVEF {{c1::20–50%}}, LVESD ≤{{c2::70 mm}}, and PA systolic pressure ≤{{c3::70 mmHg}} on GDMT (COAPT criteria).

Fluconazole is an acceptable alternative to echinocandins for candidemia only if the patient is {{c1::not critically ill}}, has had {{c2::no prior azole exposure}}, and is unlikely to have a fluconazole-resistant species.

Rivaroxaban dosing in AF by CrCl:
CrCl >50 mL/min → {{c1::20 mg daily}}
CrCl 15–50 mL/min → {{c2::15 mg daily}}
For primary prevention, aspirin should be used at a {{c1::low dose (≤100 mg/day)}}; higher doses confer greater bleeding risk without additional ASCVD benefit.
After initiating or intensifying LLT, a lipid panel should be checked at {{c1::4–12 weeks}}, then every {{c2::6–12 months}}.
FDA-approved pharmacotherapies for tobacco cessation include {{c1::nicotine replacement therapy}}, {{c1::bupropion}}, and {{c1::varenicline}}, each combined with behavioral counseling.

For active Class III/IV lupus nephritis, induction therapy is {{c1::glucocorticoids}} plus either {{c2::low-dose IV cyclophosphamide (Euro-Lupus regimen)}} or {{c2::mycophenolic acid analogs (MPAA)}}.
A high-intensity statin is indicated in people with HIV who have a 10-year ASCVD risk {{c1::≥20%}}, LDL {{c2::≥190 mg/dL}}, or diabetes.
Adults with LDL-C ≥{{c1::190 mg/dL}} should receive {{c2::maximally tolerated statin therapy}} regardless of 10-year ASCVD risk.

For HIV with cryptococcal meningitis, ART should be initiated {{c1::2–4 weeks}} after starting antifungal therapy: {{c1::2 weeks}} if clinically improved with negative CSF cultures, {{c1::4 weeks}} otherwise.
