For children with {{c1::single-ventricle palliation}}, AABB 2023 recommends considering transfusion at a threshold of {{c2::9 g/dL}} (conditional, low certainty).
In high-risk men (African Americans or first-degree relative with prostate cancer), a PSA of {{c1::>3 ng/mL}} is a contraindication to T therapy without urological evaluation.

The preferred biomarker for myocardial injury in suspected ACS is {{c1::high-sensitivity cardiac troponin (hs-cTn)}}, with a value above the {{c2::99th percentile upper reference limit}} defining myocardial injury.
For women aged 21–29 years, USPSTF recommends cervical cancer screening with {{c1::cytology alone}} every {{c1::3 years}} (Grade A).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table; Clinical Considerations: Screening Interval

text: {{c1::Doxycycline}} should NOT be substituted for {{c1::tetracycline}} in BQT, as this substitution reduces eradication rates.
Ketoconazole for Cushing's syndrome is dosed at {{c1::400–1600 mg/d}} in divided doses every {{c2::6–8 hours}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Table 1 / Section 6.4

text: HerpeSelect EIA results for HSV-2 with an index value {{c1::<3.0}} require confirmatory testing with {{c2::Biokit or Western blot}} before being interpreted as positive.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Genital Herpes, Type-Specific Serologic Tests section

text: In uncomplicated asymptomatic patients with IDA and negative bidirectional endoscopy, AGA suggests a trial of {{c1::iron supplementation}} over routine {{c2::video capsule endoscopy}} — conditional recommendation, very-low-quality evidence.
extra: Recommendation 7 / Video Capsule Endoscopy section; Table 3

text: In PMR, {{c1::glucocorticoids}} (not NSAIDs) are first-line therapy; NSAIDs may only be used short-term for pain from co-existing conditions.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Overarching Principles

text: The warfarin INR target for a mechanical mitral valve replacement (or mechanical aortic valve with prior stroke/TIA) is {{c1::3.0 (range 2.5–3.5)}}.
Mild DKA is defined by β-hydroxybutyrate {{c1::3.0–6.0 mmol/L}}, pH {{c2::>7.25 to <7.30}}, and bicarbonate {{c3::15–18 mmol/L}}.

HRS-AKI treatment response is defined as creatinine decreasing to {{c1::<1.5 mg/dL}} or returning to within {{c2::0.3 mg/dL}} of baseline within 14 days; discontinue vasoconstrictors at day {{c3::4}} if no response at maximum tolerated dose.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: HRS-AKI Treatment; Table 11

text: Before escalating to a biologic, patients must fail ≥{{c1::2}} different NSAIDs at maximal doses over ≥{{c2::1 month}}, or have incomplete response to that same number over ≥{{c3::2 months}}.

In adults with newly diagnosed ITP and platelet count {{c1::<30×10⁹/L}} who are asymptomatic or have minor mucocutaneous bleeding, guidelines suggest {{c2::corticosteroids}} over observation (conditional recommendation).
extra: ASH 2019 ITP Guideline — Recommendation 1a; Thresholds & Doses

text: Bezlotoxumab is dosed at {{c1::10 mg/kg IV once}} infused over {{c2::60 minutes}} during the SOC antibiotic course.
The USPSTF assigns a {{c1::Grade A}} recommendation (high certainty of substantial net benefit) for CRC screening in adults aged {{c1::50–75}} years.
For recurrent ulcer bleeding after initial successful endoscopic hemostasis, {{c1::over-the-scope clips (OTSC)}} are the preferred endoscopic modality at repeat endoscopy.
extra: Recurrent Ulcer Bleeding section, Table 8 (Schmidt 2018 RCT) — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: Growth of Candida from respiratory secretions almost always represents {{c1::colonization}} and {{c2::does not require antifungal therapy}}.
An acceptable oral alternative to fondaparinux for SVT anticoagulation is {{c1::rivaroxaban 10 mg once daily}} for 45 days.
After ACS, a nonstatin agent should be added if LDL-C remains ≥{{c1::70 mg/dL}} on maximally tolerated statin therapy; intensification is reasonable at LDL-C {{c1::55 to <70 mg/dL}}.
Clinically significant portal hypertension (CSPH) is defined by HVPG ≥ {{c1::10 mm Hg}}; decompensation risk rises sharply at this threshold.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: First-line endoscopic hemostatic modalities (strong recommendation) for ulcer bleeding include {{c1::bipolar electrocoagulation}}, {{c1::heater probe}}, and {{c1::absolute ethanol injection}}.
extra: Choice of Endoscopic Hemostatic Therapy section, Table 7 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: In high-risk IE patients with long-term transcutaneous catheters (e.g., hemodialysis, central venous catheters), repeated antibiotic use to prevent IE is considered {{c1::impractical and promotes antimicrobial resistance}}; {{c2::scrupulous sterility}} is instead emphasized.
extra: Future Considerations section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Ischemia involving ≥{{c1::10%}} of the myocardium on stress imaging is a high-risk finding; referral to invasive coronary angiography is reasonable if the patient is a revascularization candidate.
ATA 2014 recommends {{c1::against}} routine combination LT4/liothyronine therapy, based on {{c2::13 RCTs}} showing no consistent benefit over LT4 monotherapy.
Overdiagnosis of prostate cancer from PSA screening is estimated at {{c1::20%–50%}} of screen-detected cancers in trial data, and {{c2::22%–42%}} by CISNET models.
extra: Rationale: Harms of Early Detection and Treatment

text: The standard initial diagnostic test for all patients with known or suspected VHD is {{c1::transthoracic echocardiography (TTE)}}.
In Marfan syndrome, aortic dissection risk during pregnancy is very low when aortic diameter is <{{c1::4.0 cm}} but substantially higher at >{{c2::4.5 cm}}.
extra: Key Recommendations — Pregnancy in Marfan syndrome

text: In cirrhotic patients, surgical resection for HCC requires {{c1::Child-Turcotte-Pugh A}}, absence of {{c2::clinically significant portal hypertension}}, and adequate future liver remnant.
extra: Surgical Resection section, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: The minimal clinically important difference (MCID) for anti-obesity pharmacotherapy used in AGA 2022 is a mean difference of ≥{{c1::3%}} total body weight loss versus placebo.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — MCID definition for benefit assessment.

text: High-intensity statin therapy consists of {{c1::atorvastatin 40–80 mg}} or {{c1::rosuvastatin 20–40 mg}} daily, achieving ≥{{c2::50%}} LDL-C reduction.
Apixaban VTE treatment dosing: {{c1::10 mg twice daily × 7 days}}, then {{c1::5 mg twice daily}} for ≥6 months.
extra: ITAC 2022, Thresholds & Doses; ADAM-VTE, CARAVAGGIO trials

text: T enanthate or cypionate starting dose is {{c1::150–200 mg IM every 2 weeks}} (or 75–100 mg/week); the mid-interval serum T target is {{c2::350–600 ng/dL}}.
ACEi or ARB is recommended (1B) for CKD with diabetes when albuminuria is {{c1::A2–A3 (ACR ≥30 mg/g)}}; dual RAS blockade (ACEi + ARB + DRI) should be {{c2::avoided}}.

For symptomatic extracranial carotid stenosis 70–99%, {{c1::CEA}} is preferred over CAS in patients aged {{c2::≥70 years}} or those undergoing revascularization within {{c2::1 week}} of the ischemic event.
extra: Section on carotid stenosis — Thresholds & Doses; 16.0% absolute benefit over 5 years

text: Vitamin E at {{c1::800 IU/day}} (rrr α-tocopherol) for 96 weeks improves NASH histology in {{c2::nondiabetic}} patients (PIVENS trial).
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 23–28; Table 6

text: ACEi or ARB is recommended (1B) for CKD G1–G4 without diabetes when albuminuria category is {{c1::A3 (severely increased, ACR >300 mg/g)}}.
Urological transurethral endoscopic procedures are associated with an odds ratio of {{c1::8.21}} (95% CI 3.54–19.05) for {{c2::enterococcal}} IE (Mohee 2014).
extra: Table 2 / Mohee 2014 section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: The 2025 AHA/ACC guideline sets a universal BP treatment goal of {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}} for all adults with hypertension.
extra: Table 1 (What Is New); Section 5.2.2 — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: In membranous nephropathy, cumulative cyclophosphamide dose should not exceed {{c1::36 g}} (limit to {{c2::10 g}} if fertility preservation is needed); halve the dose if eGFR <50 ml/min per 1.73 m².
Universal post-treatment test-of-cure for H. pylori should be performed using {{c1::urea breath test or fecal antigen test}} at least {{c2::4 weeks}} after completing therapy.
extra: Key Concept: Post-Treatment Testing for Cure (ACG 2024 H. pylori Guideline)

text: High-dose oral glucocorticoids are defined as prednisone {{c1::1 mg/kg/day up to 80 mg/day}} (or equivalent).
extra: Table 1; Thresholds & Doses — High-dose oral glucocorticoids

text: For recurrent uveitis or coexisting IBD in axSpA, {{c1::TNFi monoclonal antibodies}} (adalimumab, infliximab, certolizumab, golimumab) are preferred over {{c2::etanercept or IL-17 inhibitors}}.
Predictors of nonresponse to fluid restriction in SIAD include UNa {{c1::≥130 mmol/L}} and UOsm {{c2::≥500 mOsm/kg}}.
For polymyxin-only susceptible Acinetobacter, colistin loading dose is {{c1::5 mg/kg IV ×1}}; maintenance is {{c2::2.5 mg × (1.5 × CrCl + 30) IV q12h}}.
The PHQ-2 cutoff of {{c1::≥2}} has higher sensitivity ({{c2::0.91}}) but lower specificity ({{c2::0.67}}) compared to the PHQ-9, making it appropriate as an initial {{c3::two-step}} screen.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests — USPSTF 2023

text: Systemic corticosteroids for moderate/severe COPD exacerbations: {{c1::prednisone 40 mg/day}} (or equivalent) for {{c2::5 days}}.
Intraarticular hyaluronic acid is {{c1::conditionally recommended against}} for knee OA and {{c2::strongly recommended against}} for hip OA.
extra: Intraarticular hyaluronic acid section — Rutjes et al. 2011 (Ann Intern Med); Table 2

text: Routine use of compression stockings to prevent postthrombotic syndrome after DVT is {{c1::not recommended}} (no significant benefit, very low certainty evidence).
extra: Guidance Statement 29; Table 22 and Figure 10, pp. e595–e596

text: The FDA-approved dose of linaclotide for IBS-C is {{c1::290 μg once daily}}, with an NNT of {{c2::6}} for global symptom improvement.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — IBS-C Pharmacotherapy section (GC-C Agonists)

text: Tenecteplase {{c1::0.4 mg/kg}} for AIS is NOT recommended due to a {{c2::harm signal}}.

A Padua Prediction Score of {{c1::≥4}} identifies high VTE risk (~11% incidence without prophylaxis), indicating pharmacological prophylaxis; a score {{c1::<4}} represents low risk (~0.3%) where prophylaxis is not routinely indicated.
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Table 1 (RAMs for medical inpatients)

text: For recurrent vulvovaginal candidiasis (≥4 episodes/year), after {{c1::10–14 days}} induction therapy, maintenance is {{c2::fluconazole 150 mg weekly for 6 months}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XV, Recommendation 121

text: Prasugrel loading dose is {{c1::60 mg}}; maintenance is {{c2::10 mg daily}} if weight ≥60 kg and age <75 y, or {{c2::5 mg daily}} if weight <60 kg or age ≥75 y.
LA grade C/D erosive esophagitis requires follow-up endoscopy after PPI treatment to assess healing and evaluate for {{c1::Barrett's esophagus}}.
For EMS-transported STEMI patients, the goal FMC-to-first-device time for PPCI is ≤{{c1::90 minutes}}.
Diagnostic endoscopy for GERD should ideally be performed after stopping PPIs for {{c1::2–4 weeks}} to maximize yield for erosive esophagitis and avoid masking eosinophilic esophagitis.
The USPSTF 2021 issued an {{c1::I statement}} (insufficient evidence) for pharmacotherapy for tobacco cessation in {{c1::pregnant persons}}.
extra: Practice Considerations: Suggestions for I Statements (Pharmacotherapy in Pregnancy) — USPSTF 2021

text: The 2021 USPSTF requires a minimum smoking history of {{c1::≥20 pack-years}} for lung cancer screening eligibility (reduced from {{c2::30}} pack-years in 2013).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table; Box: A-50-80-20-15 vs. A-55-80-30-15

text: In the DENSE trial (women aged 50–75 with extremely dense breasts and negative mammogram), supplemental MRI reduced the interval invasive cancer rate from {{c1::4.7}} to {{c2::2.2}} per 1000 women screened.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Supplemental Screening — DENSE RCT (Bakker et al., NEJM 2019)

text: In a patient with a newly discovered thyroid nodule ≥1 cm, the first laboratory test to order is {{c1::serum TSH}}; if it is subnormal, perform a {{c2::radionuclide (preferably ¹²³I) thyroid scan}} before considering FNA.
For adults undergoing {{c1::cardiac surgery}}, a restrictive RBC transfusion threshold of {{c2::<7.5 g/dL}} is acceptable per AABB 2023.
extra: Recommendation 1, remark — AABB 2023 RBC Transfusion Guideline

text: For initial CDI episodes, the 2021 IDSA/SHEA update conditionally prefers {{c1::fidaxomicin}} over {{c2::vancomycin}} (moderate certainty).
extra: Recommendation I; Table 2 (PICO 1) — sustained response RR 1.16 (95% CI 1.09–1.24)

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends using {{c1::bicarbonate}} rather than lactate as the RRT buffer, especially in {{c2::circulatory shock or liver failure/lactic acidemia}}.
{{c1::Antibiotic cycling}} as a stewardship strategy is NOT recommended by IDSA/SHEA 2016 due to {{c2::insufficient supporting evidence}}.
Topical therapy for impetigo/ecthyma consists of {{c1::mupirocin or retapamulin}} applied twice daily for {{c2::5 days}}.
Transition to ticagrelor monotherapy (dropping aspirin) is recommended {{c1::≥1 month}} after PCI in patients who tolerated DAPT and are at elevated bleeding risk.
Warranty periods for prior cardiac testing: normal CCTA (no plaque/stenosis) = {{c1::2 years}}; normal stress test (adequate stress) = {{c2::1 year}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Table 7 (Warranty Period for Prior Cardiac Testing)

text: The 24-hour ABPM threshold corresponding to an office BP of 130/80 mm Hg is {{c1::125/75 mm Hg}}.
Echinocandins are NOT recommended for CNS candidiasis due to {{c1::poor CNS penetration}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XIII, Recommendation 92

text: AAV induction with rituximab: {{c1::375 mg/m²/week × 4 weeks}} OR {{c2::1 g at weeks 0 and 2}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — AAV induction rituximab (Chapter 9, S72–S77)

text: Newborns of HBsAg-positive mothers must receive {{c1::HBIG plus HBV vaccine}} within {{c2::12 hours}} of birth.

Aspirin for primary prevention is not indicated in adults already at increased bleeding risk, including those with a history of {{c1::GI ulcers}}, recent bleeding, or concurrent use of {{c2::NSAIDs, anticoagulants, or steroids}}.
extra: Key Recommendations; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: Temporal artery biopsy for GCA diagnosis should be obtained within {{c1::2 weeks}} of starting oral glucocorticoids to maximize histopathologic yield.
extra: Table 2 (GCA PICO 1–7, 9); Thresholds & Doses — Biopsy timing

text: In CPVT, adding {{c1::flecainide}} to a beta blocker suppresses ventricular arrhythmias by up to {{c2::76%}}.
After a negative temporal artery biopsy in suspected GCA, the next step is noninvasive vascular imaging ({{c1::CTA, MRA, FDG-PET, or ultrasound}}) of large vessels plus clinical assessment.
Spironolactone and eplerenone should be used with caution in CKD stage {{c1::III (GFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m²)}} and avoided in CKD stage {{c2::IV}}.
A positive vasoreactivity response in IPAH/HPAH/DPAH requires mPAP reduction {{c1::≥10 mmHg}} to an absolute value {{c1::≤40 mmHg}}, with unchanged or increased cardiac output.
extra: Recommendation Table 1 (RHC and vasoreactivity testing, p. 3648) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: Initiation of dofetilide or sotalol requires at least {{c1::3-day}} inpatient monitoring for {{c2::QTc prolongation}} and renal function.
For a purely cystic thyroid nodule (malignancy risk <1%), ATA 2015 states that FNA is {{c1::not required}}.
SGLT2 inhibitors should be avoided or used with caution when eGFR is {{c1::<30 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.

The 2016 IDSA/ATS guideline recommends diagnosing VAP using {{c1::noninvasive}} sampling (endotracheal aspiration) with {{c2::semiquantitative}} cultures rather than invasive bronchoscopic sampling with quantitative cultures.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Key Recommendations (VAP diagnosis)

text: Before considering aspirin initiation, 10-year CVD risk should be estimated using the {{c1::ACC/AHA Pooled Cohort Equations}}.
extra: Key Recommendations; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: ACR 2020 conditionally recommends {{c1::low-dose}} colchicine over high-dose colchicine for flare management due to equivalent efficacy and fewer adverse effects.
In LQTS with syncope, first-line therapy is {{c1::beta-blockers}} (preferably {{c1::nadolol or propranolol}}).
Rifamycin-based LTBI regimens are preferred over isoniazid monotherapy because of equivalent {{c1::efficacy}}, lower {{c2::hepatotoxicity}}, and higher {{c2::treatment completion rates}}.
An abnormal result from any stool-based test, CT colonography, or flexible sigmoidoscopy must be followed up with {{c1::colonoscopy}} to achieve the intended screening benefit.
extra: Table 1: Characteristics of Recommended Colorectal Cancer Screening Strategies

text: In childhood NS, target trough levels are: cyclosporine {{c1::60–150 ng/ml}}; tacrolimus {{c2::5–10 ng/ml}}.
Liver biopsy is required for {{c1::LR-M}} and {{c1::LR-TIV}} observations, and for patients without {{c2::cirrhosis or HBV}} regardless of imaging findings.
extra: Diagnosis section / Figure 6 (LI-RADS), AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: African American men have a prostate cancer mortality rate of {{c1::44.1}} per 100,000, compared with {{c2::19.1}} per 100,000 in white men — approximately {{c3::2×}} higher.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Screening for Prostate Cancer in African American Men

text: Bezlotoxumab may benefit primary CDI patients with ≥1 recurrence risk factor: age {{c1::≥65 years}}, {{c2::immunocompromised}} status, or {{c3::severe CDI}} on presentation.
Virological breakthrough during HBV treatment is defined as a >{{c1::1 log10 (10-fold)}} rise in HBV DNA from nadir.
extra: Table 6 — Definitions; Table 8 — Antiviral resistance management

text: Doxycycline PEP for bacterial STIs in MSM consists of {{c1::200 mg orally within 24 hours}} after unprotected anal sex, reducing chlamydia and syphilis by approximately {{c2::70–73%}} (no effect on gonorrhea).
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Clinical Prevention Guidance, Postexposure Prophylaxis section

text: Fluid restriction is not required for ascites management unless serum sodium is {{c1::≤125 mmol/L}}.
KDIGO 2024 gives a 1A recommendation for SGLT2 inhibitors in adults with T2D and CKD when eGFR is {{c1::≥20 ml/min/1.73 m²}}.

The ADA Standards of Care functions as a {{c1::living document}}, with interim updates published at {{c2::professional.diabetes.org}} when new evidence or regulatory changes arise between annual editions.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Revision Process section

text: Anticoagulation for cancer-associated VTE should be continued for a minimum of {{c1::6 months}}; extension up to {{c2::12 months}} is supported in patients with active cancer.
For minor noncardioembolic AIS (NIHSS ≤5) or high-risk TIA (ABCD2 ≥4), DAPT consists of {{c1::clopidogrel 300 mg load then 75 mg/day}} plus aspirin for {{c2::21 days}}, initiated within 24–72 hours.
extra: Key Recommendations; Thresholds & Doses (DAPT for minor stroke)

text: The 2024 USPSTF breast cancer screening recommendation does NOT apply to persons with {{c1::BRCA1/BRCA2 variants}}, {{c2::prior high-dose chest radiation at young age}}, prior breast cancer, or high-risk breast lesion on prior biopsy.
After initiating GCs for PMR, taper to {{c1::10 mg/day}} prednisone equivalent within {{c2::4–8 weeks}}.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 4

text: For candidemia in both nonneutropenic and neutropenic adults, {{c1::echinocandin}} is the first-line initial therapy (strong recommendation; high-quality evidence).
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section I, Recommendation 1

text: Before H. pylori post-treatment testing with urea breath test or fecal antigen test, PPIs (and likely PCABs) must be stopped for at least {{c1::2 weeks}} to avoid {{c2::false-negative}} results.
extra: Key Concept: Post-Treatment Testing for Cure (ACG 2024 H. pylori Guideline)

text: The Oxford index predicts IVCS non-response: >{{c1::8 bowel movements on day 3}}, OR {{c2::3–8 bowel movements + CRP >45 mg/L}}, predicts an ~85% colectomy rate.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC section

text: In ACS, supplemental oxygen is indicated only if SpO2 <{{c1::90%}}; the lowest observed mortality is at SpO2 {{c2::94%-96%}}.
Persons without CVD who are currently taking aspirin should {{c1::not stop}} without first consulting their {{c1::clinician}}.
extra: Key Recommendations; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: Daptomycin dosing for MRSA SSTI is {{c1::4 mg/kg IV every 24 hours}}.
For hemodynamically stable hospitalized adults, the AABB 2023 restrictive RBC transfusion threshold is {{c1::<7 g/dL}} (strong recommendation, moderate certainty).
When treating HIV with active TB on rifamycin-containing regimens, dolutegravir should be dosed at {{c1::50 mg twice daily}} (vs. once daily for 3HP preventive therapy).
Refer adults to nephrology when eGFR is {{c1::<30 ml/min/1.73 m²}}, ACR is {{c2::>300 mg/g}} with hematuria, or there is a sustained eGFR fall of >{{c3::20%}}.
For overweight/obese patients with knee and/or hip OA, clinically meaningful benefits from weight loss begin at ≥{{c1::5%}} body weight loss.
extra: Weight loss section — Messier et al. 2018 (Arthritis Care Res)

text: Acute anti-impulse therapy targets for AAS are heart rate {{c1::60–80 bpm}} and SBP <{{c1::120 mmHg}}; {{c2::IV beta blockers}} (esmolol, metoprolol, labetalol) are first-line agents.
LTOT improves survival when PaO2 {{c1::≤ 55 mmHg}} or SaO2 {{c2::≤ 88%}} at rest; it is NOT indicated for moderate resting or exercise-induced desaturation alone.
extra: GOLD 2026 — Figure 3.17 / Chapter 3 Oxygen Therapy section

text: In PMR, an initial prednisone dose of {{c1::≤7.5 mg/day}} is discouraged, and a dose of {{c2::>30 mg/day}} is strongly avoided.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 3

text: ACR 2020 recommends {{c1::against}} ULT in asymptomatic hyperuricemia; the NNT is {{c2::24}} patients treated for 3 years to prevent 1 incident gout flare.
Tofacitinib induction: {{c1::10 mg BID × 8 weeks}}; maintenance: {{c2::5 mg or 10 mg BID}} (higher-dose maintenance preferred in prior TNF-exposed patients).
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; OCTAVE trials

text: Systemic thrombolytic therapy for acute PE is reserved for patients with {{c1::systolic BP < 90 mm Hg for ≥ 15 min}} (hypotension) or those who deteriorate hemodynamically after starting anticoagulation.
A 4Ts score of {{c1::≤3}} indicates low probability for HIT: no HIT laboratory testing and no empiric non-heparin anticoagulation should be initiated.
In adults with TTG-IgA ≥10× ULN who cannot undergo biopsy, a diagnosis of {{c1::'likely CD'}} is permissible, ideally confirmed with {{c2::EMA}} on a second sample.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Diagnosis section (Evidence and rationale)

text: SGLT2 inhibitors are recommended for CCD patients with {{c1::HFrEF}} regardless of {{c2::diabetes status}} to reduce MACE and HF hospitalization.
extra: 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline, Section 4.2.8 (SGLT2 Inhibitors and GLP-1 Receptor Agonists)

text: Stress ECG is contraindicated in the presence of resting ST depression >{{c1::0.5 mm}}, or conduction abnormalities including {{c2::LBBB, WPW, or paced rhythm}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (Stress Testing Contraindications)

text: Mild DKA is admitted to a {{c1::regular or observation unit}}; moderate DKA to a {{c2::step-down or intermediate care unit}}; severe DKA or HHS to the {{c3::ICU}}.
extra: Table 2 — DKA severity classification; Key Recommendations

text: In acute pancreatitis without hypovolemia, the recommended initial IV hydration rate is {{c1::≤1.5 mL/kg/hr}} of lactated Ringer's.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — de-Madaria et al. NEJM 2022

text: Pulse IV glucocorticoid dosing in adults for ANCA vasculitis is methylprednisolone {{c1::500–1000 mg IV}} for {{c1::3–5 days}}.
LT4 should be separated by at least {{c1::4 hours}} from calcium carbonate, ferrous sulfate, bile acid sequestrants, aluminum antacids, and sucralfate.
The reduced dose of rivaroxaban for extended-phase VTE anticoagulation is {{c1::10 mg once daily}}.
In adults with uncertain LLT decision, a CAC score of {{c1::0 AU}} (with no high-risk conditions) supports {{c2::deferring therapy}} and reassessing in {{c3::3–7 years}}.
A biochemical incomplete response to DTC therapy is defined as suppressed Tg {{c1::≥1 ng/mL}} OR stimulated Tg {{c2::≥10 ng/mL}} OR rising anti-Tg antibodies, with {{c2::negative imaging}}.

First-line treatment for GRA (FH-I) is the lowest effective dose of a long-acting glucocorticoid taken {{c1::at bedtime}} to suppress the early morning ACTH surge; preferred agents are {{c2::dexamethasone (0.125–0.25 mg/d)}} or {{c3::prednisone (2.5–5 mg/d)}}.
Pharmacologic treatment of neurogenic orthostatic hypotension includes {{c1::midodrine}} and {{c1::droxidopa}}, in addition to volume expansion and compression garments.
The blood glucose target during HHS treatment is {{c1::200–250 mg/dL}} until resolution criteria are met.
Pre-capillary PH requires mPAP >20 mmHg plus PAWP {{c1::≤15 mmHg}} and PVR {{c1::>2 WU}}.
extra: Table 5 (Haemodynamic definitions of PH) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: In omeprazole equivalents, the relative acid-suppression potency of esomeprazole is {{c1::1.60}} and pantoprazole is {{c2::0.23}} (omeprazole = 1.00).
extra: GERD Medical Management section (PPIs subsection); Thresholds & Doses

text: Linezolid dosing for MRSA SSTI is {{c1::600 mg IV or PO every 12 hours}}.
In adults with LDL-C ≥{{c1::190 mg/dL}} without ASCVD or HeFH, the LDL-C goal is <{{c2::100 mg/dL}}; the goal is <{{c3::70 mg/dL}} if HeFH or additional risk factors are present.
ATA 2014 recommends {{c1::against}} treating nonthyroidal illness syndrome (sick euthyroid) with LT4 or liothyronine in critically ill patients without {{c2::pre-existing hypothyroidism}}.
The 2018 USPSTF recommends {{c1::against}} PSA-based prostate cancer screening in men aged {{c2::≥70}} years (Grade {{c1::D}}).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade D for men ≥70 y

text: In cancer patients hospitalized with COVID-19, therapeutic-dose anticoagulation may benefit {{c1::moderately ill (non-ICU)}} patients but should not be used routinely in {{c2::critically ill}} patients due to increased bleeding.
extra: ITAC 2022, Appendix 6, Q10 Conclusions; ACTION, REMAP-CAP/ATTACC/ACTIV-4a, RAPID trials

text: After radical prostatectomy, approximately {{c1::1 in 5}} men develop long-term urinary incontinence requiring pads, and approximately {{c2::2 in 3}} develop long-term erectile dysfunction.
extra: Rationale: Harms of Early Detection and Treatment

text: Recurrent cellulitis prophylaxis options include oral {{c1::penicillin or erythromycin}} bid for 4–52 weeks, or IM {{c2::benzathine penicillin}} every 2–4 weeks.
DoxyPEP ({{c1::doxycycline 200 mg}} within {{c2::72 hours}} of condomless sex) is recommended for cisgender MSM and transgender women to reduce bacterial STI incidence, with quarterly STI screening.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 6 (HIV and STI Prevention Recommendations)

text: Patients with clinically significant hepatic fibrosis ({{c1::≥F2}}) should {{c2::abstain completely}} from alcohol to prevent fibrosis progression.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 6–7 (Role of Alcohol section)

text: First-line medical therapy to slow aortic growth in Marfan syndrome and related genetic aortopathies includes {{c1::beta blockers}} and {{c1::ARBs}}; these agents are contraindicated in pregnancy.
The first-line vasopressor in septic shock is {{c1::norepinephrine}}, preferred over dopamine, epinephrine, and vasopressin.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Vasoactive Agents section (Figure 2)

text: After natalizumab discontinuation, risk of relapse and MRI-detected disease activity increases within {{c1::6 months}}; if switching to fingolimod, initiate within {{c2::8–12 weeks}} of discontinuation.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level A/B, Switching Rec 9 / Statements 9a–9b

text: For adults with ITP ≥3 months who are corticosteroid-dependent or unresponsive, ASH 2019 suggests {{c1::TPO-RA}} over {{c2::rituximab}} as second-line therapy (conditional recommendation).
In SPMS, DMT discontinuation may be advised when the patient is non-ambulatory (EDSS {{c1::≥7}}) for ≥{{c2::2 years}} without relapses or gadolinium-enhancing lesions.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level C, Stopping Rec 2 / Statement 2b

text: After taking sildenafil or vardenafil, nitrates must be withheld for ≥{{c1::24 hours}}; after tadalafil, nitrates must be withheld for ≥{{c2::48 hours}}.
UOsm {{c1::<100 mOsm/kg}} suggests primary polydipsia, low solute intake, or beer potomania as the cause of hyponatremia.
extra: Figure 1 — European guideline diagnostic algorithm; Table 1

text: Fremanezumab may be dosed as {{c1::225 mg monthly}} or {{c2::675 mg quarterly}} SC.
Compared with 2013 criteria, the 2021 A-50-80-20-15 criteria increase screening eligibility by approximately {{c1::107%}} in non-Hispanic Black adults and {{c2::112%}} in Hispanic adults.
extra: Box: A-50-80-20-15 vs. A-55-80-30-15; Thresholds & Doses

text: For more active mild-to-moderate UC, 5-ASA induction doses up to {{c1::4.8 g/day}} may provide additional benefit.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC section

text: A CAC score ≥{{c1::100 AU}} or ≥{{c1::75th percentile}} favors initiating LLT with a target LDL-C of <{{c2::70 mg/dL}}.
In very high-risk CCD patients on maximal statin ± ezetimibe, a PCSK9 monoclonal antibody is indicated when LDL-C remains ≥{{c1::70 mg/dL}} (or non-HDL-C ≥{{c2::100 mg/dL}}).
A short-term indwelling urinary catheter is defined as {{c1::<30 days}}; IDSA 2019 recommends against {{c2::screening or treating ASB}} in these patients.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section XI (Indwelling Catheters); Thresholds & Doses

text: AAV induction therapy consists of {{c1::glucocorticoids}} combined with either {{c2::cyclophosphamide}} or {{c2::rituximab}}.
Voclosporin, approved as add-on therapy for lupus nephritis, is a {{c1::calcineurin inhibitor (CNI)}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Guideline Overview; FDA/EMA approval context

text: Complex perianal fistulas in CD should be managed with {{c1::seton placement}} before initiating biologic therapy.
extra: Fistulizing Crohn's Disease section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: Percutaneous LAA occlusion (pLAAO) is a {{c1::Class 2a}} recommendation for AF patients with a long-term {{c2::contraindication to anticoagulation}} due to a nonreversible cause.
In adults with lower CVD risk (PREVENT <7.5%), initiate antihypertensive medication if BP remains ≥130/80 mm Hg after a {{c1::3- to 6-month}} trial of {{c2::lifestyle modification}}.
extra: Section 5.2.2 (BP Treatment Threshold and CVD Risk Estimation) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Only three beta blockers have proven mortality benefit in HFrEF: {{c1::bisoprolol}}, {{c1::sustained-release metoprolol succinate}}, and {{c1::carvedilol}}.
For terminating stable sustained monomorphic VT, {{c1::procainamide}} IV is preferred over amiodarone (PROCAMIO trial).
extra: Section 6 / Table 7 (Acute VA Management) — PROCAMIO trial

text: Rivaroxaban {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}} added to aspirin in high-risk stable ASCVD reduced the primary endpoint (CV death/stroke/MI) from 5.4% to {{c2::4.1%}} vs aspirin alone.
In patients with PAD and type 2 diabetes, {{c1::GLP-1 agonists (liraglutide, semaglutide)}} and {{c1::SGLT-2 inhibitors (canagliflozin, empagliflozin, dapagliflozin)}} should be used to reduce MACE.
The US guideline recommends {{c1::3% NaCl 100 mL IV over 10 min, repeated up to 3 times}} as needed for severe symptomatic hyponatremia.
The physiological PPF criterion for FVC is an absolute decline of {{c1::≥5%}} predicted within 1 year.

AAV induction with oral cyclophosphamide: {{c1::2 mg/kg/d}} × 3 months (max 6 months); reduce to {{c2::1.5 mg/kg/d}} if age >60, {{c2::1.0 mg/kg/d}} if age >70; reduce by 0.5 mg/kg/d if GFR <30 ml/min per 1.73 m².
extra: Thresholds & Doses — AAV induction cyclophosphamide (Chapter 9, S72–S77)

text:
Beta blocker target doses in HFrEF:
Bisoprolol: {{c1::10 mg/day}}
Metoprolol succinate: {{c2::200 mg/day}}
Carvedilol: {{c3::25–50 mg twice daily}}
Lp(a) ≥{{c1::200 nmol/L}} (≥{{c1::75 mg/dL}}) warrants referral to a {{c2::lipid specialist}}.
IVT (alteplase or tenecteplase) is recommended for eligible adults with AIS within {{c1::4.5 hours}} of symptom onset.

All adults with resistant hypertension should be screened for primary aldosteronism {{c1::regardless of whether hypokalemia is present}}; most antihypertensives except {{c2::MRA}} can be continued during initial screening.
Each prone positioning session in ARDS should last {{c1::≥ 16 consecutive hours}} to achieve a mortality benefit.
extra: Recommendation 7.2, Domain 7: Prone Positioning — PROSEVA trial basis; 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: For isolated subsegmental PE without proximal DVT and {{c1::low recurrence risk}}, clinical surveillance is preferred over anticoagulation.
extra: Guidance Statement 3; Summary of Recommendations, p. e546

text: In IgAN, a 6-month glucocorticoid course is considered only if proteinuria remains >{{c1::0.75–1 g/d}} after ≥{{c2::90 days}} of optimized supportive care.

The medication-overuse headache threshold is ≥{{c1::10}} days/month for {{c2::triptans, ergot derivatives, opioids, or combination analgesics}}, and ≥{{c3::15}} days/month for nonopioid analgesics, acetaminophen, or NSAIDs.
extra: Table 5 — ICHD-3 criteria for medication-overuse headache

text: In Marfan syndrome, elective aortic root replacement is indicated at ≥{{c1::5.0 cm}}; earlier repair at {{c1::4.5–5.0 cm}} is reasonable with family history of dissection, rapid growth, or pregnancy planning.
extra: Key Recommendations; Thresholds & Doses — Marfan syndrome

text: According to ATA 2014, {{c1::levothyroxine (LT4)}} is the standard of care for hypothyroidism and is preferred over thyroid extracts or combination therapy.
extra: ATA 2014 Hypothyroidism Guideline, Recommendation 1a (p. 1676) — Strong, Moderate-quality evidence.

text: Nonresponsive celiac disease is defined as persistent symptoms despite GFD for ≥{{c1::6–12 months}}, requiring systematic workup including reassessment of diagnosis, dietitian-guided gluten exposure review, and evaluation for {{c2::functional disorder, food intolerances, microscopic colitis, SIBO, and pancreatic insufficiency}}.
In IgA-sufficient children younger than {{c1::2 years}}, {{c2::TTG-IgA alone}} (without DGP-IgG) is sufficient for celiac disease screening.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Testing in Children section

text: ACR 2020 identifies {{c1::allopurinol}} as the preferred first-line ULT for all gout patients, including those with moderate-to-severe CKD (stage ≥3).
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2 (Choice of initial ULT and prophylaxis)

text: When idarucizumab is unavailable for dabigatran-associated ICH, the alternative reversal agent is {{c1::aPCC (FEIBA) 50 U/kg}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1; Thresholds & Doses

text: Clarithromycin- and levofloxacin-containing regimens should NOT be used empirically for H. pylori because US resistance rates are approximately {{c1::31.5%}} and {{c2::~40%}}, respectively.
extra: Key Concept 6 & Table 2 (ACG 2024 H. pylori Guideline)

text: A 'prolonged' high-pressure recruitment maneuver is defined as airway pressure {{c1::≥ 35 cmH₂O}} maintained for {{c2::≥ 1 minute}}; the guideline strongly recommends {{c3::against}} its use.
extra: Recommendation 6.3, Domain 6: Recruitment Maneuvers — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: In RA patients with a prior lymphoproliferative disorder for which rituximab is an approved therapy, {{c1::rituximab}} is conditionally recommended over other DMARDs.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific Populations); conditional recommendation

text: ESUS should NOT be treated empirically with {{c1::anticoagulation}} or {{c1::ticagrelor}}, as randomized trials (NAVIGATE ESUS, RESPECT ESUS) showed no benefit over {{c2::aspirin}}.
Albumin dosing for HRS-AKI is {{c1::1 g/kg on day 1}}, then {{c2::40–50 g/day}} for the duration of vasoconstrictor therapy.

Pneumococcal vaccination is recommended for all celiac disease patients due to a {{c1::~2-fold}} increased risk of pneumococcal infection, driven in part by {{c2::hyposplenism}} found in approximately one-third of CD patients.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Pneumococcal Vaccine section

text: After ileocolonic resection in CD, colonoscopy should be performed at {{c1::6 months}} post-resection to assess for endoscopic recurrence.
extra: Postoperative Crohn's Disease section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: Primary, secondary, and early latent syphilis is treated with {{c1::benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM}} as a {{c2::single dose}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Syphilis, Primary and Secondary / Latent sections

text: IPC devices for DVT prophylaxis should be started {{c1::from the day of ICH diagnosis}}; graduated compression stockings alone are {{c2::not effective}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.3.3 (Thromboprophylaxis); CLOTS trials

text: In CTEPH: lifelong {{c1::anticoagulation}} is mandatory; {{c2::PEA}} is the treatment of choice for surgically accessible lesions; {{c3::BPA}} is recommended for inoperable patients or residual PH after PEA.
extra: Recommendation Table 24 (CTEPH management, p. 3698) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: Pharmacological VTE prophylaxis (UFH or LMWH) should be initiated {{c1::48–96 hours}} after ICH onset.
Liraglutide titration for obesity begins at {{c1::0.6 mg}} daily for 7 days, then increases weekly to reach a maintenance dose of {{c2::3.0 mg}} SQ daily.
Late latent or syphilis of unknown duration is treated with benzathine penicillin G {{c1::2.4 million units IM weekly × 3 doses}} ({{c1::7.2 million units}} total).
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Syphilis, Latent Syphilis section

text: Shunt closure (ASD/VSD/PDA) is recommended at PVR {{c1::<3 WU}}, should be considered at PVR {{c1::3–5 WU}}, and is not recommended for ASD at PVR {{c1::>5 WU}} despite PAH treatment.
In critically ill medical inpatients, {{c1::LMWH}} is preferred over {{c2::UFH}} for VTE prophylaxis (conditional recommendation).
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 4–5 (Critically ill patients: pharmacological prophylaxis)

text: In the 2022 USPSTF update, the lower age threshold for aspirin shared decision-making was changed from {{c1::50}} years (2016) to {{c2::40}} years.
extra: What's New section; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: In adults ≥40 years on GCs, fracture risk should be reassessed clinically every {{c1::12 months}} and BMD repeated every {{c2::1–3 years}}.
The ERSPC number needed to screen (men 55–69 years) to prevent 1 prostate cancer death over 13 years is {{c1::781}} (95% CI, 490–1929).
A lenient rate control strategy targets a resting HR of {{c1::<110 bpm}} and is acceptable in most stable AF patients (based on RACE II).
For second or subsequent CDI recurrences, one option is vancomycin 125 mg 4× daily × 10 days followed by rifaximin {{c1::400 mg 3 times daily}} × {{c2::20 days}}.
In real-world data, tolvaptan caused excessive correction (>12 mmol/L/day) in {{c1::23%}} of patients with profound hyponatremia.
extra: Vaptans section — Tzoulis et al. real-world data

text: In non-intubated AHRF (excluding cardiogenic pulmonary edema and COPD), {{c1::HFNO}} is recommended over conventional oxygen therapy to reduce {{c2::intubation risk}}.
extra: Recommendation 3.1, Domain 3: HFNO — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: For AF-related ischemic stroke or TIA, {{c1::a DOAC}} is preferred over warfarin, except in patients with {{c2::moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis}} or {{c2::mechanical heart valves}}.
extra: Section 5.4.1 — DOACs preferred over VKA for nonvalvular AF; 19% reduction in stroke/systemic embolism vs warfarin

text: Finerenone (nonsteroidal MRA) is recommended (2A) for adults with T2D and CKD with eGFR >{{c1::25 ml/min/1.73 m²}}, normal serum potassium (K⁺ ≤{{c2::4.8 mmol/l}}), and persistent albuminuria >30 mg/g despite maximum-tolerated RASi.
When measuring free testosterone, the preferred method is {{c1::equilibrium dialysis}} (or accurate calculation); {{c1::direct analog-based immunoassays}} should NOT be used.
USPSTF recommends against cervical cancer screening in women who have had a hysterectomy with cervical removal and no history of {{c1::CIN 2/3 or cervical cancer}} (Grade D).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade D; Key Recommendations

text: The USPSTF assigns a {{c1::Grade B}} recommendation (moderate certainty of moderate net benefit) for CRC screening in adults aged {{c1::45–49}} years.
For cancer-associated VTE, {{c1::DOACs (apixaban, edoxaban, rivaroxaban)}} are preferred over {{c2::VKA}} for both initial and long-term treatment.
Adrenal CT alone misclassifies the cause of primary aldosteronism in {{c1::37.8%}} of patients, which is why {{c2::bilateral adrenal venous sampling (AVS)}} is required for all surgical candidates.
For confirmed antiphospholipid syndrome requiring anticoagulation, use {{c1::VKA}} over DOAC therapy, targeting an INR of {{c2::2.5}}.

The 9H regimen consists of isoniazid 5 mg/kg daily (adults; max 300 mg) given {{c1::270 times}}; the twice-weekly variant requires {{c2::directly observed therapy (DOT)}} and consists of {{c3::76 doses}}.
extra: Table 4; Alternative Regimens: Six or Nine Months of Daily Isoniazid

text: ICD is recommended for secondary prevention in survivors of {{c1::SCA or hemodynamically significant VT}} not due to a reversible cause, regardless of {{c2::LVEF}} (Class I).
{{c1::LMWH thromboprophylaxis}} should be administered to all hospitalized patients with acute colitis, including those with {{c2::active rectal bleeding}}.
According to AAN 2018, counsel people with newly diagnosed MS about DMT options at a {{c1::dedicated follow-up treatment visit}}, not at the time of {{c2::diagnosis}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level B, Statement 1

text: In lupus nephritis, complete response is defined as proteinuria <{{c1::0.5 g/g (PCR)}} within 6–12 months.

Routine pulmonary function testing is NOT recommended in asymptomatic SCD patients; order when there is {{c1::wheezing, unexplained dyspnea, recurrent acute chest syndrome, or abnormal 6-minute walk test}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Pulmonary Function Screening section

text: When ceftriaxone is unavailable, an alternative gonorrhea regimen is {{c1::gentamicin 240 mg IM}} plus {{c1::azithromycin 2 g orally}}, each as a single dose.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Gonococcal Infections, Thresholds & Doses

text: The KDIGO 2024 Lupus Nephritis Guideline is a focused update of the LN chapter from the {{c1::2021 KDIGO Glomerular Diseases Guideline}}, primarily addressing {{c2::newly FDA/EMA-approved therapies}} (belimumab and voclosporin).
In active PsA despite TNFi monotherapy, switching to a {{c1::different TNFi}} is preferred over switching to IL-17i, IL-12/23i, abatacept, or tofacitinib.
extra: Table 3 — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendation.

text: Childhood NS induction: prednisone/prednisolone {{c1::60 mg/m²/d or 2 mg/kg/d}} (max {{c1::60 mg/d}}) daily for 4–6 weeks, then {{c2::40 mg/m² or 1.5 mg/kg}} (max 50 mg) alternate-day for 4–6 weeks.
In PLHIV aged 40–75 years on stable antiretroviral therapy, {{c1::pitavastatin}} is the preferred statin to minimize drug–drug interactions.
For secondary prevention in CCD, the preferred aspirin dose is {{c1::81 mg daily}}; {{c2::clopidogrel}} is an acceptable alternative if aspirin is not tolerated.

A {{c1::6-month}} trial of DHEA {{c2::25–50 mg orally each morning}} is offered to women with PAI who have low libido, depression, or low energy despite optimized glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid therapy.
extra: Rec. 3.11–3.13 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: After ICD implantation for syncope with LVEF <35%, the recommended symptom-free driving restriction is {{c1::3 months}}.
In SCD patients with CKD receiving ESA therapy, hold or reduce the ESA if hemoglobin exceeds {{c1::10 g/dL}} (hematocrit {{c1::30%}}) to reduce risk of vaso-occlusion, stroke, and VTE.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Hydroxyurea and Erythropoiesis-Stimulating Agents section

text: The standard vancomycin regimen for initial or first recurrent CDI is {{c1::125 mg orally 4 times daily}} for {{c2::10 days}}.
For RAI-refractory progressive metastatic DTC, ATA 2015 recommends first-line kinase inhibitor therapy with {{c1::sorafenib or lenvatinib}}.
When transitioning from IV to subcutaneous insulin, overlap subcutaneous insulin {{c1::1–2 hours}} before discontinuing the IV infusion to prevent rebound hyperglycemia or ketoacidosis.
extra: Figure 5 — Transition to maintenance subcutaneous insulin; Key Recommendations

text: The FDA-approved rituximab maintenance dose for GPA/MPA is {{c1::500 mg IV every 6 months}}.
Initial IV fluid resuscitation in hyperglycemic crises is given at {{c1::500–1,000 mL/h}} for the first {{c2::2–4 hours}} in adults without cardiac or renal compromise.
For subacute HIT A, ASH 2018 recommends preferring a {{c1::DOAC}} over {{c2::VKA}} for anticoagulation.
In immune-tolerant CHB (HBeAg positive, HBV DNA >1,000,000 IU/mL, normal ALT, no significant fibrosis), treatment should {{c1::not}} be initiated; ALT should be monitored every {{c2::6 months}}.
For fistulizing Crohn's disease, infliximab is dosed at {{c1::5 mg/kg}} at weeks {{c2::0, 2, and 6}}, then every 8 weeks.
extra: Fistulizing Crohn's Disease section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: Refractory GERD is defined as persistent heartburn and/or regurgitation despite {{c1::8 weeks of double-dose PPI b.i.d.}} therapy.
The 2023 AHA Science Advisory {{c1::does not change}} existing guidance recommending antibiotic prophylaxis before invasive dental procedures in {{c2::high-risk}} patients.
extra: Current Position section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: For children with {{c1::uncorrected congenital heart disease}}, AABB 2023 recommends an individualized transfusion threshold of {{c2::7–9 g/dL}} based on the specific lesion (conditional, low certainty).
On the fludrocortisone suppression test, a day-4 seated PAC >{{c1::6 ng/dL (170 pmol/L)}} at 10 AM with PRA <{{c2::1 ng/mL/h}} confirms primary aldosteronism.
Clinically significant portal hypertension (CSPH) is defined as a hepatic venous pressure gradient ≥ {{c1::10 mmHg}}; surrogates include ascites, varices, or platelet count <{{c2::100 × 10⁹/L}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — CSPH definition, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: The 2018 USPSTF recommends that PSA-based screening in men aged {{c1::55–69}} years be {{c1::individualized after informed discussion of benefits and harms}} (Grade C).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade C for men 55–69 y

text: The key change from the 2012 USPSTF cervical cancer screening recommendation was the addition of {{c1::primary hrHPV testing alone every 5 years}} as an acceptable strategy for women aged 30–65 years.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation section

text: Preferred initial ART regimens for most people with HIV are {{c1::bictegravir/TAF/FTC}} or {{c2::dolutegravir plus TXF/XTC}}, both InSTI-based.
Fondaparinux (2.5 mg SC daily) is contraindicated if CrCl <{{c1::30 mL/min}} and must {{c2::not be used as the sole anticoagulant during PCI}}.
Among all reviewed anti-obesity agents, AGA 2022 suggests {{c1::semaglutide 2.4 mg SQ weekly}} may be prioritized for most patients due to the greatest magnitude of net benefit.
extra: Table 1, Recommendation 2; Table 4 (TBWL MD vs placebo: 10.76%).

text: When ABI is {{c1::>1.40}} (noncompressible), a {{c2::toe-brachial index (TBI)}} should be obtained instead; TBI ≤0.70 is considered abnormal.
extra: Section 3.1 (Resting ABI and Physiological Testing) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: After treating chlamydia in pregnancy, a test of cure by NAAT should be performed at approximately {{c1::4 weeks}} after treatment.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Chlamydial Infections, Pregnant Women section

text: In CLTI, wound healing potential is predicted by TcPO2 {{c1::>30 mm Hg}} or SPP {{c2::>40 mm Hg}}.
extra: Section 3.1 (Resting ABI and Physiological Testing) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: Ambulatory cancer patients starting chemotherapy with a Khorana score {{c1::≥2}} should be offered thromboprophylaxis; greatest benefit is seen at score {{c2::≥3}}.
In PAD patients not at increased bleeding risk, {{c1::rivaroxaban 2.5 mg twice daily}} plus {{c2::aspirin 81 mg daily}} reduces both MACE and MALE (COMPASS/VOYAGER PAD data).
extra: Section 5.1 (Antiplatelet and Antithrombotic Therapy) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: The ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR guideline contains a {{c1::strong recommendation against}} the use of {{c2::TNFα blocking agents}} for PMR treatment.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 8

text: For dabigatran-associated ICH, specific reversal is achieved with {{c1::idarucizumab}} {{c1::5 g IV (two 2.5-g boluses)}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1 and Figure 2

text: For primary prevention in adults with ≥10% 10-year ASCVD risk, the LDL-C target is <{{c1::70 mg/dL}} and non–HDL-C target is <{{c2::100 mg/dL}}, using a {{c3::high-intensity statin}}.
{{c1::Serology}} must NOT be used for post-treatment assessment of H. pylori eradication.
extra: Key Concept: Post-Treatment Testing for Cure (ACG 2024 H. pylori Guideline)

text: In patients with mechanical prosthetic valves, {{c1::NOACs}} are contraindicated; lifelong {{c1::VKA (warfarin)}} anticoagulation is required.
In HHS, serum osmolality should be corrected at a rate of {{c1::3.0–8.0 mOsm/kg/h}} to avoid {{c2::osmotic demyelination syndrome}}.
Both US and European guidelines recommend {{c1::against}} vaptan use in acute or severely symptomatic hyponatremia.
After valve replacement for Candida endocarditis, antifungal therapy should continue for at least {{c1::6 weeks}}; longer if {{c2::perivalvular abscess}} is present.

First-line management of vasovagal syncope consists of {{c1::education, reassurance, and nonpharmacological measures}} (increased fluid/salt intake and physical counterpressure maneuvers); pharmacotherapy is reserved for refractory cases.
The most commonly used aldosterone-to-renin activity ratio (ARR) cutoff for primary aldosteronism screening is {{c1::30}}.
TENS is {{c1::strongly recommended against}} for {{c2::knee and/or hip}} OA in the 2019 ACR/AF guideline.
extra: Table 1 — Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body recommendations

text: Edoxaban is {{c1::contraindicated}} in AF patients with CrCl {{c2::>95 mL/min}} due to reduced efficacy at high renal clearance.
Lung-protective ventilation in ARDS targets a tidal volume of {{c1::4–8 mL/kg}} {{c1::predicted body weight (PBW)}} to reduce mortality.
extra: Recommendation 5.1, Domain 5: Low Tidal Volume — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: For RA patients with NTM lung disease requiring escalation beyond csDMARD monotherapy, {{c1::abatacept}} is conditionally preferred over other bDMARDs or tsDMARDs, and {{c2::TNF inhibitors}} should be avoided.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific Populations); conditional recommendation

text: The blood glucose target during DKA treatment is {{c1::150–200 mg/dL}} until resolution criteria are met.

For persistent febrile neutropenia, empiric antifungal coverage targets: Candida → {{c1::echinocandin}} (or {{c2::lipid amphotericin B}} for C. parapsilosis); Aspergillus → {{c2::voriconazole}}; Mucor/Rhizopus → {{c2::lipid amphotericin B}}.
extra: Section XXIV, Recommendation 69; Table 6 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text: HPV vaccination is routinely recommended starting at age {{c1::11–12 years}} (may begin at age 9) and through age {{c2::26 years}}; shared decision-making applies for adults aged {{c2::27–45 years}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Human Papillomavirus, Vaccination section

text: USPSTF 2022: Adults aged {{c1::≥60}} years should {{c1::not initiate}} aspirin for primary prevention of CVD (Grade {{c1::D}}).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade D; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: Metformin in CKD: initiate if eGFR ≥{{c1::30}} ml/min/1.73 m²; halve the dose when eGFR {{c1::30–44}}; do not initiate or stop if eGFR <{{c1::30}} ml/min/1.73 m².
In HIT patients with liver dysfunction, heart failure, anasarca, or post-cardiac surgery, argatroban should be reduced to {{c1::0.5–1.2 mcg/kg/min}}.
Roflumilast is considered for patients with persistent exacerbations and eosinophils < 100 cells/µL who have {{c1::FEV1 < 50% predicted}} and {{c2::chronic bronchitis}}.
Per 2020 AGA guidelines, the preferred ferritin cutoff to diagnose iron deficiency in anemic patients is {{c1::45 ng/mL}}, replacing the older threshold of {{c2::15 ng/mL}}.
extra: Recommendation 1 / Ferritin Cutoff section; Table 3 (Executive Summary)

text: Do not start insulin if serum K⁺ is {{c1::<3.5 mmol/L}} until repleted; give {{c2::10–20 mmol}} K⁺ per liter IV fluid if K⁺ is below this threshold, targeting K⁺ of {{c3::4–5 mmol/L}}.
extra: Figure 4 — Treatment pathways; Table 3; Thresholds & Doses

text: Antihypertensives contraindicated in pregnancy include {{c1::atenolol}}, {{c1::ACEi}}, {{c1::ARBs}}, {{c1::direct renin inhibitors}}, {{c1::nitroprusside}}, and {{c1::MRA}}.
extra: Section 5.5 (Hypertension and Pregnancy) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: On-demand ({{c1::2-1-1}}) TDF/FTC PrEP is recommended specifically for {{c2::cisgender men}} with planned receptive anal sex.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 6; Table 4 (Biomedical HIV Prevention by Exposure Type)

text: An absolute contraindication to fibrinolytic therapy in STEMI is SBP >{{c1::180 mmHg}} or DBP >{{c1::110 mmHg}} (severe uncontrolled hypertension).
In cirrhosis with ascites, avoid {{c1::NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, α1-adrenergic blockers, and aminoglycosides}} due to risk of AKI and diuretic refractoriness.
High-dose echinocandin regimens for Candida endocarditis: caspofungin {{c1::150 mg}} daily, micafungin {{c1::150 mg}} daily, or anidulafungin {{c2::200 mg}} daily.
A 10-day (rather than 14-day) course of BQT reduces H. pylori eradication efficacy to approximately {{c1::77%}}.
For a troponin assay to be valid, the coefficient of variation at the 99th percentile should be {{c1::≤10%}}.
ATA 2015 recommends considering repeat FNA for a benign thyroid nodule if there is a {{c1::≥20% increase in two nodule dimensions with a minimum 2 mm increase}}, or a {{c2::>50% increase in volume}}.
On the saline infusion test, a post-infusion PAC <{{c1::5 ng/dL}} excludes PA, while a PAC >{{c1::10 ng/dL}} confirms PA; values of 5–10 ng/dL are indeterminate.
In older adults, the GDS-15 cutoff of {{c1::≥5}} has a sensitivity of {{c2::0.94}} and specificity of {{c2::0.81}} for MDD.
extra: Supporting Evidence: Accuracy of Screening Tests — mean age 69–85 years, USPSTF 2023

text: Empirical first-line antibiotics for community-acquired SBP are {{c1::IV ceftriaxone or cefotaxime}}.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Table 9: Antibiotics for Infections in Cirrhosis

text: Methotrexate for induction of remission in CD is dosed at {{c1::25 mg}} SC/IM once weekly, and may be reduced to {{c2::15 mg/week}} after 4 months of steroid-free remission.
The TSH target for primary hypothyroidism per ATA 2014 is {{c1::0.4–4.0 mIU/L}} (laboratory reference range).
extra: ATA 2014 Hypothyroidism Guideline, Recommendation 1b; Thresholds & Doses.

text: At Step 5 severe asthma, biologic options include: anti-IgE {{c1::omalizumab}} (≥6 yr), anti-IL-5/5Rα (mepolizumab ≥6 yr, benralizumab or {{c2::depemokimab}} ≥12 yr), anti-IL-4Rα dupilumab (≥6 yr), and anti-TSLP tezepelumab (≥12 yr).
For adults with CAC ≥{{c1::1000 AU}}, the LDL-C target is <{{c2::55 mg/dL}} and non–HDL-C target is <{{c3::85 mg/dL}}.
Albuminuria category A2 corresponds to ACR {{c1::30–300 mg/g}}, while A3 corresponds to ACR {{c1::>300 mg/g}} — the thresholds that trigger RASi recommendations in CKD.
Systemic thrombolytics are reserved for acute PE with hypotension defined as systolic BP {{c1::<90 mmHg}} for {{c1::≥15 minutes}} with acceptable bleeding risk.
extra: Guidance Statement 9; Thresholds & Doses; Table 5, p. e562

text: Patients with IPF should be evaluated every {{c1::3–6 months}} for disease progression and referred for {{c2::lung transplant}} evaluation at diagnosis if at increased mortality risk.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of IPF; IPF follow-up section

text: DKA resolution requires venous pH {{c1::>7.3}} or bicarbonate {{c1::>18 mmol/L}} AND ketones {{c2::<0.6 mmol/L}}.
KDIGO 2024 recommends protein intake of {{c1::0.8 g/kg/day}} in CKD G3–G5 adults and advises against intake >{{c2::1.3 g/kg/day}}.
KDIGO 2024 recommends statin therapy (1A) for all adults ≥{{c1::50 years}} with eGFR <{{c2::60 ml/min/1.73 m²}}.
Discontinue ACEi/ARB if serum creatinine rises {{c1::>30%}} within {{c2::4 weeks}} of initiation; continue these agents even if eGFR falls below 30 ml/min/1.73 m².
For severe EGPA induction, {{c1::cyclophosphamide or rituximab}} is preferred over mepolizumab.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 3, EGPA Rec 2

text: GINA 2026 Track 1 (preferred) uses {{c1::low-dose ICS-formoterol}} as the anti-inflammatory reliever (AIR) at all steps, rather than a SABA reliever.
extra: Box 4-3 (p.80) — Asthma treatment tracks for adults and adolescents

text: In newly diagnosed GCA with {{c1::threatened vision loss}}, initiate {{c2::IV pulse glucocorticoids}} over high-dose oral glucocorticoids.
In adults with CKD G1–G4 and severely increased albuminuria ({{c1::A3}}) without diabetes, {{c2::ACEi or ARB}} is recommended as first-line antihypertensive therapy (Level 1B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.2.1; key trials REIN, AIPRI

text: For non-IPF PPF, {{c1::pirfenidone}} has {{c2::insufficient evidence}} for a graded recommendation; further research is recommended.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Treatment of PPF — Pirfenidone section (RELIEF trial; uILD trial)

text: For new-onset AF after cardiac surgery that persists or recurs, anticoagulation for a minimum of {{c1::60 days}} is recommended, with follow-up rhythm assessment before discontinuation.
According to ACG 2022, {{c1::PPIs}} are the first-line pharmacologic therapy for GERD, with all standard-dose agents having similar efficacy for healing erosive esophagitis.
extra: GERD Medical Management section (PPIs subsection); Table 1

text: In the 2019 ACR/AF OA guideline, exercise is {{c1::strongly}} recommended for all patients with knee, hip, and/or hand OA.
extra: Table 1 — Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body recommendations

text: First-line treatment for grade 2 ascites is {{c1::moderate sodium restriction (90 mmol/day)}} plus {{c2::spironolactone ± furosemide}}.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: Ascites Management (Grade 2/3, Sodium Restriction, Diuretics)

text: For a first episode of childhood nephrotic syndrome, oral glucocorticoid induction is given for {{c1::8 weeks (4 weeks daily + 4 weeks alternate-day)}} or 12 weeks (6+6).
In patients with diabetes and multivessel CAD, {{c1::CABG}} is preferred over PCI; PCI may be considered only if the patient is {{c2::a poor surgical candidate}} (FREEDOM trial).
extra: Section 8.2 — FREEDOM trial; CABG preferred in diabetes with multivessel CAD

text: Very high-dose GC is defined as prednisone ≥{{c1::30}} mg/day AND cumulative dose ≥{{c1::5 gm}}; adults ≥30 years on this regimen are treated as moderate-to-high fracture risk.
extra: Figure 2; Thresholds & Doses — Very high-dose GC definition

text: The recommended adult dexamethasone dose for initial ITP therapy is {{c1::40 mg/day × 4 days}}.

At PAH diagnosis, a {{c1::three}}-strata risk model is used; at follow-up, a {{c2::four}}-strata model (low/intermediate-low/intermediate-high/high) based on WHO-FC, 6MWD, and BNP/NT-proBNP is recommended.
extra: Table 16 (Three-strata risk model, p. 3657); Table 18 (Four-strata follow-up risk model, p. 3659) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: In HFrEF/HFpEF with iron deficiency, {{c1::intravenous ferric carboxymaltose}} improves exercise capacity and reduces HF hospitalizations; {{c2::oral iron}} is inadequate.
CRT is COR 1 for HFrEF with LVEF ≤35%, {{c1::LBBB}} morphology with QRS ≥{{c2::150 ms}}, and NYHA class II–ambulatory IV on GDMT.
S1P modulators approved for moderately to severely active UC: etrasimod {{c1::2 mg/day}} orally and ozanimod {{c2::0.92 mg/day}} orally.

AKI Stage 1 = creatinine rise {{c1::≥0.3 mg/dL within 48 h or up to 2× baseline}}; Stage 2 = {{c2::2–3× baseline}}; Stage 3 = {{c3::>3× baseline, or creatinine >4 mg/dL with acute rise ≥0.3 mg/dL, or initiation of RRT}}.
In anti-GBM GN, plasma exchange is performed at {{c1::40–50 ml/kg/d}} against 5% albumin until antibodies are undetectable (~{{c2::14 days}}).
extra: Thresholds & Doses — Anti-GBM plasma exchange (Chapter 11, S86–S87)

text: The aerobic exercise prescription for hypertension management is {{c1::90–150 minutes/week}} at {{c2::65–75% heart rate reserve}}.

All eligible ACS patients should be referred to {{c1::cardiac rehabilitation}} at hospital discharge; {{c2::home-based programs}} are a reasonable alternative when in-person attendance isn't possible.
The bacterial co-infection rate in hospitalized adults with SARS-CoV-2 pneumonia is approximately {{c1::3.5%}}, compared to {{c2::~30%}} among intubated patients with influenza.
extra: Question 2 (Empiric Antibiotics with Positive Virus Test) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; Thresholds & Doses.

text: Tafamidis is considered {{c1::not cost-effective}} at its current price of approximately {{c2::$225,000/year}}, with an ICER >$180,000/QALY.
In the ADA evidence-grading system, Grade {{c1::E}} represents {{c1::expert consensus}} in the absence of adequate clinical evidence.

For patients with atrial fibrillation after ICH, optimal timing for anticoagulation resumption is approximately {{c1::7–8 weeks}} post-ICH; resumption {{c2::earlier}} is associated with significantly increased bleeding risk.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 9.1.3 (Management of Antithrombotic Agents)

text: Warfarin remains first-line anticoagulation for AF patients with {{c1::moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis}} or {{c2::mechanical heart valves}}.
The exercise prescription for AF risk factor modification targets {{c1::moderate-to-vigorous aerobic exercise}} for {{c2::210 minutes/week}}.
In treatment-naive active PsA, a {{c1::TNFi biologic}} is conditionally recommended over an OSM as first-line therapy.
extra: Table 1 (PICO 10a–e) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendation, low-quality evidence.

text: KDIGO 2024 KFRE-based thresholds: ≥{{c1::3–5%}} at 5 years → nephrology referral; >{{c2::10%}} at 2 years → multidisciplinary CKD care; >{{c3::40%}} at 2 years → KRT preparation/vascular access planning.
Recommended direct-visualization CRC screening intervals: colonoscopy every {{c1::10 years}}, CT colonography every {{c2::5 years}}, and flexible sigmoidoscopy alone every {{c2::5 years}}.
extra: Table 1: Characteristics of Recommended Colorectal Cancer Screening Strategies

text: In syncope patients with LVEF {{c1::≤35%}}, ICD implantation per primary prevention guidelines is appropriate without requiring {{c2::EPS}} first.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Key Recommendations; Thresholds & Doses

text: Clinical stability criteria for antibiotic shortening in CAP include: temp {{c1::<37.8°C}}, HR <100 bpm, RR <24 breaths/min, SpO₂ >90% on room air, sBP >90 mmHg, and {{c2::normal mental status}} — all criteria must be met.
extra: Question 3 (Antibiotic Duration) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; Table 6.

text: A key 2019 change from the 2012 ACR guideline: glucosamine was upgraded from conditionally recommended against to {{c1::strongly recommended against}} for knee, hip, and/or hand OA.
Initial management of AKI in cirrhosis includes stopping {{c1::diuretics}}, withdrawing {{c2::nephrotoxins (especially NSAIDs)}}, and giving albumin {{c3::1 g/kg/day for 2 days}}.
In the English case-crossover study (Thornhill 2022), CIED implantation was associated with an IE odds ratio of {{c1::1.54}} (95% CI 1.27–1.85) using the step model.
extra: Table 3 / Thornhill 2022 section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: In PsA with frequent serious infections, {{c1::an OSM}} should be used over a {{c2::TNFi}} (strong recommendation, black box warning).
extra: Table 6 (PICO 64) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; strong recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.

text: AAN 2018 recommends offering DMT to patients with a single clinical demyelinating event (CIS) and {{c1::≥2 brain lesions characteristic of MS}} who choose therapy after discussing risks and benefits.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level B, Starting Rec 7 / Statement 7b

text: The ACP 2023 osteoporosis guideline is classified as a {{c1::living guideline}}, meaning its recommendations are subject to update as new evidence emerges.
For patients at intermediate HIT risk (0.1%–1.0%), platelet count should be monitored every {{c1::2–3 days}} from day {{c2::4}} to day {{c2::14}} (or until heparin is stopped).
For beta-blocker or CCB overdose causing bradycardia, glucagon is given as {{c1::3–10 mg IV bolus}} followed by an infusion of {{c2::3–5 mg/h}}.
ICD is indicated post-MI for: LVEF ≤{{c1::30%}} (NYHA I-III), LVEF {{c1::31-35%}} (NYHA II-III), or LVEF ≤40% with {{c2::inducible VT}}; all require ≥40 days post-MI and expected survival ≥1 year.
When anaphylaxis coexists with acute asthma, administer {{c1::epinephrine (adrenaline)}} first, then bronchodilators.
When pharmacological VTE prophylaxis is contraindicated due to unacceptable bleeding risk, ASH 2018 recommends {{c1::mechanical prophylaxis}} over no prophylaxis (conditional recommendation).
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Summary of Recommendations (acutely/critically ill patients)

text: Pacemaker implantation for VVS should be reserved for patients with {{c1::documented asystole during spontaneous clinical syncope}} and minimal {{c2::vasodepressor response}}.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Section 5.2 (Pacemakers in VVS)

text: For acute decompensated HF, IV loop diuretic should be initiated at ≥{{c1::2×}} the patient's home oral daily dose (mg-to-mg conversion).
extra: Section 9.3 / Table 12 — IV loop diuretic strategy

text: In ACS, the aspirin loading dose is {{c1::162-325 mg}}, followed by a maintenance dose of {{c2::75-100 mg daily}}.
A FIB-4 {{c1::>3.48}} combined with VCTE LSM {{c1::≥20 kPa}} achieves a specificity of {{c2::90%}} for cirrhosis using the sequential NIT approach.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Elastography subsection (NITs section)

text: NRT in pregnant persons showed a cessation rate of 11.9% vs 10.1% control — {{c1::not statistically significant}} (RR {{c1::1.11}} [95% CI 0.79–1.56]; 5 trials, n=2,033).
extra: Practice Considerations: Suggestions for I Statements (Pharmacotherapy in Pregnancy) — USPSTF 2021

text: KDIGO 2024 states that {{c1::race}} should NOT be incorporated into eGFR equation computations.
IV methylprednisolone for ASUC: doses above {{c1::60 mg/day}} provide no additional benefit; no difference between single dose, divided doses, or continuous infusion.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; ASUC Management section

text: In ACS complicated by cardiogenic shock with multivessel CAD, {{c1::culprit-vessel-only PCI}} is recommended; routine multivessel PCI at the index procedure is {{c1::not recommended}}.
The 2021 USPSTF lung cancer screening recommendation carries a Grade {{c1::B}} rating, reflecting {{c2::moderate}} certainty of {{c2::moderate}} net benefit.
Intracranial hemorrhage incidence in ITP is approximately {{c1::1.4%}} in adults and {{c2::0.1%–0.4%}} in children.
After CABG, perioperative blood glucose should be maintained at {{c1::<180 mg/dL}} using {{c2::continuous IV insulin infusion}}.
extra: Section 13.1 / Table 13 — Sternal wound infection prevention; perioperative glucose control

text: In clinical trials for PMR, methotrexate was used at {{c1::7.5–10 mg orally per week}}.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 7

text: In PsA, {{c1::biologic monotherapy}} is preferred over biologic + MTX combination therapy; combination may be considered when the TNFi used is {{c2::infliximab}} or {{c2::adalimumab}} (to reduce immunogenicity).
Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics for necrotizing fasciitis consist of {{c1::vancomycin or linezolid}} plus {{c2::piperacillin-tazobactam or a carbapenem}} ± metronidazole.
extra: Section VIII, Recommendations 27–29; Table 4 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text:
Dabigatran dosing in AF by CrCl:
CrCl >30 mL/min → {{c1::150 mg twice daily}}
CrCl 15–30 mL/min → {{c1::75 mg twice daily}}
CrCl <15 mL/min → {{c2::contraindicated}}

Surveillance colonoscopy for colorectal cancer in CD is recommended after ≥{{c1::8 years}} of disease with ≥{{c2::30%}} colonic involvement; PSC patients should begin surveillance at {{c3::diagnosis}} regardless of disease extent.
extra: Endoscopy / Surveillance section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: Low-risk classification by EDACS requires: EDACS score ≤{{c1::16}} AND negative serial hs-cTn AND {{c2::no ischemic ECG changes}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Table 6 (Clinical Decision Pathways)

text: The European guideline recommends {{c1::urea or loop diuretics plus oral NaCl}} as preferred second-line agents for SIAD, and recommends against {{c2::lithium and demeclocycline}}.
extra: Treatment of Chronic Hyponatremia section; Table 2

text: The 2026 ADA Standards of Care is based on a systematic literature search (PubMed, Medline, EMBASE) covering {{c1::1 June 2024}} to {{c2::18 July 2025}}.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Grading of Evidence section

text: For inpatient VTE prophylaxis in acutely ill medical patients, ASH 2018 recommends {{c1::LMWH}} over {{c2::DOACs}} (strong recommendation).
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 11–12 (DOACs in acutely ill medical patients)

text: Initial evaluation of all syncope patients must include a thorough {{c1::history}}, {{c1::physical examination}}, and {{c1::12-lead ECG}}.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Key Recommendations; Table 4

text: In hemodynamically stable STEMI patients with multivessel CAD, {{c1::complete revascularization (multivessel PCI)}} is recommended, preferring {{c2::single-procedure}} multivessel PCI over staged PCI.
extra: Section 7.4.1 — 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline; BIOVASC and MULTISTARS AMI trials

text: {{c1::Intracoronary imaging (IVUS or OCT)}} is recommended to guide PCI for {{c2::complex coronary lesions}} in ACS.
In gout patients with a history of CVD or a new CV event taking febuxostat, ACR 2020 conditionally recommends {{c1::switching to an alternative ULT}} if available.

Track 1 AIR for adults/adolescents at Steps 1–2: {{c1::budesonide-formoterol 160/4.5 mcg delivered}} (200/6 mcg metered), 1 inhalation as needed; seek medical care if >{{c2::12}} total inhalations in 24 hours.
VT/VF storm is defined as {{c1::≥3 episodes}} of sustained VT, VF, or appropriate ICD shocks within {{c2::24 hours}}.
Spironolactone/eplerenone target dose is {{c1::50 mg/day}}; reduce dose by half if eGFR is {{c2::31–49}} mL/min/1.73 m².

Triptans and ergot derivatives should be avoided in patients with {{c1::coronary artery disease}}, {{c1::peripheral vascular disease}}, or {{c1::uncontrolled hypertension}} due to vasoconstriction; gepants and ditans {{c2::do not cause vasoconstriction}} and may be used instead.
In active lupus nephritis, {{c1::belimumab}} is recommended as an {{c2::add-on}} immunosuppressive agent (not monotherapy), based on FDA and EMA approval.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Guideline Overview; Key Recommendation

text: For severe symptomatic ICAS (70–99%), aggressive medical management with {{c1::aspirin 325 mg + clopidogrel for 90 days}} followed by aspirin alone is preferred over {{c2::PTAS}} as first-line therapy (SAMMPRIS trial).
After an amiodarone IV loading bolus, the maintenance infusion is {{c1::1 mg/min × 6 h}}, then {{c1::0.5 mg/min × 18 h}}.
Trichomoniasis in women is treated with {{c1::metronidazole 500 mg orally BID × 7 days}}, which is superior to the single 2-g dose.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Trichomoniasis, Treatment section

text: Lymphogranuloma venereum (LGV) is treated with {{c1::doxycycline 100 mg orally BID × 21 days}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — LGV, Treatment section

text: After each dose of lasmiditan, patients must not drive for at least {{c1::8 hours}}; lasmiditan is classified as a {{c2::Schedule V}} controlled substance.
extra: Key Recommendations — lasmiditan safety; Thresholds & Doses — lasmiditan approved doses

text: In sepsis, insulin therapy should be initiated when blood glucose is ≥{{c1::180 mg/dL}}, with a target glucose range of {{c1::144–180 mg/dL}}.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Glucose Control section (network meta-analysis of 35 RCTs)

text: High-dose insulin for beta-blocker or CCB overdose is dosed as {{c1::1 unit/kg IV bolus}} followed by {{c2::0.5 units/kg/h}} infusion.

AVS may be omitted in PA patients who are <{{c1::35}} years old with spontaneous hypokalemia, marked aldosterone excess, and CT findings {{c2::unequivocally consistent with a unilateral cortical adenoma}}.
When a stimulation test is not feasible, a morning cortisol {{c1::<140 nmol/L (5 mcg/dL)}} combined with ACTH {{c2::>2× the upper limit of normal}} is highly suggestive of PAI.
extra: Rec. 2.3 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: Colonoscopy with biopsy or polypectomy in high-risk patients is associated with an excess of {{c1::7.3}} IE cases per {{c2::10,000}} procedures compared with no colonoscopy (Garcia-Albeniz 2016).
extra: Table 2 / Garcia-Albeniz 2016 section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: The recommended corticosteroid dose for qualifying septic shock is {{c1::hydrocortisone 200 mg/day IV}}.
On bidirectional endoscopy in postmenopausal women/men with IDA, pooled data show lower GI malignancy in {{c1::8.9%}} and upper GI malignancy in {{c2::2.0%}} of patients.
extra: Recommendation 2 / Postmenopausal Women and Men section (pooled, 18 studies)

text: The 2026 guideline recommends measuring Lp(a) {{c1::at least once}} in {{c2::all adults}} for ASCVD risk assessment.

Serial {{c1::procalcitonin (PCT)}} measurements are suggested in adult {{c2::ICU}} patients with suspected infection to decrease antibiotic use, associated with ~2–4 more antibiotic-free days without increased mortality.
extra: Recommendation 19 (Section XVIII) — weak recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.

text: Dietary sodium restriction for all patients with glomerular disease: <{{c1::2.0 g/d}} (<90 mmol/d).
{{c1::Rifabutin}} may substitute for rifampin in LTBI treatment when rifampin is contraindicated due to {{c2::drug-drug interactions}} and isoniazid cannot be used.
TCAs (e.g., amitriptyline or desipramine) improve global IBS symptoms with an NNT of {{c1::~4.5}}; therapy is initiated at {{c2::10 mg daily}} and titrated upward.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Neuromodulator section; Table 2

text: The target dose of sacubitril-valsartan is {{c1::97/103 mg twice daily}}; when switching from an ACEi, wait at least {{c2::36 hours}} before initiating.
The full LT4 replacement dose for patients with minimal endogenous thyroid function is {{c1::1.6–1.8 μg/kg}} actual body weight per day.
In pregnant women, untreated ASB carries a {{c1::20–35%}} risk of pyelonephritis, which is reduced to {{c2::1–4%}} with antimicrobial treatment.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section III; Thresholds & Doses

text: In ALI, skeletal muscle tolerates ischemia for approximately {{c1::4–6 hours}}, defining the urgency of revascularization.
extra: Section 11.2.3 (Anticoagulation for ALI) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: For adults with hematologic or oncologic disorders, AABB 2023 recommends considering RBC transfusion at <{{c1::7 g/dL}} ({{c2::conditional}} recommendation, low certainty).
Amiodarone-induced thyrotoxicosis type 1 is treated with {{c1::MMI 40 mg/day}}; type 2 is treated with {{c2::prednisone 40 mg/day}} for 2–4 weeks then tapered.
During HCC surveillance, a new or enlarging solid liver lesion ≥ {{c1::1 cm}} on ultrasound triggers diagnostic workup with multiphase CT or contrast-enhanced MRI.
Severe mitral stenosis is defined by a mitral valve area {{c1::≤1.5 cm²}} and diastolic pressure half-time {{c2::≥150 ms}}.
4R (rifampin daily × 4 months) is a strongly recommended preferred LTBI regimen for {{c1::HIV-negative}} adults and children; there is {{c2::no evidence}} supporting its use in HIV-positive persons.
extra: Table 3; Preferred Regimens: Four Months of Daily Rifampin

text: Epinephrine injection should {{c1::never be used as monotherapy}} for endoscopic hemostasis; it must always be combined with {{c2::a second hemostatic modality}}.
extra: Choice of Endoscopic Hemostatic Therapy section, Table 7 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: In the 3HP regimen for children aged 2–11 years, the isoniazid dose is {{c1::25 mg/kg}} (max 900 mg) once weekly (compared to {{c2::15 mg/kg}} for adults).
Once started, an SGLT2i in T2D + CKD should be {{c1::continued even if eGFR subsequently falls below 20 ml/min/1.73 m²}}, until {{c2::dialysis or transplant}} is initiated.
SGLT2 inhibitors should be {{c1::held on admission}} for any hyperglycemic crisis; they should {{c2::not}} be routinely resumed after DKA resolution in T2D and are {{c2::not}} used in T1D.

Pregnant patients with syphilis and penicillin allergy must undergo {{c1::penicillin desensitization}} and be treated with penicillin G, because no proven alternatives exist for preventing {{c2::congenital syphilis}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Syphilis, Pregnant Women section

text: For unprovoked VTE or VTE provoked by a persistent risk factor, extended-phase anticoagulation with a {{c1::DOAC}} should be offered.
extra: Guidance Statement 20; Tables 17 and Figure 6, pp. e584–e586

text: When ICI-based combinations are contraindicated (e.g., post-transplant patients, severe autoimmune disease), first-line systemic therapy for advanced HCC is {{c1::sorafenib}} or {{c1::lenvatinib}}.
extra: Systemic Therapy / First-line section, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: In borderline- or intermediate-risk adults, a CAC score of {{c1::≥100 AU or ≥75th age/sex/race percentile}} favors statin initiation.
For the treatment phase of VTE (first 3 months), {{c1::apixaban, dabigatran, edoxaban, or rivaroxaban}} are strongly recommended over VKA.
extra: Guidance Statement 14; Summary of Recommendations, p. e547

text: The recommended dose of dapagliflozin for HF is {{c1::10 mg once daily}}, and empagliflozin is {{c1::10 mg once daily}}; both are excluded if eGFR <{{c2::20–30}} mL/min/1.73 m².
Topical NSAIDs are {{c1::strongly}} recommended for knee OA and {{c2::conditionally}} recommended for hand OA, and should be considered before oral NSAIDs.
