By liver stiffness measurement (TE), cACLD is ruled out by LSM < {{c1::10 kPa}} and ruled in by LSM ≥ {{c2::15 kPa}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3 (Rule of Five)

text: EVT may be considered (COR 2b) for pediatric patients aged {{c1::28 days–6 years}} with LVO within {{c2::24 hours}}.
{{c1::MRE}} is preferred over CTE for small bowel imaging in CD patients {{c2::under 35 years}} or those requiring serial examinations, due to absence of radiation exposure.
extra: Table 1 (GRADE Recommendations Summary); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: KDIGO 2021 recommends avoiding {{c1::dual RAS blockade}} (ACEi + ARB, ACEi + DRI, or ARB + DRI) in all CKD patients, with or without diabetes (Level 1B).
extra: Chapter 3, Recommendation 3.3.1; evidence from ONTARGET, VA-NEPHRON-D, ALTITUDE

text: Orthostatic hypotension is defined as a drop in systolic BP {{c1::≥20 mm Hg}} or diastolic BP {{c1::≥10 mm Hg}} upon standing.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Table 3; Thresholds & Doses

text: Adults ≥40 years at moderate-to-high fracture risk on long-term GCs: preferred first-line pharmacologic therapy is {{c1::oral bisphosphonate}}, over IV bisphosphonate, teriparatide, denosumab, or raloxifene.
ASH 2018 recommends {{c1::against}} routine VTE prophylaxis in chronically ill patients and nursing home residents ({{c2::conditional}} recommendation).
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendation 15 (Chronically ill/nursing home patients)

text: Idarucizumab for urgent dabigatran reversal is given as {{c1::5 g IV}} (two 2.5 g vials) over {{c2::5 minutes}}.
Ustekinumab induction is weight-based IV (~{{c1::6 mg/kg}}); maintenance is {{c2::90 mg SC every 8 weeks}} (superior remission rates vs every 12 weeks).
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; UNIFI trial

text: EVT for basilar artery occlusion (COR 1) is recommended within {{c1::24 hours}} when NIHSS ≥{{c2::10}} and PC-ASPECTS ≥{{c3::6}} with prestroke mRS 0–1.
At maximum phentermine-topiramate ER dose (15 mg/92 mg), discontinue if less than {{c1::5% TBWL}} is achieved after {{c2::12 additional weeks}}; taper the dose to reduce {{c3::seizure}} risk.
extra: Recommendation 4 / Special Clinical Considerations; Thresholds & Doses.

text: In pregnant persons, {{c1::health education alone}} (without counseling) is {{c1::not effective}} for smoking cessation.
extra: Practice Considerations: Interventions for Pregnant Persons — USPSTF 2021

text: In symptomatic children, a nonbiopsy diagnosis of celiac disease is acceptable if TTG-IgA is {{c1::>10× ULN}} confirmed by {{c2::positive EMA on a second blood sample}}, with family agreement; HLA testing is {{c3::not required}}.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Diagnosis section (Evidence and rationale); Figure 1

text: Long-acting cabotegravir PrEP is given as {{c1::600 mg (3 mL) IM gluteal}} injection; the first 2 injections are {{c2::4 weeks apart}}, then every 8 weeks thereafter.

Patients with antiphospholipid syndrome and stroke should receive {{c1::warfarin (INR 2–3)}}, not DOACs; {{c2::rivaroxaban}} is specifically associated with higher thrombotic risk in triple-positive antiphospholipid antibody patients.
ASH 2018 recommends {{c1::against}} extending pharmacological VTE prophylaxis beyond hospital discharge in acutely or critically ill medical patients ({{c2::strong}} recommendation).
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 13–14 (Extended-duration outpatient prophylaxis)

text: The 2018 USPSTF upgraded its recommendation for PSA screening in men 55–69 years from Grade {{c1::D}} (2012) to Grade {{c2::C}} (2018), based on longer-term {{c3::ERSPC}} follow-up data.
Argatroban is initiated at {{c1::2 mcg/kg/min}} for patients with normal organ function, targeting an aPTT of {{c2::1.5–3.0×}} baseline.
The USPSTF 2021 recommends {{c1::behavioral counseling AND FDA-approved pharmacotherapy}} for nonpregnant adults who smoke (Grade {{c1::A}}).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: Enoxaparin standard dose is 1 mg/kg SC q12h; if CrCl <30 mL/min, reduce to {{c1::1 mg/kg SC once daily}}.
In all men with confirmed hypogonadism, {{c1::LH and FSH}} should be measured to distinguish primary (testicular) from secondary (hypothalamic-pituitary) hypogonadism.
Risankizumab induction: {{c1::1,200 mg IV every 4 weeks × 3 doses}}; maintenance: {{c2::180 mg or 360 mg SC every 8 weeks}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC section

text: In adults with newly diagnosed ITP and platelet count {{c1::≥30×10⁹/L}} who are asymptomatic or have minor mucocutaneous bleeding, guidelines recommend {{c2::observation}} over corticosteroids (strong recommendation).
extra: ASH 2019 ITP Guideline — Recommendation 1b; Thresholds & Doses

text: In AAV, plasma exchange is considered if SCr >{{c1::5.7 mg/dl (500 µmol/l)}} requiring dialysis, or with {{c2::diffuse alveolar hemorrhage}} and hypoxemia.
In critically ill patients with AKI, the target plasma glucose is {{c1::110–149 mg/dl}} (6.1–8.3 mmol/l) (Grade 2C).
For cancer-associated thrombosis with luminal GI malignancy, prefer {{c1::apixaban or LMWH}} over edoxaban or rivaroxaban due to higher {{c2::GI bleeding}} risk with the latter agents.

ADA conflict-of-interest disclosure covers relationships from {{c1::1 year prior to committee appointment}} through {{c2::publication}}; co-chairs must remain unconflicted for 1 year after publication.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Funding Source / COI section

text: First-line vasoconstrictor for HRS-AKI is {{c1::terlipressin}} (preferred), plus albumin; {{c2::norepinephrine}} is used if terlipressin is unavailable.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: HRS-AKI Treatment; Table 11

text: The 2021 USPSTF CRC screening recommendation applies only to {{c1::average-risk}} adults; individuals with prior CRC, adenomatous polyps, IBD, {{c2::Lynch syndrome}}, or familial adenomatous polyposis require individualized management.
extra: Practice Considerations: Starting and Stopping Ages; Recommendation Summary Table

text: For women aged 30–65 years, USPSTF-recommended cervical cancer screening options include: cytology alone every {{c1::3}} years, hrHPV testing alone every {{c2::5}} years, or cotesting every {{c2::5}} years — all Grade A.
extra: Recommendation Summary Table; Table: Characteristics of Cervical Cancer Screening Tests

text: A fecal calprotectin level >{{c1::100 μg/g}} indicates postoperative endoscopic recurrence in CD (sensitivity ~89%).
extra: Monitoring Disease Activity section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: E-cigarettes (ENDS) are {{c1::not recommended}} as a tobacco-cessation method per the 2019 ACC/AHA Primary Prevention Guideline.
Colchicine for secondary prevention in CCD is dosed at {{c1::0.5 mg daily}} and should be avoided if eGFR is {{c2::<30 mL/min/m²}}.
HPA axis recovery after resection of an ACTH-producing tumor takes approximately {{c1::6–12 months}} in adults and a mean of {{c2::12.6 ± 3.3 months}} in pediatric series.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Section 5 / Thresholds & Doses

text: ACR 2020 conditionally recommends that ULT {{c1::may be initiated during an active gout flare}} rather than waiting for flare resolution.
For adults undergoing orthopedic surgery or with preexisting {{c1::cardiovascular disease}}, AABB 2023 accepts a restrictive transfusion threshold of {{c2::<8 g/dL}}.
extra: Recommendation 1, remark — AABB 2023 RBC Transfusion Guideline

text: ASPs should specifically target reduction of {{c1::clindamycin}}, {{c1::broad-spectrum cephalosporins}}, and {{c1::fluoroquinolones}} due to their high association with CDI risk.
extra: Recommendation 5 (Section V) — strong recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.

text: In septic shock, a {{c1::restrictive}} RBC transfusion strategy is recommended, with a hemoglobin threshold of <{{c2::70 g/L}} (TRISS trial).
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — RBC Transfusion Targets section

text: Empiric vancomycin dosing for HAP/VAP: {{c1::15 mg/kg}} IV q8–12h with a loading dose of {{c2::25–30 mg/kg}} ×1 for severe illness; target trough {{c3::15–20 mg/mL}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Table 3; Thresholds & Doses

text: Lung cancer screening should be discontinued when a person has not smoked for {{c1::15 years}} OR develops a health problem that substantially limits {{c2::life expectancy or ability/willingness to undergo curative lung surgery}}.
Each LDCT screening scan delivers an estimated {{c1::0.65–2.36 mSv}} of radiation, compared with an average US background exposure of {{c2::2.4 mSv/year}}.
extra: Harms of Screening and Treatment section

text: Severe-range hypertension in pregnancy is defined as SBP ≥{{c1::160}} mm Hg or DBP ≥{{c1::110}} mm Hg and must be treated within {{c2::30–60 minutes}}.
extra: Section 5.5 (Hypertension and Pregnancy) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: In ARDS complicating sepsis, use a tidal volume of {{c1::4–8 mL/kg PBW}} (target 6 mL/kg) and plateau pressure ≤{{c2::30 cmH₂O}}.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Protective Ventilation section

text: Bupropion SR achieves a cessation rate of {{c1::19.0%}} vs 11.0% placebo at ≥6 months (RR {{c1::1.64}} [95% CI 1.52–1.77]; 46 studies, n=17,866).

HBsAg-positive patients on immunosuppressive therapy should receive antiviral prophylaxis continued for ≥{{c1::6 months}} after therapy completion, or ≥{{c2::12 months}} following anti-CD20 (e.g., rituximab) therapy.
In acute pancreatitis with hypovolemia, give a bolus of {{c1::10 mL/kg}} of lactated Ringer's and then reassess; most patients benefit from {{c2::3–4 L}} in the first 24 hours.
In a low-pretest-probability patient with suspected AAS, a D-dimer <{{c1::500 ng/mL}} makes acute aortic syndrome unlikely.
For secondary prevention after ICH, the blood pressure target is {{c1::≤130/80 mmHg}}; uncontrolled hypertension is the dominant modifiable risk factor for recurrence.
Symptomatic Candida cystitis caused by a fluconazole-susceptible organism is treated with fluconazole {{c1::200 mg (3 mg/kg) PO daily for 2 weeks}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XIV, Thresholds & Doses

text: Tofacitinib and upadacitinib are labeled for use after {{c1::inadequate response or intolerance to ≥1 TNF inhibitor}}; caution is advised in patients with {{c2::cardiovascular risk factors or VTE history}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC / Induction section

text: Tenecteplase weight-based dosing: 30 mg if <60 kg; 35 mg if 60-69 kg; 40 mg if 70-79 kg; 45 mg if 80-89 kg; {{c1::50 mg}} if ≥{{c1::90 kg}}.
AASLD 2023 recommends HCC surveillance with {{c1::semiannual liver ultrasound plus AFP}} in all patients with cirrhosis (Child-Turcotte-Pugh A or B).
extra: Surveillance section — Combined ultrasound + AFP recommended over ultrasound alone; sensitivity 63% vs. 53% for early HCC detection.

text: In ACS patients undergoing PCI, {{c1::ticagrelor or prasugrel}} is preferred over {{c1::clopidogrel}} as the P2Y12 inhibitor.
Primary prevention ICD is indicated for ischemic or nonischemic cardiomyopathy with NYHA class II–III HF and LVEF {{c1::≤35%}} after optimal GDMT.

The 2023 guideline makes {{c1::no recommendation}} for or against routine continuous NMBA infusion in moderate-to-severe ARDS, citing conflicting evidence from the {{c2::ACURASYS}} and {{c2::ROSE}} trials.
extra: Recommendation 8.1, Domain 8: Neuromuscular Blockade — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: After TAVR, new high-degree AV block persisting >{{c1::48 hours}} warrants pacemaker implantation; the median time to PPM implantation post-TAVR is approximately {{c2::3 days}}.
Uncomplicated vulvovaginal candidiasis is treated with a single {{c1::fluconazole 150 mg}} oral dose.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XV, Thresholds & Doses

text: Universal HCV screening is recommended {{c1::each pregnancy}}, except in settings where HCV positivity rate is {{c2::<0.1%}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Pregnant Women, Universal Screening section

text: In sepsis, {{c1::procalcitonin}} combined with clinical evaluation should guide antibiotic discontinuation when optimal duration is unclear; procalcitonin-guided de-escalation is associated with reduced mortality (RR 0.89 across 14 RCTs).
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Biomarkers to Discontinue Antibiotics section

text: Nonsevere CDI is defined as WBC {{c1::≤15,000 cells/µL}} AND serum creatinine {{c2::<1.5 mg/dL}}.
For isolated distal DVT without severe symptoms or risk factors for extension, perform {{c1::serial ultrasound imaging for 2 weeks}} rather than anticoagulating empirically.
HPV-vaccinated women {{c1::should continue standard cervical cancer screening}} because long-term vaccine efficacy data are still accruing.
A spot urine Na/K ratio {{c1::>1}} indicates adequate natriuresis (patient should be losing weight); a ratio {{c1::≤1}} indicates insufficient natriuresis and warrants increasing diuretics.
For 2026, the {{c1::National Kidney Foundation (NKF)}} newly endorsed Section {{c2::11}} (CKD and Risk Management) of the ADA Standards of Care.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Methodology and Procedure section

text: Naltrexone-bupropion ER should be discontinued if less than {{c1::5% total body weight loss}} is achieved after {{c2::12 weeks}} on the maintenance dose, as the patient is likely a non-responder.
ACP 2023 states that selection of bisphosphonate agent, route, and frequency should account for {{c1::patient preferences}}, {{c2::tolerability}}, and adherence considerations.
In HF during pregnancy, {{c1::ACEi, ARB, ARNi, MRA, SGLT2i, and ivabradine}} are contraindicated; acceptable agents include {{c2::furosemide, metoprolol, hydralazine, and nitrates}}.
A toe pressure of {{c1::<30 mm Hg}} indicates severe ischemia and is associated with major amputation risk and poor wound healing in CLTI.
extra: Section 3.1 (Resting ABI and Physiological Testing) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: The 2019 EVALI outbreak (>2,800 US cases, 68 deaths as of February 2020) was strongly linked to {{c1::vitamin E acetate}} in {{c2::THC-containing}} e-cigarettes.
extra: Practice Considerations: Suggestions for I Statements (e-Cigarettes) — USPSTF 2021

text: HCC surveillance with {{c1::ultrasound every 6 months}} is recommended for all cirrhotic HBV patients; {{c2::AFP alone}} is not recommended except when ultrasound is unavailable.
extra: Updated Recommendations on Treatment; HCC Surveillance section

text: Routine polysomnography is {{c1::NOT recommended}} for asymptomatic SCD patients; obtain when symptoms such as {{c2::snoring, witnessed apneas, excessive daytime sleepiness, or unexplained hypoxemia}} are present.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Sleep-Disordered Breathing: Screening section

text: The BP target for neurologically stable patients with prior stroke or TIA is {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}}; antihypertensive therapy should be initiated when SBP exceeds {{c1::130 mm Hg}}.
During rituximab maintenance for GPA/MPA, use {{c1::scheduled re-dosing}} rather than ANCA titer- or CD19 B-cell-guided re-dosing.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Rec 10 (GPA/MPA)

text: In treatment-naive active PsA, the preferred biologic hierarchy is: {{c1::TNFi}} preferred over {{c1::IL-17i}}, which is preferred over {{c1::IL-12/23i}}.
extra: Table 1 — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendations.

text: For nonischemic cardiomyopathy, primary prevention ICD requires at least {{c1::3 months}} of guideline-directed medical therapy before implantation.
extra: Section 7.2.2 (Primary Prevention ICD, NICM) — DANISH trial

text: In RA patients with {{c1::NYHA class III or IV}} heart failure, a non-TNFi bDMARD or tsDMARD is conditionally recommended over a TNF inhibitor.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific Populations); conditional recommendation

text: Cryptococcal antigen (CrAG) screening is recommended at HIV diagnosis if CD4+ is {{c1::≤100 cells/μL}}.
LQTS is diagnosed by QTc {{c1::≥500 ms}}, LQTS risk score {{c2::≥3.5}}, QTc 480–499 ms with syncope, or a pathogenic LQTS gene mutation.
All HBV-HIV–coinfected patients should receive antiretroviral therapy containing {{c1::two HBV-active drugs}}: tenofovir (TAF or TDF) plus {{c2::lamivudine or emtricitabine}}.
extra: Updated Recommendations on Treatment; Special Populations

text: For treatment-phase anticoagulation in VTE, {{c1::a DOAC (apixaban, dabigatran, edoxaban, or rivaroxaban)}} is preferred over {{c1::VKA}} (strong recommendation).
In patients with HIT requiring RRT, all heparin should be stopped and {{c1::argatroban}} or a {{c1::Factor Xa inhibitor}} used instead (Grade 1A).
For children 6–11 yr, low-dose ICS (budesonide metered) is {{c1::100–200 mcg/day}}; medium-dose is {{c1::>200–400 mcg/day}}; high-dose is >400 mcg/day.
USPSTF 2024 assigns {{c1::Grade I (insufficient evidence)}} for supplemental screening with {{c1::ultrasound or MRI}} in women with dense breasts and an otherwise negative mammogram.
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade I for supplemental screening in dense breasts

text: The fluconazole alternative dosing for candidemia is {{c1::800 mg (12 mg/kg)}} loading, then {{c2::400 mg (6 mg/kg)}} IV/PO daily.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section I, Thresholds & Doses

text: Before IVT, blood pressure must be below {{c1::SBP 185 mm Hg}} and {{c1::DBP 110 mm Hg}}.

Preferred first-line therapies for immune-active chronic hepatitis B in adults are {{c1::TAF}}, {{c1::TDF}}, or {{c1::peg-IFN-α}}; {{c2::entecavir}} is also preferred due to its high resistance barrier.

Before initiating PAH-specific therapy in SCD, PH group must be classified via {{c1::right-heart catheterization}}, and a {{c2::multidisciplinary team (hematology, PH specialist, pulmonology or cardiology)}} should be consulted.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — PAH Treatment section (Figure 2)

text: When initiating ULT, ACR 2020 strongly recommends concomitant anti-inflammatory prophylaxis (colchicine, NSAIDs, or prednisone/prednisolone) for at least {{c1::3–6 months}}, extended if flares continue.
Intramuscular methylprednisolone, as an alternative to oral GCs in PMR, is started at {{c1::120 mg every 3 weeks}} for the first 9 weeks.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 5

text: PJP prophylaxis is indicated for all GPA/MPA or EGPA patients receiving {{c1::cyclophosphamide or rituximab}}, and when glucocorticoids exceed {{c2::20 mg/day}} combined with MTX, AZA, or MMF.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Rec 24 / EGPA Rec 15

text: ATA 2015 recommends biopsy of sonographically suspicious cervical lymph nodes if the smallest diameter is {{c1::≥8–10 mm}}, provided the result would change management.
TEER for secondary MR (COAPT criteria) requires LVEF {{c1::20–50%}}, LVESD ≤70 mm, pulmonary artery systolic pressure ≤70 mm Hg, and NYHA class {{c2::II–IV}} on optimal GDMT.
FMT is {{c1::not recommended}} for IBS outside of {{c2::clinical trials}} per ACG 2021.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Gut-Directed Psychotherapy section; Table 2

text: Stable disease in axSpA is defined as symptoms at an acceptable level for a minimum of {{c1::6 months}}.
In septic shock or confirmed/very likely sepsis, IV antibiotics must be administered within {{c1::1 hour}}.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Time to Antibiotics section (Figure 1)

text: For cirrhotic ascites, spironolactone is started at {{c1::100 mg/day}} and titrated up to a maximum of {{c2::400 mg/day}}, with dose adjustments no more often than every {{c3::72 hours}}.
Apixaban dose is reduced to {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}} if ≥2 of the following are met: age ≥{{c2::80}} years, weight ≤{{c2::60 kg}}, or SCr ≥{{c2::1.5 mg/dL}}.

For critically ill hemodynamically stable children without hemoglobinopathy, cyanotic cardiac condition, or severe hypoxemia, AABB 2023 recommends a restrictive transfusion threshold of {{c1::<7 g/dL}} (strong recommendation, moderate certainty).
In primary aldosteronism, screen with ARR when BP is resistant to {{c1::≥3 antihypertensive drugs (including a diuretic)}}, or controlled on {{c2::≥4 drugs}}.

In GINA 2026 Track 2 (alternative), when ICS-formoterol is unavailable, the preferred reliever across Steps 1–5 is {{c1::as-needed low-dose ICS-SABA}}; SABA alone is acceptable only if {{c2::this option is also unavailable}}.
EVL for esophageal varices is repeated every {{c1::2–4 weeks}} (favoring 4 weeks) until obliteration; surveillance endoscopy is performed every {{c2::6–12 months}} after eradication.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations & Thresholds; Section: Prevention of recurrent hemorrhage

text: RAI remnant ablation is {{c1::NOT}} routinely recommended for ATA {{c2::low-risk}} DTC after total thyroidectomy.
Hydrocortisone is indicated in septic shock when norepinephrine or epinephrine is ≥{{c1::0.25 mcg/kg/min}} for ≥{{c2::4 hours}} to maintain target MAP.
In adult kidney transplant recipients, the BP target is SBP {{c1::<130 mm Hg}} and DBP {{c2::<80 mm Hg}}.
A 30-day MACE risk of {{c1::<1%}}, identified by a validated clinical decision pathway, defines low-risk chest pain; these patients do {{c2::not}} require routine admission or stress testing.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Table 8 (Low-Risk Definition); Section 4.1.1

text: For purposes of IDA evaluation, anemia in men is defined as hemoglobin {{c1::<13 g/dL}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — AGA 2020 GI Evaluation of IDA

text: For children with newly diagnosed ITP and non–life-threatening mucosal bleeding, ASH 2019 makes a strong recommendation for corticosteroid courses of ≤{{c1::7 days}}.
After successful endovascular reperfusion for large vessel occlusion stroke, do not lower SBP below {{c1::140 mm Hg}} within the first {{c2::24–72 hours}}.
Colonoscopy perforation rates per 10,000 procedures: {{c1::3.1}} for screening colonoscopy vs {{c2::5.4}} for follow-up colonoscopy after a positive stool test.
The most compelling trial regimen for corticosteroids in severe CAP is {{c1::hydrocortisone 200 mg/day}} continuous IV infusion for {{c2::4–7 days}}, followed by a taper to a total of 8–14 days.
extra: Question 4 (Corticosteroids) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; Dequin et al. cited; Thresholds & Doses.

text: CABG is supported to improve survival in patients with multivessel CAD and LV dysfunction defined as EF {{c1::≤35%}}, based on 10-year STICH trial data.
Per KDIGO 2017, calcitriol and vitamin D analogs should {{c1::not be routinely used}} in pre-dialysis CKD G3a–G5; reserve for {{c2::severe, progressive hyperparathyroidism}} in CKD G4–G5.
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Chapter 4.2, Recommendation 4.2.2 (reversed 2009 recommendation)

text: Steroidogenesis inhibitors (ketoconazole, metyrapone, mitotane) are recommended as {{c1::second-line medical therapy after failed TSS}} in Cushing's disease, and as {{c2::primary therapy for occult or metastatic ectopic ACTH secretion}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Rec. 6.4 / Section 6.4 (1|QQQE)

text: Ezetimibe is dosed at {{c1::10 mg orally once daily}}, reducing LDL-C by ~{{c2::18%}} as monotherapy and ~{{c3::25%}} when added to a statin.
Statins are {{c1::safe}} and recommended for CVD risk reduction across the NAFLD disease spectrum, including in patients with {{c2::compensated cirrhosis}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statement 1; Dyslipidemia subsection

text: In bicuspid aortic valve, aortic replacement is recommended when sinus or ascending aorta diameter reaches {{c1::≥5.5 cm}}, or {{c1::≥5.0 cm}} in the presence of risk factors (e.g., family history of dissection, growth ≥0.5 cm/year).

In older adults with bacteriuria and delirium or a fall but without localizing genitourinary symptoms or systemic signs of infection, IDSA 2019 recommends {{c1::assessing for other causes and observing}} rather than treating with antimicrobials.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section V (Older Adults: Nonlocalizing Symptoms)

text: Inhaled {{c1::treprostinil}} at a target dose of {{c2::72 µg four times daily}} may be considered in PH associated with ILD (INCREASE trial).
extra: Recommendation Table 23 (Group 3 PH/lung disease, p. 3693); Thresholds & Doses — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: First-line antihypertensive drug classes include {{c1::thiazide-type diuretics}}, {{c1::long-acting dihydropyridine CCBs}}, {{c1::ACE inhibitors}}, and {{c1::ARBs}}; beta-blockers are NOT first-line unless a compelling indication exists.
Routine follow-up biopsy in {{c1::asymptomatic children}} on GFD is {{c2::not recommended}}; mucosal healing is achieved in {{c3::95%}} of children within 2 years.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Diet and Long-term Outcomes section

text: HEART score risk strata: ≤3 = {{c1::low-risk}}; 4–6 = {{c2::intermediate-risk}}; 7–10 = {{c3::high-risk}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Table 6 (Clinical Decision Pathways)

text: After IVT, the recommended SBP target is {{c1::140–180 mm Hg}}; intensive reduction to <140 mm Hg is {{c1::not recommended (no benefit)}}.
extra: Section 4.3 (Blood Pressure Management); COR 3: No Benefit

text: On VCTE, a liver stiffness measurement of {{c1::<8 kPa}} rules out advanced fibrosis (especially when used after FIB-4 stratification).
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Elastography subsection (NITs section)

text: For perinatal depression screening, the EPDS cutoff of {{c1::11–12}} yields sensitivity of 81–90% and specificity of 83–88%.
Chronic migraine is defined as headache on ≥{{c1::15}} days/month for >{{c2::3 months}}, with ≥{{c3::8}} days/month meeting migraine criteria.
extra: Table 1 — ICHD-3 diagnostic criteria for chronic migraine

text: MSM at ongoing risk should be screened for gonorrhea, chlamydia, and syphilis every {{c1::3–6 months}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — STI Detection Among Special Populations, MSM section

text: Ramucirumab is eligible as second-line systemic therapy for HCC only in patients with AFP ≥ {{c1::400 ng/mL}} (REACH-2 trial).
extra: Thresholds & Doses — AFP ≥400 ng/mL criterion, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: Co-administration of {{c1::low-dose methotrexate}} alongside TNFi is {{c2::not recommended}} in AS or nr-axSpA.
{{c1::Pioglitazone}} improves NASH histology and can be considered in patients with {{c2::T2DM or pre-DM}} who have biopsy-confirmed NASH.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 23–28; Table 6

text: Mirikizumab induction: {{c1::300 mg IV every 4 weeks × 3 doses}}; maintenance: {{c2::200 mg SC every 4 weeks}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC section

text: For noncardioembolic ischemic stroke or TIA, {{c1::antiplatelet therapy}} (not anticoagulation) is first-line; warfarin is not superior to aspirin and carries {{c2::higher bleeding risk}}.
extra: Section 5.19 (Figure 6) — Antiplatelet algorithm for noncardioembolic stroke/TIA

text: Long-term beta-blocker therapy is NOT recommended for CCD patients who lack {{c1::MI within the past year}}, {{c1::LVEF ≤50%}}, or another primary beta-blocker indication.
Antihypertensive pharmacotherapy should be initiated at BP ≥130/80 mm Hg in patients with a 10-year ASCVD risk of {{c1::≥10%}}.
extra: Section 4.4 / Figure 4 and Table 7; Thresholds & Doses

text: TBLC has an overall diagnostic yield of approximately {{c1::79%}}, rising to ~{{c2::85%}} when ≥3 samples are collected.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF (TBLC section)

text: Obtaining ≥4 duodenal biopsy specimens significantly increases celiac disease diagnosis rate ({{c1::1.8%}} vs {{c1::0.7%}}, P<0.0001) compared with fewer specimens.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Thresholds & Doses; Diagnosis section

text: Pregnant women with PAI should use {{c1::hydrocortisone}} rather than {{c2::dexamethasone}}, because the latter crosses the placenta; the dose should be increased by {{c3::20–40%}} from week 24 onward.
extra: Section 3.0, Rec. 3.x — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: For HAP (non-ventilated), add empiric MRSA coverage when the MRSA rate exceeds {{c1::20%}} of S. aureus isolates, or when prevalence is unknown.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section XII; Thresholds & Doses

text: Penicillin skin testing has a negative predictive value of {{c1::97%–99%}}, supporting safe use of β-lactams in patients with a reported penicillin allergy.
extra: Recommendation 13 (Section XII) — weak recommendation, low-quality evidence.

text: The USPSTF 2021 recommends {{c1::behavioral counseling alone}} (not pharmacotherapy) for pregnant persons who smoke (Grade {{c1::A}}).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — USPSTF 2021 Tobacco Cessation

text: For subclinical hyperthyroidism with TSH persistently {{c1::0.1–0.4 mU/L}}, consider treatment in patients ≥65 years and those with cardiac disease, osteoporosis, or symptoms; {{c2::observe}} asymptomatic patients <65 years.
The target INR for warfarin in AF is {{c1::2–3}}; bleeding risk markedly increases when INR {{c2::>4}}.
Semaglutide titration for obesity: 0.25 mg × 4 wks → 0.5 mg × 4 wks → 1.0 mg × 4 wks → 1.7 mg × 4 wks → {{c1::2.4 mg}} (target maintenance reached at {{c2::16 weeks}}).
The reduced dose of apixaban for extended-phase VTE anticoagulation is {{c1::2.5 mg twice daily}}.
A 10-year ASCVD risk of {{c1::≥20%}} is classified as high risk, warranting statin therapy for primary prevention.
Mild oropharyngeal candidiasis is treated with {{c1::clotrimazole troches 10 mg 5 times daily}} OR {{c2::miconazole mucoadhesive buccal 50 mg daily}} for 7–14 days.
Galcanezumab is initiated with a {{c1::240 mg loading dose}}, followed by {{c2::120 mg monthly}} SC.
Rivaroxaban VTE treatment dosing: {{c1::15 mg twice daily × 21 days}}, then {{c1::20 mg once daily}} for ≥6 months.
extra: ITAC 2022, Thresholds & Doses; SELECT-D, CASTA-DIVA trials

text: In the 2026 GOLD update, Group E now requires only {{c1::≥ 1}} moderate or severe exacerbation per year (previously ≥ 2).
Stage 1 hypertension is defined as SBP {{c1::130–139}} mm Hg OR DBP {{c1::80–89}} mm Hg.
extra: Table 4 (Categories of Blood Pressure in Adults) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Initial management of ASUC includes IV corticosteroids, testing for {{c1::C. difficile}}, flexible {{c2::sigmoidoscopy}} (not full colonoscopy), and early multidisciplinary surgical consultation.

Lung transplantation referral is recommended at intermediate-high/high risk or REVEAL score {{c1::>7}}; listing is recommended at high risk or REVEAL score {{c2::≥10}} on optimized therapy including parenteral prostacyclin.
extra: Thresholds & Doses; Key Recommendations — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: IV pulse glucocorticoids for GCA in adults: methylprednisolone {{c1::500–1,000 mg/day IV}} for {{c1::3–5 days}}.
extra: Table 1; Thresholds & Doses — IV pulse glucocorticoids (adults)

text: PCT plus clinical criteria may be used to guide antibiotic {{c1::discontinuation}} (but NOT {{c2::initiation}}) in HAP/VAP.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section XXIV (PCT-guided discontinuation)

text: In the 4R regimen, rifampin is dosed at {{c1::10 mg/kg}} daily in adults and {{c2::15–20 mg/kg}} daily in children (max {{c1::600 mg}}) for 120 doses.
extra: Table 4; Preferred Regimens: Four Months of Daily Rifampin

text: Patients with HFimpEF (prior LVEF ≤40%, now >40%) should {{c1::continue HFrEF GDMT}}, because withdrawal leads to relapse in approximately {{c2::40%}} within 6 months.
In a high-probability 4Ts patient, the functional assay may be omitted if the ELISA optical density is {{c1::≥2.0}} units.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Thresholds & Doses; Table 4

text: On-demand or intermittent PPI therapy is acceptable for {{c1::NERD and uncomplicated GERD without erosive esophagitis}}.

In {{c1::nonneutropenic}} patients with candidemia where the catheter is the presumed source, CVCs should be removed {{c2::as early as possible}}; removal is {{c3::individualized}} in neutropenic patients.
Rapid aortic growth in sporadic aneurysms is defined as ≥{{c1::0.5 cm in 1 year}} or ≥{{c2::0.3 cm/year for 2 consecutive years}}, and is an independent indication for surgery.
For non-severe GPA induction, {{c1::methotrexate}} + glucocorticoids is preferred as first-line over cyclophosphamide, rituximab, azathioprine, MMF, or SMX/TMP.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Recs 5–8 (GPA/MPA)

text: ACR 2020 conditionally recommends against ULT after a first gout flare UNLESS the patient has CKD stage >3, SU >{{c1::9 mg/dl}}, or {{c2::urolithiasis}}.
For isolated distal DVT without severe symptoms or extension risk factors, {{c1::serial leg imaging for 2 weeks}} is preferred over immediate anticoagulation.
extra: Guidance Statement 1–2; Summary of Recommendations, p. e546

text: For ambulatory cancer patients (Khorana score ≥2), thromboprophylaxis options are rivaroxaban {{c1::10 mg once daily}} (CASSINI) or apixaban {{c2::2.5 mg twice daily}} (AVERT), for up to 6 months.
To reduce infection risk during CIED implantation in high-risk IE patients, {{c1::antibiotic-impregnated envelopes}} should be considered.
extra: Future Considerations section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: In patients with pre-DM, T2DM, or ≥2 metabolic risk factors, FIB-4 should be repeated every {{c1::1–2 years}} due to higher risk of fibrosis progression.
The pragmatic low-dose aspirin regimen recommended for eligible patients who elect to start is {{c1::81 mg/day}}.
extra: Treatment or Intervention section; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: Celiac disease prevalence in first-degree relatives is approximately {{c1::5%–20%}} (siblings up to {{c1::20%}}, other first-degree relatives up to {{c2::10%}}).
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Screening section, Table 4

text: 6MWD risk thresholds in PAH: low risk {{c1::>440 m}}; intermediate risk {{c1::165–440 m}}; high risk {{c1::<165 m}}.
extra: Table 16 (Three-strata risk model, p. 3657) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: The target serum digoxin concentration in HFrEF is {{c1::0.5–<0.9 ng/mL}}; concentrations ≥{{c2::1.2 ng/mL}} are associated with increased mortality.
ACG 2021 conditionally recommends {{c1::CBT-GI or gut-directed hypnotherapy}} as adjunctive treatment for global IBS symptoms regardless of subtype; NNT = {{c2::4}} using IBS-SSS.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Gut-Directed Psychotherapy section; Ford et al. Am J Gastroenterol 2019 meta-analysis

text: The suggested dialysate calcium concentration in CKD G5D is {{c1::1.25–1.50 mmol/l}} ({{c1::2.5–3.0 mEq/l}}).
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Chapter 4.1, Recommendation 4.1.4 (evidence grade upgraded 2D→2C)

text: IE prophylaxis before dental procedures is reasonable for highest-risk patients, including those with {{c1::prosthetic valves}}, {{c1::prior IE}}, or {{c1::congenital heart disease with residual shunts}}.
Cilostazol improves claudication symptoms but is contraindicated in {{c1::heart failure of any severity}}.
extra: Section 5.1 / claudication pharmacotherapy — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: First-line treatment for urogenital, rectal, and oropharyngeal chlamydia in nonpregnant adults is {{c1::doxycycline 100 mg orally BID × 7 days}}, preferred over azithromycin due to higher microbiologic failure rates with azithromycin.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Chlamydial Infections, Treatment section

text: In hemodynamically stable hospitalized patients with UGIB, transfuse RBCs when hemoglobin falls below {{c1::7 g/dL}}.
extra: Red Blood Cell Transfusion section, Table 3 (Villanueva 2013) — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: On VCTE, a liver stiffness measurement of {{c1::>12 kPa}} is associated with a high likelihood of {{c2::advanced fibrosis}} (PPV 0.34–0.71).
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Elastography subsection (NITs section)

text: The 2021 USPSTF recommends annual LDCT lung cancer screening for adults aged {{c1::50–80}} years (Grade B).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table; Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation section

text: Esophageal candidiasis is treated with fluconazole {{c1::200–400 mg (3–6 mg/kg) PO daily for 14–21 days}}.
Long-term DAPT beyond {{c1::90 days}} is NOT recommended for secondary stroke prevention due to {{c2::excess bleeding (ICH)}} without additional stroke benefit (SPS3, MATCH trials).
extra: Section 5.19 — DAPT not recommended beyond 90 days; SPS3, MATCH trial evidence

text: The recommended HCC surveillance interval is {{c1::every 6 months (semiannual)}}; {{c2::quarterly}} surveillance does not improve outcomes.
extra: Surveillance section — HCC surveillance interval, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: For VKA-associated ICH, anticoagulation should be reversed with {{c1::4-factor PCC}} (not FFP) plus {{c2::IV vitamin K 5–10 mg}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1 and Figure 2

text: In HCM, an LV wall thickness of {{c1::≥30 mm}} is an established SCD risk factor warranting ICD consideration.
Among men aged 55–69 years screened with PSA over 13 years, an estimated {{c1::~1.3}} prostate cancer deaths and {{c2::~3}} cases of metastatic prostate cancer are prevented per 1,000 men screened.
extra: Table: Estimated Effects After 13 Years; Rationale: Benefits of Early Detection and Treatment

text: In obese patients with GERD, {{c1::RYGB (Roux-en-Y gastric bypass)}} is preferred over fundoplication as the antireflux surgical procedure due to higher reflux recurrence after fundoplication in this population.
extra: Surgical and Endoscopic Options section; Table 1

text: Before initiating anti-TNF therapy in CD, screen for {{c1::latent tuberculosis}} (TST and/or IGRA) and {{c2::hepatitis B}} (HBsAg, anti-HBs, anti-HBc), and update vaccinations.
extra: Anti-TNF Agents section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: Sick day rules: double the hydrocortisone dose for fever {{c1::>38°C}} and triple the dose for fever {{c2::>39°C}}.
extra: Table 3 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: For active TAK not on immunosuppressive therapy, initiate {{c1::high-dose oral glucocorticoids plus a non-GC immunosuppressive agent}} (methotrexate, azathioprine, or TNF inhibitor) rather than glucocorticoids alone; prefer non-GC agents over {{c2::tocilizumab}} as initial therapy.
Preoperative KI for Graves' thyroidectomy: Lugol's solution {{c1::5–7 drops}} or SSKI {{c2::1–2 drops}} three times daily for {{c3::10 days}} before surgery.
Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) for CDI should be considered only after at least {{c1::2 recurrences}} (i.e., {{c2::3 total CDI episodes}}).
For intermediate-risk patients without known CAD, {{c1::CCTA}} is the preferred initial test in patients {{c2::<65 years}} not on optimal preventive therapies.
A fasting lipid panel should be rechecked {{c1::4-8 weeks}} after initiating or adjusting lipid-lowering therapy post-ACS.

The only USPSTF-recommended modality for lung cancer screening is {{c1::low-dose computed tomography (LDCT)}}; {{c2::sputum cytology, chest radiography, and biomarker measurement}} are not recommended.
Oral prednisone induction for UC is dosed at {{c1::40–60 mg/day}} as a single dose; no added benefit is demonstrated above {{c2::60 mg/day}}.
Pancreatic necrosis is defined on contrast-enhanced CT as nonviable parenchyma {{c1::>3 cm}} or {{c1::>30% of the pancreas}}.
POTS is defined by a heart rate increase of {{c1::≥30 bpm}} ({{c1::≥40 bpm}} in ages 12–19) within 10 minutes of standing, with standing HR often >120 bpm.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Table 3; Thresholds & Doses

text: Universal lipid screening in children is recommended at ages {{c1::9–11 years}}; statin therapy may be initiated at age ≥{{c2::8 years}} if LDL-C ≥{{c3::160 mg/dL}} after a lifestyle trial in HeFH.

For factor Xa inhibitor–associated ICH (rivaroxaban, apixaban, edoxaban), the preferred specific reversal agent is {{c1::andexanet alfa}}; {{c2::4-factor PCC}} is a reasonable alternative when it is unavailable.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.2.1 and Figure 2

text: Ticagrelor loading dose is {{c1::180 mg}}; maintenance dose is {{c2::90 mg twice daily}}.
extra: Table 9 / Section 11.1 — Antiplatelet Agents for PCI

text: UC should be diagnosed with {{c1::lower endoscopy and histologic confirmation}}; full colonoscopy with terminal ileum visualization is performed at index evaluation unless {{c2::severe disease}} warrants sigmoidoscopy only.
High-risk neutropenia, for which no ASB recommendation can be made (knowledge gap), is defined as ANC {{c1::<100 cells/mm³}} for {{c2::≥7 days}} following cytotoxic chemotherapy.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Key Recommendations; Thresholds & Doses

text: Guselkumab induction: {{c1::200 mg IV at weeks 0, 4, and 8}}; maintenance: {{c2::SC q4w (same induction dose) or 100 mg SC q8w}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC section

text: In patients with LVEF {{c1::36%–50%}} and AV block expected to require ventricular pacing >{{c2::40%}} of the time, physiologic pacing (CRT or His bundle pacing) is preferred over RV pacing to prevent heart failure.
extra: Section 6.4.4.1 (Pacing Techniques for AV Block); BLOCK HF trial

text: Severe AS is defined by aortic Vmax {{c1::≥4 m/s}} or mean gradient {{c2::≥40 mm Hg}}, with AVA typically ≤1.0 cm².
extra: 2020 ACC/AHA VHD Guideline — Table 13 (Stages of AS)

text: In PPI-refractory patients referred for workup, only approximately {{c1::21%}} have symptoms that are truly reflux-related on rigorous objective testing.
The LT4 suppressive dose for thyroid cancer is {{c1::2.1–2.7 μg/kg/day}}, higher than the standard replacement dose.
Before initiating natalizumab, {{c1::JC virus antibody}} testing is required, and must be repeated every {{c2::6 months}} during therapy.
extra: Table 1 (GRADE Recommendations Summary); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: In Group E, consider initial triple therapy (LABA+LAMA+ICS) if blood eosinophils are {{c1::≥ 300 cells/µL}}.
CRT for LBBB with QRS {{c1::120–149 ms}} carries a Class of Recommendation of {{c2::2a}}.
Weight loss of 3–5% improves hepatic {{c1::steatosis}}, but weight loss of {{c2::>10%}} is generally required to improve NASH histology and fibrosis.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Treatment section; Guidance Statement 29

text: A FIB-4 score of {{c1::>2.67}} indicates high risk for advanced fibrosis and warrants direct referral to {{c2::gastroenterology/hepatology}}.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 9–16; Figure 2

text: Alosetron is restricted under a {{c1::REMS program}} to {{c2::women}} with severe, chronic IBS-D (>6 months) refractory to other therapies, dosed at 0.5–1 mg twice daily.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — IBS-D Pharmacotherapy section (Alosetron); Thresholds & Doses

text: 3HR (isoniazid + rifampin daily × 3 months) is a {{c1::conditionally recommended preferred}} LTBI regimen for adults and children of all ages, including HIV-positive persons as drug interactions allow.
Carotid sinus hypersensitivity is defined as a pause {{c1::≥3 seconds}} and/or systolic BP decrease {{c2::≥50 mmHg}} on carotid sinus massage.
extra: 2017 ACC/AHA/HRS Syncope Guideline — Table 3; Thresholds & Doses

text: In Cushing's syndrome of any etiology (adrenal, pituitary, ectopic), {{c1::surgical resection of the causal lesion}} is recommended as first-line treatment unless surgery is not possible or unlikely to achieve cortisol reduction.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Section 3 (First-line treatment), Rec. 3.1c

text: PCSK9 monoclonal antibodies (alirocumab 75–150 mg or evolocumab 140 mg every 2 weeks) reduce LDL-C by approximately {{c1::45%–64%}}.

In hypertensive adults with CKD, RAASi (ACEi or ARB, not both) is indicated when eGFR is {{c1::<60 mL/min/1.73 m²}} and albuminuria is {{c2::≥30 mg/g}} to reduce CVD risk and delay kidney disease progression.
extra: Section 5.3.8 (Hypertension Treatment in Patients With CKD) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: A CDAI of {{c1::<150}} defines symptomatic remission in Crohn's disease.
extra: Table 1 (GRADE Recommendations Summary); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: VV-ECMO is strongly recommended for severe ARDS meeting {{c1::EOLIA criteria}} at an {{c2::experienced ECMO center}}, including COVID-19 ARDS.
extra: Recommendation 9.1, Domain 9: VV-ECMO — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: Exercise PH is defined by an mPAP/CO slope {{c1::>3 mmHg/L/min}} between rest and exercise on RHC.
extra: Table 5 (Haemodynamic definitions of PH) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: In antiphospholipid syndrome, dose-adjusted {{c1::VKA}} targeting an INR of {{c2::2.5}} is suggested over DOAC therapy during the treatment phase.
extra: Guidance Statement 17; Table 12 and Figure 5, pp. e576–e578

text: In a patient with IgA deficiency, celiac disease serology should use {{c1::IgG-based tests (DGP-IgG and/or TTG-IgG)}} instead of TTG-IgA.
In elderly patients (>70–80 years), the ATA 2014 guideline suggests raising the TSH target to {{c1::4–6 mIU/L}}, reflecting the age-related rightward shift in TSH distribution.
extra: ATA 2014 Hypothyroidism Guideline, Thresholds & Doses — TSH target for elderly.

text: Low-dose aspirin increases major GI bleeding risk by approximately {{c1::58%}} (Peto OR 1.58, 95% CI 1.38–1.80) in primary prevention trials.
extra: Supporting Evidence / Harms of Preventive Medication; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: In DMARD-naive RA patients with low disease activity, {{c1::hydroxychloroquine}} is conditionally preferred over other csDMARDs, and {{c2::sulfasalazine}} is conditionally preferred over methotrexate.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 2 (DMARD Initiation); conditional recommendations

text: The US guideline specifies a minimum correction rate of {{c1::4–8 mmol/L per day}} (or {{c1::4–6 mmol/L per day}} in high ODS risk patients); the European guideline specifies no minimum.
extra: Table 2 — US vs. European guideline comparison

text: In ACS patients with anemia not actively bleeding, a liberal transfusion strategy targets hemoglobin ~{{c1::10 g/dL}}.
The blood pressure target for CCD patients with hypertension is systolic {{c1::<130 mm Hg}}, which reduces CVD complications by ~25% and all-cause mortality by ~27%.
On a third-generation assay, overt hyperthyroidism is consistent with a TSH of {{c1::<0.01 mU/L}}.
In GIOP, bisphosphonate treatment failure (prompting class switch) is defined as an incident fracture ≥{{c1::18 months}} after initiation OR BMD loss ≥{{c2::10%}}/year.
extra: Table 4; Thresholds & Doses — Treatment failure definition

text: Preventive migraine treatment should be OFFERED at ≥{{c1::6}} headache days/month (any disability), ≥{{c2::4}} days/month with some disability, or ≥{{c3::3}} days/month with severe disability.
extra: Table 4 — Thresholds for offering vs. considering preventive treatment

text: For refractory ascites, {{c1::TIPS}} is preferred over repeated LVP in well-selected patients; use a {{c2::small-diameter (<10 mm) covered stent}} to reduce hepatic encephalopathy risk.

All patients with candidemia should have a {{c1::dilated ophthalmological examination}} within the {{c2::first week}}; neutropenic patients should be examined within the first week after {{c2::neutrophil recovery}}.
The DETECT algorithm is recommended in SSc patients with disease duration {{c1::>3 years}}, FVC {{c2::≥40%}}, and DLCO {{c3::<60%}} for PAH screening.
Enteric-coated PPIs should be taken {{c1::30–60 minutes before the first meal}} for once-daily dosing; {{c2::bedtime dosing}} is discouraged.
Recurrent provoked VTE in SCD warrants {{c1::indefinite}} anticoagulation, because SCD itself is considered a chronic underlying risk factor.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — VTE Management table

text: {{c1::LMWH and fondaparinux}} should be avoided if CrCl is <30 mL/min; {{c2::apixaban and edoxaban}} should be avoided if CrCl is <15 mL/min.
The USPSTF 2023 assigns a Grade {{c1::B}} recommendation for MDD screening in all adults ≥19 years, including pregnant/postpartum persons and older adults ≥65 years.
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — USPSTF 2023 Depression/Anxiety Screening

text: Bivalirudin for PPCI: bolus {{c1::0.75 mg/kg}}, infusion {{c2::1.75 mg/kg/h}}; post-PCI infusion continues at 1.75 mg/kg/h for {{c3::2-4 hours}}; reduce to 1 mg/kg/h if CrCl <30 mL/min.
extra: Section 4.4 / Table 10 — 2025 ACC/AHA ACS Guideline; BRIGHT-4 trial

text: In the Moayyedi 2019 RCT, pantoprazole 40 mg daily for 3 years showed no significant increase in adverse events except {{c1::enteric infections}} (OR {{c2::1.33}}, 95% CI 1.01–1.75 vs placebo).
extra: Table 5 (Major Putative Adverse Effects of Chronic PPI Therapy)

text: AGA 2022 issues a {{c1::conditional recommendation against}} orlistat due to its unfavorable GI adverse effect profile relative to modest weight loss; patients who use it must supplement with {{c2::fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K)}} taken ≥2 hours apart.
extra: Table 1, Recommendation 6; Thresholds & Doses (orlistat TBWL MD 2.78% — below MCID).

text: After initiating or dose-increasing a RASi, BP, serum creatinine, and serum potassium should be checked within {{c1::2–4 weeks}}.
In adults with confirmed aldosterone deficiency, fludrocortisone is started at {{c1::50–100 mcg/d}} (median maintenance {{c1::0.1 mg/d}}); salt intake should not be restricted.
extra: Rec. 3.7 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: For treatment-naive H. pylori patients with unknown antibiotic susceptibility, the preferred first-line empiric regimen is {{c1::bismuth quadruple therapy (BQT)}} for {{c2::14}} days.
In CKD G5/G5D, KDIGO 2017 recommends monitoring calcium and phosphate every {{c1::1–3 months}} and PTH every {{c2::3–6 months}}.
Confirmed MRSA HAP/VAP should be treated with {{c1::vancomycin}} or {{c1::linezolid}}; {{c2::aminoglycoside}} monotherapy should be avoided for P. aeruginosa.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Key Recommendations (definitive therapy)

text: Dalteparin VTE treatment: {{c1::200 IU/kg SC daily × 30 days}}, then {{c1::150 IU/kg SC daily}} thereafter for ≥6 months.
KDIGO 2012 recommends against using {{c1::low-dose dopamine}} (1A), {{c2::fenoldopam}} (2C), or ANP to prevent or treat AKI.
The USPSTF 2021 issued an {{c1::I statement}} (insufficient evidence) for using e-cigarettes as a cessation aid in {{c1::any adult, including pregnant persons}}.
extra: Practice Considerations: Suggestions for I Statements (e-Cigarettes) — USPSTF 2021

text: In newly diagnosed GCA without cranial ischemia, glucocorticoids should be initiated as {{c1::high-dose oral}} (not IV pulse), using a {{c2::daily}} rather than alternate-day schedule.
Echinocandins are not used for urinary tract Candida infections because of {{c1::inadequate urinary excretion}}; {{c2::fluconazole}} is the preferred agent for susceptible organisms.
Ivabradine is indicated in HFrEF with LVEF ≤35%, sinus rhythm, resting HR ≥{{c1::70 bpm}}, NYHA II–III, on maximally tolerated beta blocker; target dose is {{c2::7.5 mg twice daily}}.

Initial therapy for Candida endocarditis is {{c1::lipid formulation amphotericin B 3–5 mg/kg daily ± flucytosine}} OR {{c2::high-dose echinocandin}}, followed by valve replacement and long-term fluconazole suppression.
Lung ultrasound is an acceptable diagnostic alternative to chest radiography for suspected CAP only in centers with {{c1::formally verified LUS expertise}}.
extra: Question 1 (Lung Ultrasound) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; conditional recommendation, low-quality evidence.

text: In patients with compensated cirrhosis and confirmed or likely CSPH, {{c1::NSBB (preferably carvedilol)}} should be initiated to prevent first hepatic decompensation; NSBBs are not indicated without CSPH.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations; PREDESCI trial (Villanueva et al., Lancet 2019): HR 0.51

text: KDIGO 2024 recommends treating {{c1::symptomatic}} hyperuricemia in CKD with urate-lowering therapy (1C), but advises against using urate-lowering agents solely to delay CKD progression in {{c1::asymptomatic}} hyperuricemia (2D).
GINA 2026 does NOT recommend {{c1::SABA-only}} treatment for adults, adolescents, or children 6–11 years; all patients must receive {{c2::ICS-containing}} treatment.
In SND, {{c1::atrial-based pacing (DDD or AAI)}} is preferred over single-chamber VVI pacing to reduce incident {{c2::atrial fibrillation}} and prevent pacemaker syndrome.
In the 3HP regimen, the adult isoniazid dose is {{c1::15 mg/kg}} (rounded to nearest 50 or 100 mg; max {{c2::900 mg}}) once weekly for 12 doses.
extra: Table 4; Preferred Regimens: Three Months of Weekly Isoniazid Plus Rifapentine

text: {{c1::Olezarsen}} (an apoC-III inhibitor), {{c1::80 mg}} once monthly subcutaneously, is recommended for adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome and fasting TG ≥1000 mg/dL to reduce TG (~43%) and pancreatitis risk.
In STEMI complicated by cardiogenic shock with multivessel disease, the recommended primary revascularization strategy is {{c1::culprit-vessel-only PCI}} (CULPRIT-SHOCK trial).
extra: Section 5.1 — Culprit-only PCI in cardiogenic shock

text: The ACP 2023 osteoporosis guideline applies to {{c1::primary osteoporosis}} and explicitly excludes {{c1::secondary osteoporosis}}.
extra: ACP 2023, Guideline scope section — Ann Intern Med doi:10.7326/M22-1034

text: For acute HIT patients requiring renal replacement therapy, acceptable anticoagulants include {{c1::argatroban}}, {{c1::danaparoid}}, or {{c1::bivalirudin}}; for subacute/remote HIT on dialysis not requiring systemic anticoagulation, {{c2::regional citrate}} is preferred.
The protective anti-HBs titer is ≥{{c1::10 mIU/mL}}; a booster dose is indicated if the titer falls below this level in {{c2::immunocompromised}} patients.

The four HRCT UIP pattern categories and their confidence for UIP histology are:
UIP pattern: {{c1::>90%}}
Probable UIP: {{c2::70–89%}}
Indeterminate: 51–69%
Alternative diagnosis: ≤50%

The 2023 guideline strongly recommends {{c1::against}} ECCO₂R for ARDS treatment outside of RCTs, due to lack of mortality benefit and excess {{c2::cerebral hemorrhage}} (4.5% vs 0% in the REST trial).
extra: Recommendation 9.2, Domain 9: ECCO₂R — REST trial; 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: USPSTF 2022 removed the prior colorectal cancer benefit claim for aspirin because evidence was deemed {{c1::insufficient}} that low-dose aspirin reduces CRC incidence or mortality; CRC benefit should {{c1::not}} factor into aspirin initiation decisions.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation section; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: Permanent pacing for symptomatic SND primarily improves {{c1::quality of life}}, not {{c1::mortality}}.
Organ failure in acute pancreatitis is defined by a modified Marshall score of {{c1::≥2}}.
In head-to-head comparisons, {{c1::varenicline}} is more effective than both NRT and bupropion SR for tobacco cessation; NRT and bupropion SR {{c2::do not differ significantly}} from each other.
extra: Practice Considerations: Interventions for Nonpregnant Adults — USPSTF 2021

text: ACR 2020 mandates starting febuxostat at <{{c1::40 mg/day}} before titrating upward to achieve the serum urate target.
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 2; Thresholds & Doses

text: For non-ischemic cardiomyopathy, ICD primary prevention requires at least {{c1::3 months}} of GDMT before implantation.
If invasive quantitative VAP cultures fall below threshold — PSB <{{c1::10³ CFU/mL}} or BAL <{{c2::10⁴ CFU/mL}} — antibiotics should be {{c3::withheld}}.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Thresholds & Doses; Key Recommendations

text: Compared with LMWH, DOACs increase major bleeding risk in hospitalized patients with a relative risk of {{c1::1.70}} (95% CI 1.02–2.82), corresponding to {{c2::2–8}} more major bleeds per 1000 patients.
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 11–12; DOAC vs. LMWH bleeding data

text: Corticosteroid-dependent ITP is defined as ongoing need for prednisone >{{c1::5 mg/day}} (or equivalent) or frequent courses to maintain platelet count ≥{{c2::30×10⁹/L}}.
Catheter ablation receives a {{c1::Class 1}} indication as first-line rhythm control therapy in selected patients with {{c2::paroxysmal AF}}.
Primary prevention ICD implantation must be deferred until at least {{c1::40 days}} post-MI.
A PVC burden {{c1::>15%}} of total beats (usually >20%) is the threshold associated with PVC-induced cardiomyopathy.
Finerenone dosing: start at {{c1::10 mg/day}} if eGFR 25–59 ml/min/1.73 m², and {{c1::20 mg/day}} if eGFR ≥60 ml/min/1.73 m²; uptitrate to 20 mg if K⁺ remains ≤4.8 mmol/l.
The two core ASP interventions strongly recommended by IDSA/SHEA 2016 are {{c1::preauthorization}} and {{c2::prospective audit and feedback (PAF)}}.
extra: Recommendation I (Section I) — strong recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.

text: MMI should be continued for approximately {{c1::12–18 months}} for Graves' disease, then discontinued if TSH and TRAb are normal.
The ACR 2020 treat-to-target serum urate goal for all patients on ULT is {{c1::<6 mg/dl}} (strong recommendation).
extra: ACR 2020 Gout Guideline, Table 3 (Recommendations for all patients taking ULT)

text: {{c1::MMI}} is preferred over PTU for ATD therapy in virtually all patients with Graves' disease, except during {{c2::the first trimester of pregnancy}}, thyroid storm, or major hepatotoxicity reactions.

IVT may be considered from 4.5–9 hours or for wake-up stroke (COR 2a) when perfusion imaging shows: penumbra-to-core ratio >{{c1::1.2}}, mismatch volume >{{c2::10 mL}}, and ischemic core <{{c3::70 mL}}.
extra: Section 4.6.3 (Extended Time Windows for IVT); Table 3

text: A temporal artery biopsy specimen is considered long-segment when it is {{c1::>1 cm}}; specimens not meeting that length are classified as {{c2::short-segment}}.
extra: Table 2 (GCA PICO 1–7, 9); Thresholds & Doses — Temporal artery biopsy specimen

text: For isolated subsegmental PE with no proximal DVT and {{c1::low recurrence risk}}, {{c2::clinical surveillance}} is preferred over anticoagulation.
In troponin-positive ACS with nonobstructive CAD on angiography, {{c1::CMR}} within {{c2::2 weeks}} is useful to distinguish MINOCA from myocarditis or other causes.
C. glabrata vulvovaginitis unresponsive to azoles is treated with intravaginal {{c1::boric acid 600 mg daily for 14 days}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XV, Thresholds & Doses

text: For steroid-resistant primary FSGS, use {{c1::cyclosporine or tacrolimus}} for ≥{{c2::6 months}} rather than continuing glucocorticoid monotherapy.
In acute limb ischemia (ALI), {{c1::intravenous unfractionated heparin}} should be administered immediately in the absence of contraindications while revascularization is planned.
extra: Section 11.2.3 (Anticoagulation for ALI) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: ACP 2023 recommends initiating pharmacologic treatment in patients with {{c1::primary osteoporosis}}, rather than in those with {{c2::low bone mass alone}}, consistent with fracture risk-based decision-making.
extra: ACP 2023, Guideline scope — Ann Intern Med doi:10.7326/M22-1034

text: After an acute pancreatitis diagnosis is established, serum amylase and lipase {{c1::should NOT be repeated}}, as values do not correlate with severity, prognosis, or readiness to refeed.
USPSTF 2022: Adults aged {{c1::40–59}} years with {{c1::≥10%}} 10-year CVD risk should have aspirin initiation decided via shared decision-making (Grade C).
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade C; USPSTF 2022 Aspirin Primary Prevention

text: After fibrinolytic therapy in STEMI, routine coronary angiography should be performed within {{c1::2-24 hours}} of transfer; rescue PCI is indicated for {{c2::signs of failed reperfusion}}.
Vericiguat is contraindicated if SBP <{{c1::100 mmHg}} or eGFR <{{c2::15}} mL/min/1.73 m².
Per ATA 2015, FNA is recommended for a low-suspicion sonographic pattern at a nodule size of {{c1::≥1.5 cm}}, with an estimated malignancy risk of {{c2::5–10%}}.
PH is haemodynamically defined by mPAP {{c1::>20 mmHg}} at rest on RHC (revised from ≥25 mmHg in the 2015 guidelines).
extra: Table 5 (Haemodynamic definitions of PH); Section 2.1 — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: Moderate hyponatremia is defined as SNa {{c1::125–129 mmol/L}}; severe (US) or profound (European) hyponatremia is defined as SNa {{c1::<125 mmol/L}}.
extra: Table 1 — Classification of hyponatremia by severity

text: Triggers for advanced HF referral include furosemide equivalent dose >{{c1::160 mg/day}}, persistent SBP ≤{{c2::90 mmHg}}, or serum sodium <{{c3::134 mEq/L}}.
After initiating T therapy, serum T and hematocrit should be monitored at {{c1::3–6 months}} and 12 months, then {{c2::annually}}; target the mid-normal range for healthy young men.
The urine output criterion for AKI Stage 3 is {{c1::<0.3 ml/kg/h for ≥24 h}} or {{c2::anuria for ≥12 h}}.
In AVH, a restrictive packed RBC transfusion strategy targets hemoglobin of approximately {{c1::7 g/dL}}; liberal FFP transfusion should be avoided as it worsens portal pressure and survival.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations & Thresholds; Section: AVH, initial bleed / Figure 4

text: SGLT2i should be withheld at least {{c1::2 days}} before surgery requiring ≥1 hospital day; proceed if blood ketones are <{{c2::1.0 mmol/l}} on admission.
In SIHD with triple-vessel CAD and preserved EF, CABG to improve survival is classified as {{c1::Class IIb}} (downgraded from Class I in prior guidelines).
extra: Section 7.1 / Figure 6 — ISCHEMIA trial informs downgrade to Class IIb

text: In the context of SND evaluation, a sinus pause is defined as a pause of >{{c1::3 seconds}}.
The MAP target during initial vasopressor resuscitation in septic shock is ≥{{c1::65 mmHg}}; higher targets have not been shown to be beneficial.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Mean Arterial Pressure section

text: Per 2021 CDC guidelines, ceftriaxone {{c1::monotherapy}} replaced dual therapy with azithromycin for uncomplicated gonorrhea; azithromycin is added only if {{c2::chlamydia co-infection has not been excluded}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Gonococcal Infections, Uncomplicated section

text: In CLTI patients with adequate single-segment great saphenous vein (diameter {{c1::≥3 mm}}), {{c2::surgical bypass}} is preferred over endovascular revascularization (BEST-CLI Cohort 1).
extra: Section 10.2 (Revascularization for CLTI) / Table 16 — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: GINA 2026 recommends stepping down treatment once asthma is well controlled for {{c1::2–3 months}}, reducing ICS dose by {{c2::25–50%}} at 3-month intervals; ICS should never be completely stopped.
Subcutaneous ICD is appropriate when transvenous access is unavailable or infection risk is high, provided the patient does NOT require {{c1::bradycardia pacing, antitachycardia pacing, or CRT}}.
First-line antibiotic for infected animal bite wounds is {{c1::amoxicillin-clavulanate}}; preemptive antibiotics are given for {{c2::3–5 days}} in high-risk situations.
extra: Animal Bites section; Table 2 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text: Belimumab and voclosporin for lupus nephritis are used in combination with {{c1::standard background immunosuppression}}, not as monotherapy.
When prescribing ACE inhibitors or ARBs for albuminuria in SCD, start at a {{c1::lower dose}} if GFR is {{c2::<45 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Albuminuria Management section

text: AAN 2018 recommends women {{c1::stop DMT before conception}} for planned pregnancies; DMT should {{c2::not be initiated during pregnancy}} unless the risk of MS activity outweighs fetal risk.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level B, Switching Rec 10 / Statements 10a–10c

text: Per KDIGO 2017, BMD testing is suggested in CKD G3a–G5D patients with CKD-MBD evidence and/or osteoporosis risk factors when results will {{c1::impact treatment decisions}}; this {{c2::reversed}} the 2009 recommendation against routine BMD.
In a phase 2 trial, semaglutide 0.4 mg s.c. daily for 72 weeks achieved NASH resolution in {{c1::59%}} of treated patients versus {{c1::17%}} with placebo.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 23–28; Table 6

text: NT-proBNP risk thresholds in PAH: low {{c1::<300 ng/L}}; intermediate {{c1::300–1100 ng/L}}; high {{c1::>1100 ng/L}}.
extra: Table 16 (Three-strata risk model, p. 3657) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: Children and adults with SCD and {{c1::advanced CKD or ESRD}} should be referred for renal transplant; post-transplant {{c2::corticosteroids}} should be used judiciously given the risk of vaso-occlusive pain.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Renal Transplant for End-Stage Renal Disease section

text: In the workup of acute pancreatitis, {{c1::serum lipase}} is preferred over {{c1::serum amylase}} for diagnosis.
The USPSTF does {{c1::not}} recommend {{c1::digital rectal examination (DRE)}} as a prostate cancer screening modality due to lack of evidence of benefit.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Screening — DRE not recommended as screening modality

text: In the ABE classification, the threshold separating more from fewer symptoms is CAT™ {{c1::≥ 10}} or mMRC {{c1::≥ 2}}.

ITP response definitions (platelet ≥30×10⁹/L and ≥2× baseline): Early response = at {{c1::1 week}}, Initial response = at {{c2::1 month}}, Durable response = at {{c3::6 months}}, Remission = platelet >100×10⁹/L at {{c4::12 months}}.
In the ASH 2018 HIT diagnostic algorithm, an intermediate or high 4Ts score should prompt an {{c1::immunoassay}}; if positive, a {{c2::functional assay}} should be performed if available.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Recommendation 2.1 (strong, moderate certainty)

text: Nonprescription dietary supplements, including {{c1::fish oil/omega-3 fatty acids}} and {{c1::vitamins}}, are NOT recommended for cardiovascular event reduction in CCD.
extra: 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline, Top 10 Take-Home Messages

text: A first unprovoked VTE in SCD warrants {{c1::indefinite}} anticoagulation.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — VTE Management table

text: PFO closure is reasonable in patients aged {{c1::18–60 years}} with nonlacunar cryptogenic stroke, no other identified cause, and high-risk PFO features ({{c2::large shunt or atrial septal aneurysm}}).
extra: Section 5.4.5 — PFO Recommendations 1–2; NNT 13 over 10 person-years

text: For catheter-related thrombosis prophylaxis, routine anticoagulation is {{c1::not recommended}}; {{c2::implanted chest ports}} are preferred over PICCs to reduce catheter-related thrombosis.
KDIGO 2024 recommends {{c1::moderate-intensity physical activity}} for ≥{{c2::150 minutes/week}} for all adults with CKD (Rec. 3.2.2.1, 1D).
High-intensity statins achieving ≥50% LDL-C reduction: {{c1::atorvastatin 40–80 mg}} or {{c1::rosuvastatin 20–40 mg}} daily.
For cancer-associated thrombosis, an oral {{c1::factor Xa inhibitor (apixaban, edoxaban, or rivaroxaban)}} is recommended over LMWH.
Inclisiran is dosed at {{c1::284 mg SQ}} at baseline, 3 months, then every {{c2::6 months}}, reducing LDL-C by ~{{c3::48–52%}}.
Track 2 ICS-SABA reliever (budesonide-salbutamol 80/90 mcg delivered): 2 inhalations per dose, maximum {{c1::6 doses (12 inhalations)}} per 24 hours.

In cancer-associated VTE, LMWH is preferred over DOACs in patients with {{c1::GI or genitourinary malignancies}} at high bleeding risk, because edoxaban and rivaroxaban carry higher {{c2::major bleeding}} rates in these tumors.
Prasugrel loading dose is {{c1::60 mg}}; maintenance is {{c2::10 mg daily}} (5 mg if weight <60 kg or age ≥75 years); it is contraindicated in patients with {{c3::prior TIA or stroke}}.
extra: Table 9 / Section 11.1 — Antiplatelet Agents for PCI

text: The European guideline recommends {{c1::3% NaCl 150 mL IV over 20 min, repeated 2–3 times}} for severe symptomatic hyponatremia, and {{c1::150 mL × 1}} for moderate symptoms.
When tapering combination methotrexate + bDMARD/tsDMARD, gradual discontinuation of {{c1::methotrexate}} is conditionally recommended over discontinuation of the bDMARD or tsDMARD.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 5 (Tapering/Discontinuation); conditional recommendation

text: In IBS patients without alarm features, ACG 2021 recommends a {{c1::positive diagnostic strategy}} using history, exam, and Rome IV criteria rather than a diagnosis of exclusion.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Table 2, Diagnostic Recommendations (strong recommendation)

text: ATA 2015 TSH target for low-risk DTC with undetectable Tg after remnant ablation: {{c1::0.5–2 mU/L}} (normal range acceptable).
During HCC surveillance, an AFP ≥ {{c1::20 ng/mL}} (independent of ultrasound findings) warrants diagnostic workup with multiphase CT or contrast-enhanced MRI.
On bidirectional endoscopy in premenopausal women with IDA, pooled data show lower GI malignancy in {{c1::0.9%}} and upper GI malignancy in {{c2::0.2%}} of patients.
The therapeutic trough target for infliximab in Crohn's disease is ≥{{c1::7.5 μg/mL}}.
extra: Anti-TNF Agents section; ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: The preferred antibiotic prophylaxis during AVH is {{c1::ceftriaxone 1 g IV q24h}}, preferred over quinolones due to high quinolone resistance.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: AVH, initial bleed / Figure 4

text: Nocturnal bradycardia associated with obstructive sleep apnea should be treated with {{c1::CPAP}} before considering permanent pacing, as CPAP reduces arrhythmia frequency by {{c2::72%–89%}}.
Current AHA/ACC and ESC guidelines (2007–2021) do {{c1::not recommend}} antibiotic prophylaxis before GI or genitourinary nondental invasive procedures (NDIPs), even in high-risk IE patients.
extra: Table 1 (Guideline comparison); Current Position section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Per AASLD 2023, AFP is {{c1::no longer recommended as a diagnostic criterion}} for HCC; patients with noncharacteristic imaging should undergo {{c2::biopsy}} regardless of AFP level.
extra: Diagnosis section / Figure 6 (LI-RADS), AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: SIRS criteria triggering adjunct anti-MRSA antibiotics for purulent SSTI include temp {{c1::>38°C or <36°C}}, tachypnea {{c2::>24 breaths/min}}, tachycardia {{c2::>90 bpm}}, and WBC {{c2::>12,000 or <400 cells/µL}}.
extra: Section II, Recommendations 2–5; Table 2 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text: High fracture risk (adults ≥40 years) is defined as FRAX (GC-adjusted) 10-year major OP fracture risk ≥{{c1::20%}} OR hip fracture risk ≥{{c2::3%}} OR BMD T-score ≤{{c3::−2.5}} OR prior osteoporotic fracture.
Treat HBeAg-negative immune-active CHB when HBV DNA >{{c1::2,000 IU/mL}} AND ALT ≥{{c2::2× ULN}}.
For VF or pulseless VT cardiac arrest after failed defibrillation, amiodarone IV is given as a {{c1::300 mg}} bolus.
In severe AR, AVR is indicated when {{c1::LVEF is ≤55%}} or {{c2::LVESD is >50 mm}} (or indexed LVESD >25 mm/m²).
FeNO supporting a Type 2 asthma diagnosis: >{{c1::50 ppb}} in adults/adolescents and >{{c1::35 ppb}} in children.
Fulminant CDI is defined by the presence of {{c1::hypotension or shock}}, {{c2::ileus}}, or {{c2::megacolon}}.

Icosapent ethyl {{c1::4 g/day}} (2 g twice daily with food) may be considered in adults ≥50 years with ASCVD or diabetes plus ≥1 risk factor and TG {{c2::150–499 mg/dL}} on maximally tolerated statin.
The minimum physical activity recommendation for ASCVD risk reduction is {{c1::≥150 min/week of moderate-intensity}} or {{c1::≥75 min/week of vigorous-intensity}} aerobic activity.
For a PMR relapse, increase prednisone to the {{c1::pre-relapse dose}}, then reduce gradually back to the relapse dose within {{c2::4–8 weeks}}.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 4 / Figure 1 Algorithm

text: Antibiotic prophylaxis for recurrent cellulitis is indicated when a patient has {{c1::≥3–4 episodes per year}} despite management of predisposing factors.
Inclisiran {{c1::284 mg}} is dosed at initiation, repeated at {{c2::3 months}}, then every {{c3::6 months}}, achieving 48%–52% LDL-C reduction.
To prevent post-ERCP pancreatitis, {{c1::rectal indomethacin 100 mg}} should be administered to all patients undergoing ERCP (before or immediately after), unless contraindicated.
extra: Preventing Post-ERCP Pancreatitis section — Elmunzer et al., Lancet 2024

text: In children ages 4–17 years on GCs, an oral bisphosphonate is added only if the child has had an {{c1::osteoporotic fracture}} AND continues GC ≥{{c1::0.1 mg/kg}}/day for ≥3 months.
extra: Table 3 — Special population recommendations: children ages 4–17 years

text: In ACS patients on oral anticoagulant + P2Y12 inhibitor, aspirin should be discontinued {{c1::1-4 weeks}} after PCI, with P2Y12 (preferably clopidogrel) continued for ≥12 months.
Urological consultation should be obtained if PSA rises by {{c1::>1.4 ng/mL within 12 months}} of T initiation, or if confirmed PSA exceeds {{c2::4.0 ng/mL}}.
All patients with PAD should receive high-intensity statin therapy targeting LDL-C {{c1::<70 mg/dL}}; {{c2::PCSK9 inhibitors or ezetimibe}} should be added if the target is not achieved.
extra: Section 5.2 (Lipid-Lowering Therapy) / Table 11 — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: Patients with rheumatic mitral stenosis and AF must receive {{c1::VKA (warfarin)}}; NOACs are not an option for this indication.
Vericiguat is indicated for worsening HFrEF with BNP ≥{{c1::300 pg/mL}} or NT-proBNP ≥{{c2::1000 pg/mL}} in sinus rhythm; target dose is {{c3::10 mg/day}}.
UFH initial dosing in ACS: bolus {{c1::60 IU/kg}} (max 4000 IU), infusion {{c2::12 IU/kg/h}} (max 1000 IU/h), targeting aPTT {{c3::60-80 s}}; for PCI support, target ACT {{c3::250-300 s}}.
Varenicline achieves a cessation rate of {{c1::25.6%}} vs 11.1% placebo at ≥6 months (RR {{c1::2.24}} [95% CI 2.06–2.43]; 27 studies, n=12,625).
Empiric therapy for febrile neutropenia with SSTI is {{c1::vancomycin}} plus an antipseudomonal agent ({{c2::cefepime, carbapenem, or piperacillin-tazobactam}}); treat bacterial SSTIs for 7–14 days.
extra: Section XXIII, Recommendations 63–68; Table 7 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text: {{c1::Smoking cessation}} carries a {{c2::strong}} recommendation in the ACR/NPF 2018 PsA guideline, supported by moderate-quality evidence.
extra: Table 8 (PICO 6) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; strong recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.

text: For adults/adolescents, low-dose ICS (budesonide equivalent) is {{c1::200–400 mcg/day}} metered; medium-dose is {{c1::>400–800 mcg/day}}; high-dose is >800 mcg/day.
Major criteria for severe CAP (either criterion alone qualifies): {{c1::septic shock requiring vasopressors}} OR {{c1::respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation}}.
extra: Table 2 — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; 2007/2019 IDSA/ATS severe CAP criteria.

text: In pregnant women with ASB, the recommended antimicrobial treatment duration is {{c1::4–7 days}}; single-dose therapy is considered {{c2::inferior}}.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section III (Pregnant Women); Thresholds & Doses

text: The KDIGO 2012 RRT dose target for intermittent hemodialysis in AKI is Kt/V ≥{{c1::3.9}} per week (Grade 1A).
Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) should {{c1::not}} be used in post-transplant HCC patients due to high risk of {{c2::graft loss}}.
extra: Systemic Therapy section — ICI use post-transplant, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: Graduated compression stockings should {{c1::NOT}} be used routinely to prevent postthrombotic syndrome after DVT (based on SOX trial data showing no benefit).
Peg-IFN-α-2a for CHB is dosed at {{c1::180 mcg}} subcutaneously weekly for {{c2::48 weeks}}.

Norepinephrine for HRS-AKI is started at {{c1::0.5 mg/hour IV}}, increased by 0.5 mg/hour every 4 hours to a maximum of {{c2::3 mg/hour}}, targeting MAP increase ≥10 mmHg or urine output >200 mL/4 hours.
Recommended protein intake in diabetes + CKD: {{c1::0.8 g/kg/day}} for non-dialysis patients; {{c2::1.0–1.2 g/kg/day}} for hemodialysis/peritoneal dialysis patients.

GOLD airflow obstruction grades by FEV1 % predicted:
GOLD 1: {{c1::≥ 80%}}
GOLD 2: {{c1::50–79%}}
GOLD 3: {{c1::30–49%}}
GOLD 4: < 30%

Rescue therapy for steroid-refractory ASUC: {{c1::infliximab 5 mg/kg IV}} and {{c1::cyclosporine 2 mg/kg IV}} are equally effective; prefer infliximab in patients who have failed immunomodulators or have low albumin (<23 g/L).
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC section; CySIF/CONSTRUCT trials

text: For carotid sinus syndrome with a {{c1::cardioinhibitory}} response, {{c2::dual-chamber pacemaker}} implantation is reasonable.
The blood pressure target for patients with PAD is {{c1::<130/80 mm Hg}}, with {{c2::ACE inhibitors or ARBs}} preferred as first-line antihypertensive agents.
In critically ill medical inpatients, using UFH or LMWH over no heparin for VTE prophylaxis is a {{c1::strong}} recommendation.
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 4–5 (Critically ill patients: pharmacological prophylaxis)

text: The recommended pediatric dexamethasone dose for ITP is {{c1::0.6 mg/kg/day}}, with a maximum of {{c2::40 mg/day × 4 days}}.
Early addition of {{c1::methotrexate (MTX)}} to GCs is considered in PMR patients at high risk for {{c2::relapse, prolonged therapy, or GC-related adverse events}}.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 7

text: For rate control in AF with HFrEF, {{c1::diltiazem}} and {{c1::verapamil}} (nondihydropyridine CCBs) should be avoided.
After major abdominal/pelvic cancer surgery, extended LMWH prophylaxis for {{c1::4 weeks (28 days)}} reduces VTE risk without increasing major bleeding.
ASB is defined as ≥{{c1::10⁵ CFU/mL}} of ≥1 bacterial species in urine in the {{c2::absence of UTI symptoms}}.
Compared with normal saline, balanced crystalloids (e.g., Ringer's lactate, Plasmalyte-148) are associated with {{c1::faster DKA resolution}} and {{c2::shorter hospital stay}}.

The following agents do NOT provide meaningful histological benefit in NASH and should not be used as NASH-specific treatment: {{c1::metformin}}, {{c2::ursodeoxycholic acid}}, DPP-4 inhibitors, statins, and silymarin.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 23–28; Table 6

text:
MMI initial dosing for Graves' disease:
Free T4 1–1.5× ULN → {{c1::5–10 mg/day}}
Free T4 1.5–2× ULN → {{c2::10–20 mg/day}}
Free T4 2–3× ULN → {{c3::30–40 mg/day}}
For possible sepsis without shock, complete the infectious/noninfectious workup and initiate antibiotics within {{c1::3 hours}} if sepsis remains the leading diagnosis.
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Time to Antibiotics section (Figure 1)

text: In clinically stable patients with infected pancreatic necrosis, antibiotics should be initiated over {{c1::2–4 weeks}} before necrosectomy to allow necrosis to organize; postponing surgery until {{c2::≥30 days}} after admission is associated with decreased mortality.
extra: Surgery in AP section — ACG 2024 AP Guideline

text: After total thyroidectomy, levothyroxine replacement is started at {{c1::1.6 μg/kg/day}} with TSH checked at {{c2::6–8 weeks}}.
When escalating to a biologic or JAK inhibitor, {{c1::5-ASA should be discontinued}} because concomitant use provides no additional efficacy and increases cost.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Moderately to Severely Active UC section

text: The 2-month {{c1::rifampin + pyrazinamide}} regimen is explicitly {{c2::not recommended}} for LTBI treatment due to risk of severe {{c2::hepatotoxicity}}.
The peak VO₂ threshold for cardiac transplant listing consideration is ≤{{c1::14 mL/kg/min}} (or ≤{{c1::12 mL/kg/min}} in patients on beta blockers).

Early aggressive mobilization (high-dose, out-of-bed activity) within the first {{c1::24 hours}} after ICH {{c2::increases 14-day mortality}} and should be avoided; rehabilitation may begin 24–48 hours after moderate ICH.
