extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section on Rehabilitation

text: In patients at high risk for post-ERCP pancreatitis, rectal indomethacin 100 mg should be combined with {{c1::prophylactic pancreatic duct stenting}}.
extra: Preventing Post-ERCP Pancreatitis section — Elmunzer et al., Lancet 2024

text: Catheter ablation is recommended for {{c1::scar-related monomorphic VT}} refractory to or intolerant of antiarrhythmic drugs.
extra: Section 7.1.3 / VANISH Trial — ablation vs. escalated AAD (59.1% vs. 68.5% composite endpoint)

text: In asymptomatic severe AS, AVR is a Class I indication when LVEF drops below {{c1::50%}} (Stage C2).
extra: 2020 ACC/AHA VHD Guideline — Table 13 (Stages of AS)

text: Decompressive hemicraniectomy for malignant MCA infarction should be performed within {{c1::48 hours}}; it reduces mortality in patients ≤{{c2::60}} years.
First-line pharmacotherapy for T2DM in primary CVD prevention is {{c1::metformin}}; if additional CVD risk reduction is needed, add an {{c2::SGLT-2 inhibitor or GLP-1 receptor agonist}}.

NDIPs most strongly associated with subsequent IE in high-risk patients include {{c1::CIED implantation}}, {{c1::GI endoscopy (upper and lower)}}, {{c1::bronchoscopy}}, {{c1::bone marrow biopsy}}, and {{c1::blood transfusion}}.
extra: Current Position section — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: All EGPA patients should receive an {{c1::echocardiogram}} at diagnosis because {{c2::cardiac involvement}} is the leading cause of disease-related mortality.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 3, EGPA Rec 13

text: Long-distance travelers (>4 hours) at substantially increased VTE risk (e.g., recent surgery, prior VTE, active malignancy, or ≥2 risk factors) should receive {{c1::graduated compression stockings or prophylactic LMWH}}; {{c2::aspirin}} is an alternative only when these are not feasible (conditional recommendation).
Low-dose aspirin for primary prevention is a Class IIb recommendation only for adults aged {{c1::40–70 years}} at higher ASCVD risk without increased bleeding risk.
In GCA relapse with cranial ischemia symptoms, add {{c1::tocilizumab}} (preferred over methotrexate) and {{c2::increase the glucocorticoid dose}}.
Dapagliflozin and empagliflozin are {{c1::COR 1}} for HFrEF and {{c2::COR 2a}} for HFmrEF and HFpEF, regardless of diabetes status.
extra: Section 7.3.4; Section 7.7.1 — SGLT2i recommendations by EF category

text: Track 1 AIR for children 6–11 yr at Steps 1–2: {{c1::budesonide-formoterol 80/4.5 mcg delivered}} (100/6 mcg metered), 1 inhalation as needed; seek care if >{{c2::8}} inhalations in 24 hours.
Catheter ablation of AF receives a {{c1::Class 1}} indication as first-line therapy in patients with AF and {{c2::HFrEF}}.
extra: Section 9.2 and Table 27; CASTLE-AF and CABANA subgroup data

text: In high-risk UGIB patients (Glasgow-Blatchford Score ≥12), urgent endoscopy within {{c1::6 hours}} did NOT improve outcomes compared to endoscopy within 6–24 hours (Lau 2020 RCT).
extra: Timing of Endoscopy section, Table 6 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: After successful EVT (mTICI 2b/2c/3), targeting SBP <{{c1::140}} mm Hg for the first {{c2::72 hours}} is {{c3::harmful}} and not recommended.
Fecal calprotectin cut-offs in UC: {{c1::<60 μg/g}} predicts deep remission; {{c2::<187 μg/g}} predicts mucosal healing; {{c3::>321 μg/g}} in clinical remission predicts relapse at 6–12 months.

EOLIA VV-ECMO eligibility criteria include: PaO₂/FiO₂ {{c1::< 50 mmHg for > 3 h}}, OR PaO₂/FiO₂ {{c1::< 80 mmHg for > 6 h}}, OR pH < 7.25 with PaCO₂ {{c1::≥ 60 mmHg for > 6 h}} on maximal ventilation.
extra: Recommendation 9.1, Domain 9: VV-ECMO — EOLIA trial criteria; 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: New LV systolic dysfunction with EF {{c1::<40%}} is a high-risk feature that mandates urgent evaluation and referral for invasive coronary angiography.
Acceptable non-heparin anticoagulants for acute HIT include {{c1::argatroban}}, {{c1::bivalirudin}}, {{c1::danaparoid}}, {{c1::fondaparinux}}, or a {{c1::DOAC}}.
Clinicians should not screen men who do {{c1::not express a preference for}} prostate cancer screening.

{{c1::Fractional uric acid excretion (FEUA)}} and {{c2::plasma copeptin}} are emerging tools to differentiate SIAD from hypovolemic hyponatremia, especially when diuretics confound UNa interpretation.

For sporadic ascending aortic aneurysms, surgery is recommended at ≥{{c1::5.5 cm}}; selective surgery at {{c1::5.0–5.4 cm}} is reasonable at Multidisciplinary Aortic Team centers with low operative risk.
extra: Section 2.3 / Top 10 Take-Home Message #4; Thresholds & Doses

text: In active severe GPA/MPA with alveolar hemorrhage, plasma exchange should {{c1::NOT}} be added to induction therapy.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Rec 3 (GPA/MPA)

text: SABA overuse is defined as ≥{{c1::3 × 200-dose SABA canisters per year}}, which is associated with increased exacerbations and mortality.
The dose of atropine for acute bradycardia is {{c1::0.5–1 mg IV}}, repeated every {{c2::3–5 minutes}} to a maximum of {{c3::3 mg}}.
Per LAAOS III, surgical LAA occlusion in AF patients with CHA2DS2-VASc ≥2 undergoing cardiac surgery reduced stroke/systemic embolism by {{c1::33%}}.
The Heart Team approach is indicated for patients with {{c1::complex CAD, left main disease, multivessel disease with diabetes, or uncertain optimal revascularization strategy}}.
extra: 2021 ACC/AHA/SCAI Revascularization Guideline — General principles / Heart Team approach

text: In overweight or obese AF patients, a weight loss goal of ≥{{c1::10%}} is associated with greater maintenance of sinus rhythm and improved ablation outcomes.
Optimized BQT consists of bismuth {{c1::300 mg 4× daily}}, metronidazole {{c2::1.5–2 g/day in 3–4 doses}}, tetracycline {{c3::500 mg 4× daily}}, and a standard-dose PPI twice daily.

For mild-to-moderate migraine attacks, first-line acute agents include {{c1::NSAIDs}}, {{c1::acetaminophen}}, or {{c1::caffeinated analgesic combinations}}; migraine-specific agents are reserved for moderate-to-severe or nonresponsive attacks.
extra: Table 2 — Acute treatments with established vs. probable efficacy

text: Approximate effective radiation doses: Tc-99m SPECT MPI ≈ {{c1::~10 mSv}}; rest/stress PET (Rb-82) ≈ {{c2::~3 mSv}}; CCTA ≈ {{c3::3–5 mSv}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Thresholds & Doses

text: PREVENT-ASCVD 10-year risk categories: low {{c1::<3%}}, borderline {{c1::3%–<5%}}, intermediate {{c1::5%–<10%}}, high {{c1::≥10%}}.
In suspected IBS-D, {{c1::fecal calprotectin}} has superior NPV compared with CRP or ESR at all cutoffs for excluding IBD.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Diagnostic Testing section (IBD biomarkers); Van Rheenen meta-analysis BMJ 2010

text: For non-severe EGPA induction, {{c1::mepolizumab}} + glucocorticoids is preferred over MTX/AZA/MMF + glucocorticoids.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 3, EGPA Rec 1

text: In atrial fibrillation patients undergoing PCI, {{c1::dual therapy with a non-vitamin K oral anticoagulant plus a P2Y12 inhibitor}} is preferred over triple therapy to reduce bleeding.
{{c1::Mobile stroke units}} are recommended over conventional EMS where available to minimize {{c2::onset-to-treatment time}} for thrombolytic-eligible patients.
In adults ≥40 years starting GCs, FRAX (GC-dose–adjusted) plus BMD testing should be completed within {{c1::6 months}} of starting therapy.
extra: Figure 1 — Initial fracture risk assessment algorithm

text: After surgical resection for HCC, surveillance should include {{c1::cross-sectional imaging of the abdomen and chest plus serum AFP}} every {{c2::3–6 months}} indefinitely.
extra: Surveillance section — post-resection follow-up, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: Free testosterone measurement (by equilibrium dialysis or accurate calculation) is indicated when total T is in the borderline zone of {{c1::200–400 ng/dL}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2018 — Recommendation 1.1 technical remark / Table 2

text: Extraesophageal symptoms (chronic cough, laryngitis, asthma) should not be attributed to GERD by {{c1::laryngoscopy alone or PPI response alone}}; {{c2::objective reflux testing}} is required before antireflux intervention.
extra: Extraesophageal GERD section (PPIs and Extraesophageal Symptoms subsection)

text: In membranous nephropathy, kidney biopsy is {{c1::not required}} when nephrotic syndrome co-exists with a positive {{c2::anti-PLA2R}} antibody.
Edoxaban VTE treatment: {{c1::60 mg once daily}} after ≥{{c2::5–10 days}} of parenteral anticoagulation, continued for ≥6 months.
extra: ITAC 2022, Thresholds & Doses; HOKUSAI-VTE Cancer trial

text: After bilateral adrenalectomy for Cushing's disease, Nelson's syndrome develops in approximately {{c1::21%}} of adults, and corticotroph tumor progression on MRI occurs in approximately {{c2::50%}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Section 6.1 (Thresholds & Doses)

text: Both US and European guidelines recommend {{c1::hypertonic saline (3% NaCl)}} as the treatment of choice for acute or severely symptomatic hyponatremia, preferring a {{c2::bolus}} approach over continuous infusion to limit overcorrection.
extra: Treatment of Acute Hyponatremia section; Table 2

text: AAA repair is recommended at ≥{{c1::5.5 cm}} in men and ≥{{c1::5.0 cm}} in women, due to higher rupture risk at smaller diameters in women.
The 2026 ACC/AHA Dyslipidemia Guideline replaces the {{c1::Pooled Cohort Equations (PCE)}} with the {{c1::PREVENT-ASCVD equations}} for 10-year ASCVD risk estimation in adults aged 30–79.
A FIB-4 score of {{c1::<1.3}} indicates low risk for advanced fibrosis and allows management in {{c2::primary care}} without specialist referral.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Guidance Statements 9–16; Figure 2 (Sequential NIT algorithm)

text: Eluxadoline for IBS-D is contraindicated in patients {{c1::without a gallbladder}}, with history of {{c2::pancreatitis, alcoholism, or >3 alcoholic drinks/day}}, or Child-Pugh class C hepatic impairment.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — IBS-D Pharmacotherapy section (Eluxadoline); Thresholds & Doses

text: Progressive pulmonary fibrosis (PPF) is defined as {{c1::≥2 of 3}} criteria within the past {{c2::1 year}}, with no alternative explanation.
Simvastatin should not exceed {{c1::20 mg/day}} in decompensated cirrhosis; in CTP B–C patients, use only {{c1::10–20 mg/day}} to avoid rhabdomyolysis.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations & Thresholds; Thresholds & Doses: Total bilirubin >3 mg/dL: use with caution

text: For maintenance of remission in UC, oral 5-ASA doses {{c1::<2 g/day}} are inferior; the recommended minimum is {{c1::≥2 g/day}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC section; Thresholds & Doses

text: The 2026 ACC/AHA Dyslipidemia Guideline replaced the Pooled Cohort Equations with the {{c1::PREVENT™}} equations to estimate 10-year ASCVD risk.
On phentermine-topiramate ER 7.5/46 mg, if less than {{c1::3%}} body weight is lost after {{c2::12 weeks}}, escalate to the maximum dose (15 mg/92 mg) or discontinue.
extra: Recommendation 4 / Special Clinical Considerations; Thresholds & Doses.

text:
Enoxaparin with fibrinolytic in STEMI (age <75 y): give {{c1::30 mg IV bolus}} then 1 mg/kg SC q12h (max 100 mg first 2 doses).
Age ≥75 y: {{c1::no IV bolus}}, give 0.75 mg/kg SC q12h (max 75 mg first 2 doses).
The FDA-approved dose of lubiprostone for IBS-C in adult women is {{c1::8 μg twice daily with meals}}.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — IBS-C Pharmacotherapy section (Lubiprostone); NNT 12.5

text: Mifepristone is suggested for CS patients with {{c1::diabetes or glucose intolerance}} who are not surgical candidates or have failed surgery; dosing starts at {{c2::300 mg/d}} and is titrated up to {{c2::1200 mg/d}} based on {{c2::clinical parameters}} (not cortisol levels).
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Table 1 / Section 6.4

text: In HCM, SCD risk stratification should be repeated every {{c1::1–3 years}}.
Section {{c1::10}} (Cardiovascular Disease and Risk Management) of the ADA Standards of Care is endorsed by the {{c2::ACC}}.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Methodology and Procedure section

text: For RA patients on maximally tolerated methotrexate who are not at target, addition of a {{c1::bDMARD or tsDMARD}} is conditionally recommended over {{c2::triple therapy (hydroxychloroquine + sulfasalazine + methotrexate)}}.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 4 (Treatment Modification); conditional recommendation

text: Pacemaker deactivation at end of life is {{c1::ethically and legally permissible}} with informed patient or surrogate consent and should {{c2::not}} be considered physician-assisted suicide.
extra: 2018 ACC/AHA/HRS Bradycardia Guideline — End-of-life pacemaker management

text: Transition subcutaneous insulin total daily dose is {{c1::0.5–0.6 units/kg/day}}, reduced to {{c2::0.3 units/kg/day}} in frail patients or those with chronic kidney disease.
extra: Figure 5 — Transition to maintenance subcutaneous insulin; Thresholds & Doses

text: Rifabutin triple therapy (Talicia) for H. pylori consists of omeprazole {{c1::40 mg/day}} + rifabutin {{c2::150 mg/day (50 mg 3× daily)}} + amoxicillin {{c3::3 g/day (1,000 mg 3× daily)}} × 14 days.
In pregnancy, {{c1::PTU}} is preferred in the first trimester; switch to {{c1::MMI}} after the first trimester if ATD therapy is still needed.
The 2025 guideline recommends a sodium intake goal of <{{c1::2300 mg/day}} (optimal); the ideal limit is <{{c1::1500 mg/day}}.
For endoscopic urologic procedures with mucosal trauma, targeted antimicrobials should be initiated {{c1::30–60 minutes}} before the procedure, with a short course of {{c2::1–2 doses}} preferred.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section XIII (Endourologic Procedures); Thresholds & Doses

text: ART should be initiated {{c1::within 7 days}} of HIV diagnosis, or {{c2::same day}} if the patient is ready and no opportunistic infection is suspected.
Add-on maintenance OCS for severe asthma should not exceed {{c1::7.5 mg/day prednisone-equivalent}}; monitor for adrenal suppression and osteoporosis if expected for ≥3 months.
For very high-risk secondary prevention, the LDL-C target is <{{c1::55 mg/dL}} and non–HDL-C target is <{{c2::85 mg/dL}}, using statin plus {{c3::ezetimibe and/or PCSK9 mAb}}.
Colonoscopy is NOT routinely indicated in IBS patients {{c1::younger than 45 years}} without alarm features; it may be considered in women {{c2::>60 years}} at high risk for microscopic colitis.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Table 2, Diagnostic Recommendations; Thresholds & Doses section

text: HHS is diagnosed when glucose is {{c1::≥600 mg/dL}}, effective serum osmolality is {{c2::>300 mOsm/kg}}, β-hydroxybutyrate is {{c3::<3.0 mmol/L}}, and pH is ≥7.3 with bicarbonate ≥15 mmol/L.

Initial pharmacotherapy by GOLD group:
Group A → {{c1::bronchodilator (LABA or LAMA)}}
Group B → {{c2::LABA + LAMA}}
Group E → {{c2::LABA + LAMA}} (add ICS if eos ≥ 300 cells/µL)
Bezlotoxumab is conditionally recommended as adjunctive therapy for patients with a CDI recurrence within the last {{c1::6 months}}, added to {{c2::standard-of-care antibiotics}}.
An IMPROVE VTE score of {{c1::≥2}} warrants VTE prophylaxis in medical inpatients; scores of 0–1 carry ~0.5% VTE risk, while a score ≥4 carries ~{{c2::5.7%}} risk.
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Table 1 (RAMs for medical inpatients)

text: Secukinumab and ixekizumab (IL-17 inhibitors) should be {{c1::avoided}} in patients with coexisting {{c2::inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)}}.
{{c1::Carvedilol}} is the preferred NSBB for portal hypertension due to greater HVPG reduction, simpler dosing, and a potential survival advantage over {{c2::propranolol/nadolol}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations; Section: Which is the optimal NSBB for PH? / Table 3

text: Simultaneous liver-kidney (SLK) transplantation is indicated for AKI lasting {{c1::≥6 consecutive weeks}} with dialysis, or eGFR/CrCl {{c2::≤25 mL/min}}.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Table 13: Eligibility Criteria for Simultaneous Liver-Kidney Transplantation

text: Initiation of a csDMARD {{c1::without}} longer-term glucocorticoids (≥3 months) is {{c2::strongly}} recommended; when short-term use is necessary, the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration should be used.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 2/Discussion; strong recommendation against ≥3-month glucocorticoids

text: Per 2020 AGA guidelines, {{c1::bidirectional endoscopy (EGD + colonoscopy, same session)}} is strongly recommended in all asymptomatic {{c2::postmenopausal women and men}} with IDA — strong recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.
extra: Recommendation 2 / Postmenopausal Women and Men section; Table 3

text: Nonstatin agents that may be added to statin therapy post-ACS include {{c1::ezetimibe}}, {{c1::PCSK9 inhibitors}}, {{c1::inclisiran}}, and {{c1::bempedoic acid}}.
The fidaxomicin extended-pulsed regimen for recurrent CDI is 200 mg orally twice daily for {{c1::5 days}}, then once every other day for {{c2::20 days}}.
Depression screening interventions are associated with lower depression prevalence at 6 months (OR {{c1::0.60}} [95% CI 0.50–0.73]) and greater remission (OR {{c2::1.58}} [95% CI 1.23–2.02]).
For cirrhotic ascites, furosemide is started at {{c1::40 mg/day}} and titrated up to a maximum of {{c2::160 mg/day}}.
For bDMARD/tsDMARD-naive RA patients not at target, {{c1::treat-to-target}} (targeting low disease activity or remission) is {{c2::strongly}} recommended over usual care.
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 4 (Treatment Modification / Treat-to-Target)

text: KDIGO 2024 recommends sodium intake of <{{c1::2 g/day}} (<90 mmol/day, equivalent to <5 g/day NaCl) in adults with CKD (Rec. 3.3.2.1, 2C).
Section {{c1::8}} (Obesity and Weight Management) of the ADA Standards of Care is endorsed by {{c2::The Obesity Society (TOS)}}.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Methodology and Procedure section

text: Prophylactic anticoagulation in nephrotic syndrome is considered when serum albumin (BCG assay) <{{c1::20–25 g/l}} or (BCP/immunometric assay) <{{c1::20 g/l}}, targeting INR {{c2::2–3}} with warfarin.
For active moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease, the oral prednisone equivalent dose is {{c1::40–60 mg/day}}, tapering {{c2::5 mg/week}} until 20 mg, then 2.5–5 mg/week.
extra: Corticosteroids section (Moderate-to-Severe Disease); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: After surgical remission of Cushing's syndrome, glucocorticoid replacement with hydrocortisone is given at {{c1::10–12 mg/m²/d}} in divided doses, with the first dose taken {{c2::as early as possible after waking}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Rec. 5.1–5.2 / Section 5

text: For STEMI patients requiring transfer for PPCI, the goal FMC-to-first-device time is ≤{{c1::120 minutes}}.
{{c1::Dexamethasone}} should be avoided as routine glucocorticoid replacement in PAI due to the risk of {{c2::Cushingoid side effects}}.
extra: Recs. 3.2–3.4 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: In AF, {{c1::antiplatelet therapy}} alone is NOT recommended as a substitute for anticoagulation for stroke prevention.
People with HIV aged {{c1::40–75 years}} with 10-year ASCVD risk of {{c2::5% to <20%}} should receive at least a moderate-intensity statin; pitavastatin 4 mg is the preferred agent.
extra: IAS-USA 2024, Box 4 (Weight Gain and Cardiometabolic Comorbidities)

text: Incidentally diagnosed asymptomatic PE should be treated with {{c1::the same initiation and treatment-phase anticoagulation}} as symptomatic PE.
extra: Guidance Statement 5; Summary of Recommendations, p. e546

text: TBLC is relatively contraindicated when estimated systolic PAP is {{c1::>40 mmHg}} or PaO2 is {{c2::<55–60 mmHg}}.
extra: Evidence-based Recommendations for Diagnosis of IPF (TBLC section)

text: After an episode of AKI, patients should be evaluated at {{c1::3 months}} for resolution or new/worsening CKD.
Use two antipseudomonal agents from different classes empirically when >{{c1::10%}} of gram-negative isolates are resistant to the agent being considered for monotherapy.
extra: 2016 IDSA/ATS HAP/VAP Guideline — Section X; Thresholds & Doses

text: PAD screening is recommended for all patients aged {{c1::≥65 years}} regardless of symptoms, and for patients aged {{c1::50–64 years}} with atherosclerotic risk factors, CKD, or family history of PAD.
extra: Section 3.1 (Resting ABI and Physiological Testing) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: Perinatal depression is defined as depressive episodes occurring during pregnancy and the first {{c1::12 months}} postpartum.
Compared with biennial mammography, annual mammography results in approximately {{c1::50%}} more false-positive results and {{c1::50%}} more overdiagnosed cases.
extra: Practice Considerations: Screening Interval — biennial preferred over annual

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::regional citrate}} over heparin as first-line anticoagulation for CRRT in patients without contraindications (Grade 2B).

Finerenone (nonsteroidal MRA) may be added in T2D + CKD when eGFR ≥{{c1::25}} ml/min/1.73 m², serum K⁺ ≤{{c2::4.8}} mmol/l, and albuminuria ≥{{c3::30 mg/g}} persists despite maximum tolerated RAS inhibition.

Naltrexone-bupropion ER is preferred in patients who are {{c1::attempting smoking cessation}} or who have {{c2::depression}}; it must be avoided in seizure disorders and with concomitant opioid therapy.
For DKA diagnosis and monitoring, the preferred method is {{c1::direct blood β-hydroxybutyrate measurement}}; urine nitroprusside testing alone should {{c2::not}} be relied upon.
Pasireotide, used as pituitary-directed medical therapy in Cushing's disease, is dosed at {{c1::600–900 µg subcutaneously twice daily}}.
extra: Endocrine Society 2015 CS Guideline — Table 1 / Section 6.4

text: Combination NRT ({{c1::long-acting patch + short-acting form}}) is more effective than {{c2::single-product NRT}} (RR 1.25 [95% CI 1.15–1.36]).
In CKD G4, KDIGO 2017 recommends monitoring calcium and phosphate every {{c1::3–6 months}}, PTH every {{c1::6–12 months}}, and alkaline phosphatase every {{c1::12 months}}.
The preferred first-line systemic therapy for advanced HCC (BCLC Stage C) with Child-Turcotte-Pugh A and ECOG PS 0–1 is {{c1::atezolizumab plus bevacizumab}}.
extra: Systemic Therapy / First-line section — IMbrave150: median OS 19.2 months, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: VKA must not be initiated in acute HIT until the platelet count has recovered to {{c1::≥150 × 10⁹/L}}; if VKA is already in use, discontinue it and administer {{c2::IV vitamin K}}.

The European guideline prioritizes {{c1::urine osmolality and urine sodium}} over clinical volume status assessment in the diagnostic workup of hyponatremia, given the low sensitivity (50–80%) and specificity (30–50%) of physical exam.
For Black women, biennial screening starting at age 40 averts {{c1::10.7}} breast cancer deaths per 1000 women lifetime, compared with {{c2::9.2}} starting at age 50.
extra: Table 2 — Estimated median lifetime benefits per 1000 women (Black women), biennial DBT starting age 40 vs. 50

text: When preparing a patient for RAI with levothyroxine withdrawal, LT4 should be withheld for at least {{c1::3–4 weeks}} to achieve a target TSH of {{c2::>30 mIU/L}}.
For newly diagnosed GCA, the preferred initial regimen is oral glucocorticoids plus {{c1::tocilizumab}}; methotrexate with glucocorticoids or glucocorticoid monotherapy are acceptable alternatives.
SGLT2i agents with proven kidney/CV benefit in CKD include {{c1::canagliflozin 100 mg}}, {{c1::dapagliflozin 10 mg}}, and {{c1::empagliflozin 10 mg}} daily.
Definitive antibiotic therapy for clostridial gas gangrene is {{c1::penicillin plus clindamycin}}; {{c2::hyperbaric oxygen}} is NOT recommended as adjunctive treatment.
First-line FDA-approved pharmacotherapies for tobacco cessation are {{c1::nicotine replacement therapy (NRT)}}, {{c2::bupropion sustained-release}}, and {{c3::varenicline}}.
extra: Practice Considerations: Interventions for Nonpregnant Adults — USPSTF 2021

text: For hospitalized patients with severe hypertension (>180/120 mm Hg) {{c1::without acute target organ damage}}, do NOT use {{c2::intermittent IV or oral}} antihypertensives to acutely lower BP.
extra: Table 1 (What Is New) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: USPSTF 2024 assigns a {{c1::Grade I (insufficient evidence)}} recommendation for screening mammography in women aged {{c1::≥75}} years.
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade I for ≥75 years

text: The only antiarrhythmic drugs appropriate for AF rhythm control in patients with HFrEF are {{c1::amiodarone}} and {{c1::dofetilide}}.
Neutropenia is defined as ANC {{c1::<500 cells/µL}} or expected to fall below 500 within {{c2::48 hours}}.
For MELD exception points in LT listing, a baseline AFP {{c1::≥1000 ng/mL}} must {{c2::decrease substantially}} before exception points are awarded.
extra: Liver Transplantation section / Figure 12, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: To achieve a survival benefit, LTOT must be used for {{c1::> 15 hours/day}}.
The benchmark for a meaningful response to preventive migraine treatment is a ≥{{c1::50%}} reduction in {{c2::monthly migraine days}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — preventive treatment response benchmark

text: In HIV-infected men with weight loss and low T, short-term T therapy (3–6 months) is associated with a gain of approximately {{c1::+1.54 kg body weight}} and {{c2::+1.22 kg lean body mass}} versus placebo.
In children with familial hypercholesterolemia, statin therapy may be initiated at age ≥{{c1::8 years}} after 3–6 months of failed lifestyle modification if LDL-C persistently ≥{{c2::160 mg/dL}}.
On upper endoscopy, {{c1::LA grade A}} esophagitis is insufficient for definitive GERD diagnosis, whereas {{c2::LA grade C}} is virtually always diagnostic.
In HFrEF patients tolerating an ACEi or ARB with NYHA class {{c1::II–III}} symptoms, switch to {{c2::sacubitril-valsartan (ARNi)}} to reduce morbidity and mortality.
PREVENT-ASCVD 10-year risk categories: low {{c1::<3%}}, borderline {{c1::3% to <5%}}, intermediate {{c1::5% to <10%}}, and high {{c1::≥10%}}.
Rifampin and rifapentine are {{c1::not interchangeable}}; prescribers must ensure the correct drug is dispensed for the intended LTBI regimen.
KDIGO 2024 recommends considering pharmacological treatment of metabolic acidosis in CKD adults to prevent serum bicarbonate falling below {{c1::18 mmol/l}}.

In asymptomatic adults with IDA and plausible celiac disease, AGA recommends {{c1::serologic testing}} first; proceed to {{c2::small bowel biopsy}} only if serology is positive — conditional recommendation, very-low-quality evidence.
In adult RCTs, a restrictive transfusion strategy reduced transfusion exposure by an absolute {{c1::32.4%}} (95% CI, 27.5%–37.3%) compared with a liberal strategy.
In the ADA Standards of Care, a Grade {{c1::A}} recommendation is based on {{c1::large RCTs or meta-analyses}}.
Immunosuppression should {{c1::NOT}} be adjusted based on {{c2::ANCA titers}} alone; treatment decisions must be based on clinical status plus diagnostic studies.
The KDIGO 2012 target effluent volume for CRRT is {{c1::20–25 ml/kg/h}}, with a prescription of {{c1::25–30 ml/kg/h}} to account for interruptions (Grade 1A).

Congenital syphilis is treated with aqueous crystalline penicillin G {{c1::100,000–150,000 units/kg/day IV}} (50,000 units/kg/dose every 12 h in first 7 days of life, then every 8 h) for {{c2::10 days}}.
extra: CDC STI Guidelines 2021 — Congenital Syphilis, Scenario 1 treatment

text: When PFT is indicated in SCD, comprehensive testing should include full spirometry plus evaluation of {{c1::diffusion capacity}} and {{c2::lung volumes}}.
extra: ASH 2019 SCD Guidelines — Pulmonary Function Screening section

text: Phentermine-topiramate ER is the preferred anti-obesity agent in patients with comorbid {{c1::migraines}}, but should be avoided in women of childbearing potential not using contraception due to {{c2::topiramate teratogenicity}}.
A CAC score of {{c1::0}} supports deferral of statin therapy, unless the patient has {{c2::diabetes, active smoking, or a strong family history}} of ASCVD.
The USPSTF 2023 assigns a Grade {{c1::I (insufficient evidence)}} statement for routine suicide risk screening in primary care, meaning it cannot assess benefits vs. harms.
extra: Table 2. Summary of USPSTF Rationale: Screening for Suicide Risk

text: In adults with celiac disease on GFD, follow-up biopsy to assess mucosal healing is suggested at approximately {{c1::2 years}} after GFD initiation; the median mucosal healing time in a US study was {{c2::3 years}}.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Diet and Long-term Outcomes section; Figure 2

text: Prophylactic antiseizure medications {{c1::should not}} be administered after ICH in the absence of clinical or electrographic seizures, as they do not improve {{c2::long-term seizure control or functional outcome}}.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.4 (Seizures and Antiseizure Drugs)

text: 10-year ASCVD risk categories: low {{c1::<5%}}, borderline {{c1::5–<7.5%}}, intermediate {{c1::≥7.5–<20%}}, and high ≥20% — used to guide statin initiation and intensity.
FEV₁ <{{c1::60% predicted}} is a high-risk threshold for exacerbations independent of symptom level; consider a {{c2::3-month trial of high-dose ICS}}.
An IMPROVE bleeding score of {{c1::≥7}} defines high bleeding risk and should prompt consideration of {{c2::mechanical}} prophylaxis over pharmacological prophylaxis.
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Table 1 (RAMs for medical inpatients)

text: For extended-phase VTE anticoagulation, the preferred reduced dose of rivaroxaban is {{c1::10 mg once daily}}.
For inpatients with severe CAP who reach clinical stability, antibiotic duration should be {{c1::≥5 days}} — this is a {{c2::strong}} recommendation.
extra: Question 3 (Antibiotic Duration) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; strong recommendation, low-quality evidence.

text: Prior to endoscopic urologic procedures with mucosal trauma, antimicrobials should be selected based on {{c1::preoperative urine culture results (targeted therapy)}}, not given {{c1::empirically}}.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section XIII (Endourologic Procedures); Key Recommendations

text: AAA surveillance intervals: diameter {{c1::3.0–3.9 cm}} → every 3 years; {{c2::4.0–4.9 cm}} → annually; {{c3::≥5.0 cm}} (men) or ≥4.5 cm (women) → every 6 months.
Oral NSAIDs are {{c1::strongly}} recommended as the initial oral medication of choice for hand, knee, and/or hip OA, preferred over all other available oral agents.
The maximum safe correction rate for hyponatremia is {{c1::10–12 mmol/L per day}} per the US guideline and {{c1::10 mmol/L per day}} per the European guideline.
extra: Table 2 — US vs. European guideline comparison

text: To diagnose DKA, β-hydroxybutyrate must be {{c1::≥3.0 mmol/L}} (or urine ketones ≥2+), AND pH must be {{c2::<7.3}} and/or bicarbonate {{c2::<18 mmol/L}}.
Former smokers qualify for annual LDCT screening if they quit within the past {{c1::15 years}}; screening is discontinued once abstinence reaches {{c1::15 years}}.
In membranous nephropathy, immunosuppression is not required if proteinuria <{{c1::3.5 g/d}}, serum albumin >{{c2::30 g/l}}, and eGFR >{{c3::60 ml/min per 1.73 m²}}.
RAI is {{c1::contraindicated}} in patients with active moderate-to-severe or sight-threatening Graves' orbitopathy; prefer {{c2::ATDs or surgery}}.
Once remission is achieved in PMR, oral prednisone should be tapered by {{c1::1 mg every 4 weeks}} until discontinuation, provided remission is maintained.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 4

text: For adults with newly diagnosed ITP, the ASH 2019 guideline makes a strong recommendation against corticosteroid courses longer than {{c1::6 weeks}} (including taper).
Risk factors for virologic failure on long-acting cabotegravir/rilpivirine include BMI {{c1::>30}}, rilpivirine resistance at baseline, and HIV subtype {{c2::A6}}.
The recommended pediatric prednisone dose for ITP is {{c1::2–4 mg/kg/day}}, with a maximum of {{c2::120 mg/day}} for 5–7 days.

For sinus node dysfunction, there is no established minimum heart rate or pause duration mandating pacing; {{c1::temporal correlation between symptoms and bradycardia}} is required before recommending permanent pacing.
In pregnancy, the preferred ART regimen is {{c1::dolutegravir plus TAF/FTC (or TDF/XTC)}}; {{c2::cobicistat}}-containing regimens are contraindicated due to inadequate drug levels.
Prone positioning is indicated in mechanically ventilated ARDS when PaO₂/FiO₂ is {{c1::< 150 mmHg}} with PEEP {{c2::≥ 5 cmH₂O}} despite optimization.
extra: Recommendation 7.1, Domain 7: Prone Positioning — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: Adequate prior screening for discontinuing cervical cancer screening at age 65 requires {{c1::3 consecutive negative cytology results}} OR {{c2::2 consecutive negative cotest results}} within the past 10 years, with the most recent test within {{c3::5}} years.
extra: Clinical Considerations: Timing of Screening (Women Older Than 65 Years)

text: The standard fidaxomicin regimen for initial or recurrent CDI is {{c1::200 mg orally twice daily}} for {{c2::10 days}}.
For purposes of IDA evaluation, anemia in nonpregnant women is defined as hemoglobin {{c1::<12 g/dL}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — AGA 2020 GI Evaluation of IDA

text: Weight loss is {{c1::strongly}} recommended for overweight or obese patients with {{c2::knee and/or hip}} OA (but not hand OA alone).
IDSA/SHEA 2016 strongly recommends programs to increase timely {{c1::IV-to-oral antibiotic transition}} to optimize antibiotic administration (strong recommendation, moderate-quality evidence).
T therapy is {{c1::not recommended}} as a means of improving glycemic control in men with type 2 diabetes mellitus and low testosterone.
For very-high-risk secondary prevention ASCVD, the LDL-C goal is {{c1::<55 mg/dL}} and the non–HDL-C goal is {{c1::<85 mg/dL}}.
For severe GPA/MPA remission induction, {{c1::rituximab}} is preferred over {{c2::cyclophosphamide}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Rec 1 (GPA/MPA)

text: Systemic antibiotics are indicated for a surgical site infection when erythema/induration extends {{c1::>5 cm from the wound edge}}, temp >{{c2::38.5°C}}, HR >{{c2::110 bpm}}, or WBC >{{c2::12,000/µL}}.
extra: Section VII, Recommendations 22–26; Figure 2 — IDSA 2014 SSTI

text: Aspirin or antiplatelet therapy is recommended for GCA or TAK with critical {{c1::vertebral/carotid (GCA) or vertebrobasilar (TAK)}} involvement.
ASB may be {{c1::protective against symptomatic UTI}} in some populations (e.g., spinal cord injury patients, healthy women); treating ASB can {{c2::increase the risk of subsequent symptomatic UTI}}.
extra: IDSA 2019 ASB — Section X (Spinal Cord Injury); Key Recommendations

text: For COVID-19 AHRF specifically, the 2023 guideline suggests {{c1::CPAP/NIV}} over HFNO to reduce intubation risk.
extra: Recommendation 3.2, Domain 3: HFNO — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: {{c1::Dupilumab or mepolizumab}} may be added for patients already on LABA+LAMA+ICS who continue to exacerbate with blood eosinophils {{c2::≥ 300 cells/µL}}.
extra: GOLD 2026 — Figure 3.11 (Evidence Supporting Use of Biologics)

text: In AF-related stroke, oral anticoagulation initiation should be delayed {{c1::14 days}} if a {{c2::large cerebral infarct}} is present; earlier initiation is reasonable after TIA without infarct.

Video capsule endoscopy after negative bidirectional endoscopy may be appropriate in IDA patients on {{c1::anticoagulation or antiplatelet therapy}} or with anemia {{c1::refractory to adequate iron supplementation}}.
{{c1::Erythromycin 125–250 mg IV}}, given {{c2::30–120 minutes}} before endoscopy in AVH, improves gastric visualization.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: AVH, initial bleed / Figure 4

text: The NLST baseline false-positive rate was {{c1::26.3%}}; retrospective application of {{c2::Lung-RADS}} reduced this to {{c3::12.8%}}.
extra: Harms of Screening and Treatment section

text: Stress CMR is contraindicated if the patient's GFR is {{c1::<30 mL/min/1.73 m²}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (Stress Testing Contraindications)

text: Stratified antibiograms (by unit, age, or population) require at least {{c1::≥30 isolates}} per organism per stratum, per CLSI M39-A4 guidance.
HFrEF is defined as LVEF {{c1::≤40%}}; HFmrEF as LVEF {{c1::41–49%}}; HFpEF as LVEF {{c1::≥50%}}.
extra: Section 2.2 / Table 4 — HF classification by LVEF

text: In asymptomatic premenopausal women with IDA, the 2020 AGA guideline suggests {{c1::bidirectional endoscopy}} over iron replacement alone, but this is a {{c2::conditional}} recommendation with shared decision-making appropriate especially in younger women.
When opioids are necessary for OA, {{c1::tramadol}} is conditionally recommended over non-tramadol opioids; non-tramadol opioids are {{c2::conditionally recommended against}}.

After negative bidirectional endoscopy with no identified etiology for IDA, AGA recommends {{c1::noninvasive H. pylori testing}} (e.g., urea breath test or stool antigen); treat if positive — conditional recommendation, low-quality evidence.
In the key case-crossover studies, the 'case period' was defined as {{c1::3 months (12 weeks)}} before IE admission, while the control period was {{c2::365–449 days}} before admission.
extra: Table 3 / Janszky 2018 & Thornhill 2022 sections — AHA 2023 IE Science Advisory

text: Oral mesalamine is {{c1::not recommended}} for induction or maintenance of remission in luminal Crohn's disease.
extra: Table 1 (GRADE Recommendations Summary); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: For severe GPA/MPA remission maintenance, {{c1::rituximab}} is preferred over methotrexate or azathioprine; methotrexate or azathioprine are preferred over {{c2::MMF or leflunomide}}.
extra: ACR/VF 2021 ANCA Vasculitis Guideline — Table 2, Recs 9, 11, 12 (GPA/MPA)

text: As soon as pregnancy is confirmed, LT4 dose should be increased by adding {{c1::2 extra doses per week}} (approximately a 30% increase).
{{c1::Single antiplatelet therapy (aspirin or clopidogrel)}} is recommended for all patients with symptomatic PAD to reduce MACE.
extra: Section 5.1 (Antiplatelet and Antithrombotic Therapy) — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: In Loeys-Dietz syndrome (TGFBR1/TGFBR2) without high-risk features, prophylactic surgery is indicated at ≥{{c1::4.5 cm}}; with high-risk features, the threshold is ≥{{c1::4.0 cm}}.
In adult primary FSGS, first-line high-dose oral glucocorticoids (prednisone 1 mg/kg/d, max 80 mg) are continued for up to {{c1::16 weeks}}.
SBP or SBE is diagnosed when ascitic or pleural fluid PMN count exceeds {{c1::250/mm³}}; this threshold triggers empirical IV antibiotics.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: SBP/SBE Diagnosis and Treatment

text: When supplemental oxygen is given during acute asthma, target SpO₂ {{c1::93–95%}} for adults/adolescents/children 6–11 yr, and {{c2::≥92%}} for children ≤5 yr.
The INR target for a current-generation bileaflet mechanical AVR without additional risk factors is {{c1::2.5}} (range {{c1::2.0–3.0}}).
Ezetimibe 10 mg daily provides ~{{c1::18%}} LDL-C reduction as monotherapy and ~{{c2::25%}} incremental reduction when added to a statin.

After adrenalectomy for PA, postoperative IV fluids should be {{c1::normal saline without KCl}} (unless serum K⁺ <3.0 mmol/L) and patients should follow a {{c2::generous sodium diet}} to prevent hyperkalemia from contralateral gland suppression.
Before starting antibiotics for SBP, inoculate at least {{c1::10 mL}} of ascitic fluid into blood culture bottles {{c2::bedside}}.
extra: AASLD 2021 — Guidance Statements: SBP/SBE Diagnosis and Treatment

text: HHS resolution requires calculated osmolality {{c1::<300 mOsm/kg}}, urine output {{c2::>0.5 mL/kg/h}}, and glucose {{c3::<250 mg/dL}}.
For IBS symptoms, {{c1::soluble fiber (psyllium/ispaghula husk)}} is preferred over {{c2::insoluble fiber (bran)}}, which provides no significant benefit; NNT for soluble fiber = 7.
extra: ACG 2021 IBS Guideline — Dietary Fiber section; Table 2

text: Budesonide MMX for mild-to-moderate UC is dosed at {{c1::9 mg/day}} for {{c2::8 weeks}}; the 6 mg arm did not reach significance vs placebo.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC section

text: In patients with inflammatory conditions or chronic kidney disease, ferritin should be supplemented with {{c1::C-reactive protein}}, {{c2::transferrin saturation}}, or {{c2::soluble transferrin receptor}} to diagnose IDA.
For a very low suspicion (spongiform) nodule pattern, ATA 2015 recommends considering FNA at {{c1::≥2 cm}}; observation without FNA is also {{c2::acceptable}}.
After TNFi failure, it is strongly recommended {{c1::against}} switching to the {{c2::biosimilar of the failed TNFi}}.
A SAAG of {{c1::≥1.1 g/dL}} supports portal hypertension as the cause of ascites, with ~97% accuracy.
Acute pancreatitis is diagnosed when {{c1::2 of 3}} criteria are met: characteristic abdominal pain, serum lipase or amylase >3× ULN, and/or characteristic CT/MRI findings.
extra: Diagnosis section — Atlanta Classification, Banks et al., Gut 2013

text: In patients aged {{c1::>65 years}}, the FIB-4 intermediate-risk threshold is adjusted upward to {{c2::>2.0}} (instead of the standard 1.3) to reduce false positives.
extra: AASLD 2023 MASLD Guidance — Figure 2; Elastography/NITs section

text: The supervised exercise therapy (SET) protocol for claudication requires a minimum of {{c1::30–45 minutes}} of active walking per 60-min session, at least {{c2::3 sessions/week}}, for a minimum of {{c3::12 weeks}}.
extra: Section 6 (Exercise Therapy) / Table 14 — 2024 ACC/AHA PAD Guideline

text: Pituitary MRI is indicated in secondary hypogonadism when serum T is {{c1::<150 ng/dL (5.2 nmol/L)}}, or when panhypopituitarism, persistent hyperprolactinemia, or mass-effect symptoms are present.
Lobectomy alone is an acceptable surgical option for DTC when the tumor is {{c1::1–4 cm}}, there is {{c2::no extrathyroidal extension}}, and the patient is {{c2::cN0}}.
In {{c1::secondary}} TNFi non-response (initial response followed by loss of efficacy), switching to a {{c2::different TNFi}} is preferred over switching to a non-TNFi biologic.
A strict treat-to-target strategy using ASDAS {{c1::< 1.3}} (inactive disease) or {{c2::< 2.1}} (low disease activity) is {{c3::not recommended}} over physician assessment-based treatment in axSpA.
extra: Table 2 (AS recommendations, Rec. 44) / Table 3 (nr-axSpA, Rec. 83) — ACR/SAA/SPARTAN 2019

text: Pregnant women with HBV DNA >{{c1::200,000 IU/mL}} in the second trimester should receive {{c2::TDF}} (started at 28–32 weeks gestation) to prevent mother-to-child transmission.
NRT (any form) achieves a cessation rate of {{c1::16.9%}} vs 10.5% placebo at ≥6 months (RR {{c1::1.55}} [95% CI 1.49–1.61]; 133 studies, n=64,640).

Vasoreactivity testing is recommended only in IPAH/HPAH/DPAH using {{c1::inhaled nitric oxide}}, {{c1::inhaled iloprost}}, or {{c1::i.v. epoprostenol}} to identify candidates for high-dose CCB therapy.
extra: Recommendation Table 1 (RHC and vasoreactivity testing, p. 3648) — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: Extended-phase anticoagulation is {{c1::recommended against}} for VTE provoked by a major transient risk factor.
extra: Guidance Statement 20; Summary of Recommendations, p. e548

text: Secondary prophylaxis after AVH requires the combination of {{c1::EVL plus NSBB}}, started within {{c2::7 days}} of admission.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations; Section: Prevention of recurrent hemorrhage

text: Dialysis initiation is typically considered when GFR falls to {{c1::5–10 ml/min/1.73 m²}} in the presence of uremic symptoms, uncontrollable volume/BP, or refractory nutritional decline.

In the treat-to-target strategy for active UC, symptomatic and endoscopic remission (Mayo Endoscopic Score ≤{{c1::1}}) should be assessed at minimum every {{c2::3 months}}; fecal calprotectin and CRP are acceptable surrogates.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Goals for Managing Patients with UC / STRIDE II section

text: In suspected Crohn's disease, {{c1::ileocolonoscopy with biopsies}} is required for initial assessment to document disease distribution and severity.
extra: Table 1 (GRADE Recommendations Summary); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: AAN 2018 recommends discussing a DMT switch when a patient on adequate DMT experiences ≥{{c1::1 relapse}}, ≥{{c2::2 unequivocally new MRI lesions}}, or increased disability over 1 year.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Level B, Switching Rec 1 / Statement 1c

text: After ischemic stroke with atherosclerotic disease, an LDL-C target of {{c1::<70 mg/dL}} is superior to 90–110 mg/dL for preventing major cardiovascular events (TST trial).
Per ATA 2015, FNA is recommended for a high-suspicion sonographic pattern at a nodule size of {{c1::≥1 cm}}, with an estimated malignancy risk of {{c2::>70–90%}}.

An oral preventive migraine agent requires a minimum trial of {{c1::8 weeks at target dose}} before declaring lack of efficacy; CGRP mAbs require at least {{c2::3 months (monthly dosing)}} or {{c2::6 months (quarterly dosing)}} before assessing benefit.
extra: Key Recommendations — preventive treatment trial adequacy; Thresholds & Doses — oral preventive trial adequacy

text: Bempedoic acid {{c1::180 mg}} once daily reduces LDL-C by approximately {{c2::21%–24%}} in statin-intolerant patients.

In the 2020 NTCA/CDC guidelines, 3HP (isoniazid + rifapentine weekly × 3 months) is a {{c1::strongly recommended preferred}} regimen for adults and children ≥{{c2::2}} years, including HIV-positive persons.
extra: Table 3; Preferred Regimens: Three Months of Weekly Isoniazid Plus Rifapentine

text: After any LT4 dose change, TSH should be rechecked at {{c1::4–6 weeks}}; once stable, monitoring moves to every {{c2::6 months, then annually}}.
For HITT, apixaban is dosed at {{c1::10 mg twice daily × 1 week}}, then {{c1::5 mg twice daily}}.
extra: ASH 2018 HIT Guideline, Thresholds & Doses; Table 2

text: If terlipressin and norepinephrine are unavailable, HRS-AKI may be treated with midodrine {{c1::5–15 mg PO every 8 hours}} plus octreotide {{c2::100–200 µg SQ every 8 hours}}, though efficacy is much lower.
CKD is diagnosed when eGFR {{c1::<60 ml/min/1.73 m²}} or ACR {{c2::≥30 mg/g}} persists for more than {{c3::3 months}}.
The combined flexible sigmoidoscopy + FIT strategy uses sigmoidoscopy every {{c1::10 years}} plus FIT every {{c2::1 year}}.
extra: Table 1: Characteristics of Recommended Colorectal Cancer Screening Strategies

text: The ESC 0/1-h algorithm rules IN ACS if T-0 hs-cTn is >{{c1::52 ng/L}} OR the 1-hour delta is >{{c2::5 ng/L}}.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Table 6 (Clinical Decision Pathways)

text: Refractory CD type 2 is characterized by a {{c1::clonal}} T-cell population, carries a {{c2::poor prognosis}}, and may require {{c3::budesonide, cladribine, or autologous stem-cell transplantation}}.
In pediatric patients ≥{{c1::6}} years with AIS due to LVO, EVT by experienced neurointerventionalists within 6 hours is reasonable (COR {{c2::2a}}).
Procainamide IV loading dose is {{c1::10–17 mg/kg}} administered at a rate of {{c2::20–50 mg/min}}.
Rituximab induction dosing in adults is either {{c1::375 mg/m² IV weekly × 4 doses}} OR {{c1::1000 mg IV on days 1 and 15}}.

Combined behavioral counseling + pharmacotherapy is more effective than either alone; the behavioral component typically involves {{c1::≥4 sessions}} with {{c2::90–300 min}} total contact (RR 1.83 vs control).
extra: Practice Considerations: Interventions for Nonpregnant Adults — USPSTF 2021

text: An alternative first-line systemic therapy for advanced HCC is {{c1::durvalumab plus tremelimumab}} (HIMALAYA trial: 36-month OS 30.7% vs. 20.2% with sorafenib).
extra: Systemic Therapy / First-line section — HIMALAYA trial, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: Injectable T undecanoate (US regimen) is dosed as {{c1::750 mg IM}}, repeated at {{c2::4 weeks}}, then every {{c2::10 weeks}} thereafter.
Kidney transplantation in anti-GBM disease should be postponed until anti-GBM antibodies have been undetectable for ≥{{c1::6 months}}.

Asymptomatic candiduria should NOT be treated with antifungals unless the patient is {{c1::neutropenic}}, is a {{c2::very low-birth-weight infant (<1500 g)}}, or is about to undergo {{c3::urologic manipulation}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XIV, Recommendation 98

text: Terlipressin for HRS-AKI is started at {{c1::2 mg/day IV}} continuous infusion and increased every 24–48 hours up to {{c2::12 mg/day}} until creatinine decreases.
In AIS, significant hypoglycemia requiring correction is defined as blood glucose <{{c1::60 mg/dL}}.
Permanent pacing is recommended for acquired {{c1::Mobitz type II}}, {{c2::high-grade}}, or {{c3::third-degree}} AV block regardless of symptoms when not caused by a reversible or physiologic cause.
The dose of mepolizumab for EGPA is {{c1::300 mg subcutaneous every 4 weeks}}.

Cotesting (hrHPV + cytology) every 5 years is considered {{c1::acceptable but not preferred}} by USPSTF because it leads to higher rates of {{c2::false positives and procedures}} compared with hrHPV testing alone.
extra: Summary; Table: Characteristics of Cervical Cancer Screening Tests

text: In the BONA-FIDE trial, adynamic bone disease (low-turnover) developed in subjects with iPTH below {{c1::150 pg/ml}} (16 pmol/l) on cinacalcet, supporting bone biopsy consideration before antiresorptive therapy in CKD G4–G5D with low BMD.
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Thresholds & Doses / Chapter 3.2

text: Anti-GBM GN is treated with {{c1::plasmapheresis}} + {{c2::cyclophosphamide}} + {{c2::glucocorticoids}}; no {{c3::maintenance}} therapy is required.
CSPH is ruled in (PPV >90% for viral hepatitis, ALD, lean NAFLD) when LSM by TE is > {{c1::25 kPa}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Thresholds & Doses; Section: Noninvasive detection of CSPH / Figure 3

text: In CKD-MBD, routine monitoring of serum calcium, phosphate, PTH, and alkaline phosphatase should begin at CKD stage {{c1::G3a}} (or {{c2::G2}} in children).
extra: KDIGO 2017 CKD-MBD Update; Chapter 4.1 / Key Recommendations

text: The optimal dietary sodium goal is {{c1::<1500 mg/day}}; a minimum reduction of {{c2::1000 mg/day}} lowers SBP by approximately 5–6 mm Hg in hypertensive patients.
Initial treatment of CNS candidiasis is {{c1::liposomal amphotericin B 5 mg/kg daily}} ± {{c2::flucytosine 25 mg/kg QID}}, with subsequent step-down to fluconazole.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section XIII, Recommendation 92

text: In the 6–24-hour EVT window (COR 1), eligibility requires NIHSS ≥6, ASPECTS {{c1::3–5}}, age <{{c2::80}} years, and prestroke mRS {{c3::0–1}}.
All PAI patients must receive a {{c1::steroid emergency card}}, a {{c1::medical alert ID}}, and a {{c1::glucocorticoid injection kit}} with training on self-administration to prevent adrenal crisis.
extra: Table 4 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: MRAs are contraindicated in HFrEF when eGFR is {{c1::≤30 mL/min/1.73 m²}} or serum potassium is {{c2::≥5.0 mEq/L}}.
Severe hypertension ≥{{c1::200/110 mm Hg}} is a contraindication to exercise ECG, stress nuclear imaging, and stress echocardiography.
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Thresholds & Doses (Stress Testing Contraindications)

text: Compared to White patients, {{c1::Black and Hispanic/Latino}} patients are less likely to be diagnosed with depression or receive mental health services in primary care.
Before elective CABG, clopidogrel should be held for ≥{{c1::5 days}} and ticagrelor for ≥{{c2::3–5 days}}; prasugrel should ideally be held for ≥{{c3::7 days}}.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — P2Y12 hold times before elective CABG

text: Routine periodic {{c1::stress or anatomic testing}} is NOT recommended in stable CCD patients without a change in clinical or functional status.
extra: 2023 AHA/ACC CCD Guideline, Section 7.1 (Follow-Up Plan and Testing)

text: For AIS within 4.5 hours, tenecteplase is dosed at {{c1::0.25 mg/kg IV bolus}} (max {{c1::25 mg}}).
Glucocorticoid replacement in adults with PAI uses hydrocortisone {{c1::15–25 mg/d}} given in {{c2::2–3 divided oral doses}}, with the largest dose at awakening.
extra: Rec. 3.2 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: Initial serologic screening for celiac disease uses {{c1::TTG-IgA plus total IgA}} to exclude IgA deficiency, performed while the patient is on a {{c2::gluten-containing}} diet.
extra: ACG 2023 Celiac Guideline — Figure 1 (Diagnostic Algorithm); Diagnosis section

text: Cerebral vein or venous sinus thrombosis: anticoagulation for at least {{c1::3 months (the treatment phase)}} is strongly recommended over no anticoagulation.
extra: Guidance Statement 6; Summary of Recommendations, p. e546

text: USPSTF recommends initiating cervical cancer screening at age {{c1::21}} years, regardless of sexual history.
extra: Recommendation Summary Table — Grade D against screening women <21 years; Thresholds & Doses

text: In adults at high primary prevention risk (≥10%), initiate {{c1::high-intensity}} statin targeting ≥50% LDL-C reduction; add {{c2::ezetimibe}} if LDL-C <70 mg/dL and non–HDL-C <100 mg/dL goals are not achieved.
When initiating biologic therapy in axSpA, {{c1::TNFi}} are conditionally preferred over {{c2::secukinumab or ixekizumab}} as the first biologic agent.
The 2024 ADA/EASD DKA glucose threshold is {{c1::≥200 mg/dL}} (or prior diabetes history), lowered from the prior threshold of {{c2::>250 mg/dL}}.
extra: Figure 2A — DKA diagnostic criteria; Thresholds & Doses

text: Belimumab, approved as add-on therapy for lupus nephritis, is a {{c1::B-lymphocyte stimulator (BLyS) inhibitor / anti-BAFF monoclonal antibody}}.
extra: KDIGO 2024 LN Guideline — Guideline Overview; FDA/EMA approval context

text: In active PsA despite an OSM, switching to a {{c1::TNFi}} (preferred), IL-17i, or IL-12/23i biologic is recommended over switching to a {{c2::different OSM}}.
extra: Table 2 (PICO 23) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; conditional recommendation, moderate-quality evidence.

text: Routine bicarbonate administration is not recommended in DKA; consider 100 mmol sodium bicarbonate in 400 mL sterile water every 2 hours only if pH is {{c1::<7.0}}, targeting pH {{c1::>7.0}}.
In Turner syndrome, an aortic size index (ASI) ≥{{c1::2.5 cm/m²}} is associated with significantly increased dissection risk and warrants consideration of prophylactic repair.
extra: Key Recommendations; Thresholds & Doses — Turner syndrome

text: ACR 2020 recommends switching {{c1::hydrochlorothiazide}} to an alternate antihypertensive and preferentially choosing {{c2::losartan}} when feasible for urate reduction in gout patients.
KDIGO 2024 targets a systolic BP of {{c1::<120 mmHg}} (using standardized office measurement) in adults with CKD and hypertension (Rec. 3.4.1, 2B).
Very severe AS is defined by aortic Vmax {{c1::≥5 m/s}} or mean gradient {{c2::≥60 mm Hg}}, and may trigger consideration of earlier AVR in asymptomatic patients.
extra: 2020 ACC/AHA VHD Guideline — Table 13 (Stages of AS)

text: A peak cortisol of {{c1::<500 nmol/L (18 mcg/dL)}} at 30 or 60 minutes after corticotropin stimulation is diagnostic of adrenal insufficiency.
extra: Rec. 2.1 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: For hospitalized nonsevere CAP, systemic corticosteroids should {{c1::NOT}} be administered — this is a {{c2::strong}} recommendation, driven by evidence of {{c3::hyperglycemia}} harm.
extra: Question 4 (Corticosteroids) — 2025 ATS/IDSA CAP Guideline; strong recommendation, low-quality evidence.

text: The 2019 ACR/AF guideline specifies an acetaminophen maximum dose of {{c1::3 g/day}} in divided doses, with regular monitoring for {{c2::hepatotoxicity}}.
Per ACP 2023, {{c1::bisphosphonates}} are recommended as first-line pharmacologic treatment to reduce fracture risk in males with primary osteoporosis.
extra: ACP 2023, Recommendation 2 — Ann Intern Med doi:10.7326/M22-1034

text: For active/refractory ANCA vasculitis, the IVIg treatment dose is {{c1::2 g/kg}}, whereas the IVIg replacement dose for hypogammaglobulinemia is {{c2::400–800 mg/kg/month}}.
SGLT2 inhibitors are recommended (1A) in adults with CKD regardless of diabetes status when ACR is {{c1::≥200 mg/g}} or the patient has {{c2::heart failure}}.
In Marfan syndrome, prophylactic aortic surgery before conception is recommended when aortic diameter is >{{c1::4.5 cm}}.
In active axial spondyloarthritis, {{c1::continuous}} NSAID use is conditionally preferred over {{c1::on-demand}} use.
In AF, {{c1::DOACs}} are preferred over {{c1::warfarin}} for stroke prevention, except in patients with moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis or mechanical heart valves.

Black women have a breast cancer mortality rate of {{c1::27.6}} per 100,000 vs. {{c2::19.7}} per 100,000 for White women (5-year age-adjusted rates), and are ~40% more likely to die from breast cancer.
GOLD 2026 recommends {{c1::active case-finding}} (spirometry in symptomatic individuals ≥ 35 years with risk factors) over {{c2::population-wide screening}} of asymptomatic individuals.
extra: GOLD 2026 — Chapter 2 Screening and Case-finding; Figure 2.9

text: Montreal classification of UC extent: E1 = {{c1::proctitis}}, E2 = {{c1::left-sided colitis}}, E3 = {{c1::extensive colitis}}.
In the NLST, LDCT screening reduced lung cancer mortality by a relative {{c1::20%}} (95% CI, 6.8%–26.7%) compared with {{c2::chest radiography}}.
extra: Benefits of Early Detection and Treatment section

text: After PCI in SIHD, {{c1::1–3 months}} of DAPT followed by {{c2::P2Y12 inhibitor monotherapy}} is reasonable to reduce bleeding without increasing ischemic events.
extra: Section 14.2 / Figure 7 — Short-duration DAPT after PCI in SIHD

text: In HIV and active TB (excluding TB meningitis), ART should be initiated within {{c1::2 weeks}} of starting TB treatment, especially if CD4+ is {{c2::<50 cells/μL}}.
AKI is defined by a SCr rise of {{c1::≥0.3 mg/dl within 48 h}} OR {{c2::≥1.5× baseline within 7 days}}.

Standard IV echinocandin doses for candidemia:
Caspofungin: {{c1::70 mg loading, then 50 mg}} daily
Micafungin: {{c2::100 mg}} daily
Anidulafungin: {{c3::200 mg loading, then 100 mg}} daily
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section I, Thresholds & Doses

text: The key change from the 2016 USPSTF guideline is that women aged {{c1::40–49}} years are now given a {{c1::Grade B recommendation for biennial screening}}, replacing individualized/shared decision-making.
extra: Update of Previous USPSTF Recommendation section

text: Microbiologic cultures should be obtained before starting antibiotics in sepsis when doing so causes no substantial delay (i.e., <{{c1::45 minutes}}).
extra: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021 — Time to Antibiotics / Cultures section

text: Phentermine and phentermine-containing medications must be discontinued at least {{c1::4 days}} before procedures requiring general anesthesia due to risk of {{c2::refractory hypotension}} from catecholamine depletion.
extra: Recommendation 4 / Special Clinical Considerations — perioperative guidance.

text: Serum PCT, BALF sTREM-1, CRP, and CPIS should {{c1::not}} be used to decide whether to {{c2::initiate}} antibiotic therapy for HAP/VAP; clinical criteria alone should guide this decision.
For all acute chest pain presentations, a 12-lead ECG should be obtained and interpreted within {{c1::10 minutes}} of patient arrival.
Lp(a) ≥{{c1::125 nmol/L (≥50 mg/dL)}} is classified as a risk-enhancing factor associated with ~{{c1::1.4}}-fold increased ASCVD risk, warranting intensified LDL-C lowering.
{{c1::Tai chi}} is strongly recommended for knee and/or hip OA; self-efficacy and self-management programs are strongly recommended for {{c2::all three joint locations}} (hand, knee, and hip).
extra: Table 1 — Physical, psychosocial, and mind-body recommendations

text: Uncomplicated acute type B aortic dissection is managed {{c1::medically}} first; adding TEVAR improves {{c2::aortic remodeling}} and may reduce late aortic-related mortality.
In IgAN, glucocorticoids should be avoided if eGFR <{{c1::30 ml/min per 1.73 m²}}, BMI >{{c2::30 kg/m²}}, diabetes, or active infection.
For major surgery, delivery, or ICU-level stress, give {{c1::100 mg IV hydrocortisone bolus}} followed by {{c2::200 mg/24 h continuous infusion}}.
extra: Table 3 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline

text: Per KDIGO 2022, SGLT2i therapy for kidney and cardiovascular protection in T2D + CKD should be initiated when eGFR is ≥{{c1::20}} ml/min/1.73 m².

For adults with ITP ≥3 months who are corticosteroid-dependent or unresponsive, ASH 2019 suggests {{c1::rituximab}} over {{c2::splenectomy}} (conditional), and splenectomy should be delayed at least {{c3::1 year}} after diagnosis when possible.
Indefinite maintenance PPI therapy is required for patients with {{c1::LA grade C or D erosive esophagitis}} or {{c2::Barrett's esophagus}}.
extra: GERD Medical Management section (PPIs subsection); Table 1

text: Per KDIGO 2024, the initial GFR assessment should use {{c1::eGFRcr}}; if cystatin C is available, {{c1::eGFRcr-cys}} should be used for greater accuracy.
A Glasgow-Blatchford Score of {{c1::0–1}} identifies very-low-risk UGIB patients eligible for discharge from the ED with outpatient follow-up (99% sensitivity).
extra: Risk Stratification section, Table 2 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: UNa {{c1::<30 mmol/L}} (with UOsm ≥100 mOsm/kg) suggests {{c2::low effective arterial blood volume}} (hypovolemic or hypervolemic hyponatremia).
Before major hepatectomy for HCC, future liver remnant (FLR) must be >{{c1::30%}} in non-cirrhotic patients and >{{c2::40%}} in cirrhotic patients.
extra: Thresholds & Doses — Future liver remnant, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: In mild-to-moderate ICH with presenting SBP 150–220 mmHg, target SBP should be lowered to {{c1::130–140 mmHg}} as soon as possible.
extra: 2022 AHA/ASA ICH Guideline, Section 5.1 (Acute BP Lowering); INTERACT2 and ATACH-2 trials

text: In thyroid storm, SSKI is given at {{c1::5 drops (250 mg) orally every 6 hours}}, started {{c2::≥1 hour after ATDs}}.
Before initiating LT4 in myxedema coma, ATA 2014 recommends giving empiric {{c1::IV glucocorticoids}} to prevent precipitating adrenal crisis.
For CI-AKI prevention, administer IV {{c1::isotonic sodium chloride or sodium bicarbonate}}; do not rely on {{c2::oral fluids}} alone (Grade 1A/1C).
An ACEi or ARB should be discontinued if serum creatinine rises by more than {{c1::30%}} within {{c2::4 weeks}} of initiation or dose increase.
A positive bronchodilator response in adults requires FEV₁ increase ≥{{c1::12%}} AND ≥{{c1::200 mL}} from baseline; if spirometry is unavailable, PEF increase ≥20% is acceptable.
ATA 2014 recommends avoiding TSH {{c1::<0.1 mIU/L}}, especially in elderly and postmenopausal women, due to risks of {{c2::atrial fibrillation and osteoporosis}}.
Cardiac sympathetic denervation is reasonable for {{c1::VT/VF storm}} refractory to medications and catheter ablation.
Intensified infliximab dosing of {{c1::10 mg/kg}} (vs standard 5 mg/kg) in ASUC is used only if serum albumin is <{{c2::2.5 g/dL}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Hospitalized Patient with ASUC section; Thresholds & Doses

text: Protein intake targets in AKI: {{c1::0.8–1.0 g/kg/d}} without dialysis (non-catabolic); {{c1::1.0–1.5 g/kg/d}} on RRT; up to 1.7 g/kg/d on CRRT or in hypercatabolic patients.
For superficial vein thrombosis at increased risk of progression, the preferred treatment is fondaparinux {{c1::2.5 mg}} subcutaneously once daily for {{c2::45 days}}.
The 2021 AHA/ACC guideline recommends {{c1::retiring the term 'atypical chest pain'}} in favor of the descriptors '{{c2::noncardiac}},' 'possible cardiac,' or 'cardiac.'
extra: 2021 AHA/ACC Chest Pain Guideline — Summary / Terminology Section

text: {{c1::Systemic glucocorticoids}} are strongly recommended {{c2::against}} in AS and nr-axSpA; local/parenteral glucocorticoids may be used for isolated sacroiliitis, enthesitis, or peripheral arthritis.
Surgery for asymptomatic severe primary MR (Stage C2) is indicated when LVEF is {{c1::≤60%}} and/or LVESD is {{c2::≥40 mm}}.
extra: 2020 ACC/AHA VHD Guideline — Key Recommendations / Thresholds

text: In adult ICUs with an invasive candidiasis rate >{{c1::5%}}, fluconazole prophylaxis is recommended at {{c2::800 mg loading, then 400 mg (6 mg/kg) daily}}.
extra: IDSA 2016 Candidiasis Guideline, Section VI, Recommendation 34

text: In PsA with active psoriatic spondylitis failing NSAIDs, {{c1::IL-12/23i}} biologics are NOT recommended for axial disease because trials were stopped for {{c2::failure to meet primary endpoints}}.
extra: Table 5 (PICOs 45–47) — ACR/NPF 2018 PsA Guideline; ustekinumab axial SpA trials stopped, refs. 36–38.

text: PCI may be safely deferred when FFR is {{c1::>0.80}} or iFR is {{c2::>0.89}}.
extra: Section 4.3 — FAME, DEFINE-FLAIR, iFR-SWEDEHEART trial data

text: Before initiating atezolizumab plus bevacizumab for HCC, {{c1::esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD)}} must be performed to evaluate for {{c2::varices}}.
extra: Systemic Therapy / First-line section, AASLD 2023 HCC Guidance.

text: The AGA 2022 recommendation to add pharmacotherapy to lifestyle intervention (rather than lifestyle alone) carries a {{c1::strong}} recommendation grade based on {{c2::moderate}} certainty evidence.
For treatment-experienced patients who have previously received optimized BQT, the preferred empiric salvage regimen is {{c1::rifabutin triple therapy}} for 14 days.
Before any ADA Standards of Care recommendation is formally approved, {{c1::80%}} consensus among all voting PPC members is required.
extra: Introduction and Methodology — Methodology and Procedure section

text: Routine {{c1::neuroimaging (CT/MRI)}} and {{c2::EEG}} are NOT recommended in uncomplicated syncope without neurological features on history or examination.

In patients with {{c1::primary}} TNFi non-response (no meaningful improvement after 3–6 months), switching to {{c2::secukinumab or ixekizumab}} (IL-17 inhibitors) is conditionally preferred over switching to a second TNFi.
TAF is dosed at {{c1::25 mg}} orally once daily; no dose adjustment is needed unless CrCl is <{{c2::15 mL/min}}.
Preemptive TIPS (within 24–72 hours of index endoscopy) is recommended for CTP class {{c1::B score >7 with active bleeding}} on endoscopy and CTP class {{c2::C score 10–13}}.
extra: AASLD 2024 — Key Recommendations & Thresholds; Section: AVH, initial bleed / Figure 4

text: KDIGO 2012 recommends {{c1::isotonic crystalloids}} rather than {{c1::colloids (albumin or starches)}} as initial volume expansion in patients at risk for AKI (absent hemorrhagic shock).
Upper endoscopy should be performed within {{c1::24 hours}} of presentation for all hospitalized patients with overt UGIB, after adequate resuscitation.
extra: Timing of Endoscopy section, Table 6 (Lau 2020) — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: In TAK refractory to glucocorticoids alone, add a {{c1::TNF inhibitor}} over tocilizumab; {{c2::abatacept}} is not recommended for TAK.
The Pooled Cohort Equations should be used to estimate 10-year ASCVD risk in all asymptomatic adults aged {{c1::40–75 years}} before initiating pharmacotherapy.

For purulent SSTIs (abscesses, furuncles, carbuncles), the primary treatment is {{c1::incision and drainage (I&D)}}; adjunct anti-MRSA antibiotics are added only if {{c2::SIRS criteria are met or host defenses are markedly impaired}}.
Unlike triptans, repeated use of {{c1::ubrogepant or rimegepant}} does not appear to cause {{c2::medication-overuse headache}}.
Ticagrelor loading dose is {{c1::180 mg}}; maintenance is {{c2::90 mg twice daily}}. When combined with ticagrelor, aspirin dose must be ≤{{c3::100 mg daily}}.
KDIGO 2021 recommends restricting dietary sodium to {{c1::<2 g/day (<90 mmol/day)}} in adults with high BP and CKD (Level 2C).
After starting or changing lipid-lowering therapy, repeat lipid profile in {{c1::4–12 weeks}}, then every {{c2::6–12 months}} thereafter.
Candida osteomyelitis is treated with fluconazole 400 mg (6 mg/kg) daily for {{c1::6–12 months}}, or echinocandin for ≥2 weeks followed by fluconazole for {{c1::6–12 months}}.
Patients with VVS occurring {{c1::>6 episodes/year}} are considered {{c2::not fit to drive}} until symptoms have resolved.
The recommended initial oral prednisone equivalent dose for PMR is {{c1::12.5–25 mg/day}}, individualized based on relapse risk and comorbidities.
extra: ACR/EULAR 2015 PMR Guideline — Box 1, Specific Recommendation 3

text: Elevated BP is defined as SBP {{c1::120–129}} mm Hg AND DBP {{c1::<80}} mm Hg.
extra: Table 4 (Categories of Blood Pressure in Adults) — 2025 AHA/ACC Hypertension Guideline

text: Prophylactic antiviral therapy is strongly recommended before initiating {{c1::rituximab}} in patients who are {{c2::HBcAb-positive}} (regardless of HBsAg status).
extra: ACR 2021 RA Guideline — Table 6 (Specific Populations); strong recommendation

text: The USPSTF recommends {{c1::against}} CRC screening in adults aged {{c1::86}} years or older, as competing mortality likely precludes any survival benefit.
extra: Practice Considerations: Starting and Stopping Ages

text: Add-on azithromycin for severe asthma is dosed at {{c1::500 mg three times per week}} for ≥6 months; check {{c2::QTc and sputum for atypical mycobacteria}} before starting.
A PSA of {{c1::>4 ng/mL}} is a contraindication to starting T therapy without prior urological evaluation.

T therapy should {{c1::not}} be routinely prescribed to all men ≥65 years with low T; individualized treatment is offered only with consistent symptoms, unequivocally low morning T, and an explicit {{c2::risk-benefit discussion}}.
The 2017 ACR GIOP guideline applies to patients taking prednisone ≥{{c1::2.5}} mg/day for ≥{{c1::3 months}}.
extra: Figure 1; Thresholds & Doses — GC threshold for guideline scope

text: Magnetic sphincter augmentation (MSA) is superior to {{c1::twice-daily PPIs}} for controlling {{c2::regurgitation}} and is a safe alternative to laparoscopic fundoplication with similar symptom-control outcomes.
extra: Surgical and Endoscopic Options section (MSA subsection)

text: The preferred IV fluid for resuscitation in acute pancreatitis is {{c1::lactated Ringer's solution}}, which is associated with lower rates of SIRS and ICU admission compared with normal saline.
extra: Initial Management section — Wu et al. CGH 2011; Lee et al. Gastroenterology 2021; de-Madaria et al. NEJM 2022

text: First-line beta blockers for reducing SCD in HFrEF are {{c1::bisoprolol, carvedilol, and metoprolol succinate}}.
Iron deficiency in HF is defined as ferritin <{{c1::100 µg/L}}, or ferritin 100–300 µg/L with transferrin saturation <{{c2::20%}}.

Brief high-pressure recruitment maneuvers (airway pressure ≥ 35 cmH₂O for < 1 minute) are suggested {{c1::against routine use}} but may be considered after {{c2::derecruitment events (e.g., circuit disconnect)}}.
extra: Recommendation 6.4, Domain 6: Recruitment Maneuvers — 2023 ATS/ESICM/SCCM ARDS Guideline

text: Device-detected AHREs {{c1::≥24 hours}} combined with an elevated CHA2DS2-VASc score make oral anticoagulation reasonable; AHREs {{c2::<5 minutes}} are not associated with increased stroke risk and do not require anticoagulation.
First-line therapy for mildly to moderately active UC is oral 5-ASA at ≥{{c1::2 g/day}}; for left-sided disease, combine with {{c2::rectal 5-ASA}}.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Management of Mildly to Moderately Active UC section

text: Metronidazole is reserved as an alternative for {{c1::nonsevere}} CDI only when {{c2::fidaxomicin and vancomycin are unavailable}}.
When LT4 dose requirements are unexpectedly high, ATA 2014 recommends evaluating for {{c1::H. pylori gastritis, atrophic gastritis, or celiac disease}}.
extra: ATA 2014 Hypothyroidism Guideline, Recommendation 3c (p. 1683) — Strong, Moderate-quality evidence.

text: AABB 2023 makes {{c1::no specific recommendation}} for or against liberal vs restrictive transfusion in patients with {{c2::acute myocardial infarction}} due to insufficient evidence.
Systemic corticosteroids must not be used for {{c1::maintenance}} in CD; total steroid course should not exceed {{c2::3 continuous months}} without introducing steroid-sparing therapy.
extra: Corticosteroids section (Moderate-to-Severe Disease); ACG 2018 CD Guideline

text: The central update in the 2020 NTCA/CDC LTBI guidelines is the demotion of {{c1::9-month isoniazid (9H)}} from preferred to {{c2::alternative}} status, in favor of short-course rifamycin-based regimens.
extra: Summary; Alternative Regimens: Six or Nine Months of Daily Isoniazid

text: KDIGO albuminuria categories by ACR: A1 = {{c1::<30 mg/g}}, A2 = {{c1::30–300 mg/g}}, A3 = {{c1::>300 mg/g}}.
Pulmonary rehabilitation is recommended for GOLD Groups {{c1::B and E}}, lasting {{c2::6–8 weeks}} with supervised exercise at least {{c3::twice weekly}}.
With natalizumab use of 25–36 months (no prior immunosuppression) and JCV antibody index {{c1::0.9–1.5}}, PML risk is approximately {{c2::0.3 per 1,000}}.
extra: AAN 2018 MS DMT Guideline — Starting Rec 16

text: Extended pharmacological VTE prophylaxis (LMWH or DOAC) post-discharge increases major bleeding with a relative risk of {{c1::2.09}} (95% CI 1.33–3.27), or {{c2::4–13}} more major bleeds per 1000 patients.
extra: ASH 2018 VTE Prophylaxis — Recommendations 13–14; extended prophylaxis bleeding risk data

text: Upadacitinib induction: {{c1::45 mg/day × 8 weeks}}; maintenance: {{c2::15 mg or 30 mg/day}} (higher-dose maintenance preferred in TNF-exposed patients).
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; U-ACHIEVE/U-ACCOMPLISH trials

text: Induction therapy for GCA/Takayasu arteritis is prednisone {{c1::40–60 mg/day}}; the taper target by 1 year is ≤{{c2::5 mg/day}} for GCA and ≤{{c3::10 mg/day}} for Takayasu arteritis.
The physiological PPF criterion for DlCO is an Hb-corrected absolute decline of {{c1::≥10%}} predicted within 1 year.
In the first 12 months post-kidney transplant with {{c1::eGFR >30 ml/min/1.73 m²}} and low BMD, consider vitamin D, calcitriol/alfacalcidol, and/or antiresorptive agents.
High-dose intermittent PPI after endoscopic hemostasis: 80-mg IV bolus followed by {{c1::40 mg orally or IV 2–4 times daily}} for {{c2::3 days}}.
extra: Antisecretory Therapy section, Figure 3 — ACG 2021 UGIB Guideline

text: Cyclosporine for steroid-refractory ASUC: {{c1::2 mg/kg IV}} (target trough {{c2::200–400 ng/mL}}); 4 mg/kg is not superior and has more adverse events.
extra: ACG 2025 UC — Thresholds & Doses; ASUC Management section

text: {{c1::Riociguat}} (target dose up to {{c2::2.5 mg three times daily}}) is recommended for symptomatic inoperable CTEPH or persistent/recurrent PH after PEA.
extra: Recommendation Table 24 (CTEPH management, p. 3698); Thresholds & Doses — 2022 ESC/ERS PH Guidelines

text: To distinguish PAI from secondary adrenal insufficiency, measure {{c1::plasma ACTH}} paired with baseline cortisol; PAI is confirmed by a {{c2::high}} value.
extra: Rec. 2.3–2.4 — Endocrine Society 2016 Adrenal Insufficiency Guideline
